title: FundMe for FundMe!
description: Hi! I'm Sayoshi Nakamario and I created HelpMe.Cash, Badgers.Cash, FogOfWar.quest, FundMe.Cash, and the crowdfunding smart contracts behind it which I call CashStarter. I'm hoping to raise 100 BCH for backpay, to run and expand upon FundMe and CashStarter, and keep me working in the BCH space in general. What is FundMe.Cash and CashStarter? FundMe is a simple website and server that interacts with a decentralized crowdfunding smart contract on BitcoinCash called CashStarter. You're on FundMe right now! It allows anyone on BCH to create a fundraising campaign similar to Kickstarter or GoFundMe - just enter a few details and click 'Create'. FundMe hosts your campaigns images and description, and the CashStarter contract manages the campaigns pledged BCH. Check the FundMe FAQ and Rules for more detailed info. Deliverables Run FundMe for 1 year Continue developing the CashStarter contracts (e.g. new features) Continue developing the FundMe.Cash website Create a beginner-friendly tutorial video series for developing on CashTokens Release a Tools website (not covered in the video) Even if the above gets finished quickly, this will keep me working on BCH apps and content full-time for at least 6 months Stretch Goals: 1 CasualBCH episode per 0.5 BCH over the 100 goal. Examples of expanding CashStarter Archive Campaigns Create a permanent backup of completed and cancelled campaigns, likely involving a new 'CashStarterArchive' contract for the blockchain data and distributed filestorage(s) for the campaign data. Pledger Voting Look into letting pledgers vote against campaigns. A simple example would be the new Archive contract giving pledgers a basic 'Yes/No' vote on whether they think the campaign has completed its goals. Much more advanced options likely exist, but it's not something I've explored much yet. Stretch Goal Modules Integrate stretch goals so pledgers can actually pick which goals they want to support, rather than only having the basic, linear Kickstarter-style unlocks. This could also be a new type of campaign where creators list out their ideas/jobs, pledges are made against specific ones, and the creator can only claim if a certain number are fully funded or a total pledged amount is reached. PledgeNFT Upgrading Allow pledgers to upgrade their pledgeNFT after a campaign has succeeded so that the pledgeNFT is aware of the campaigns final raised amount. This could enable a lot of utility for other contracts, such as voting, and 3rd-party apps that want to utilize the pledgeNFTs somehow... Delayed Distribution Campaigns Look into a new style of CashStarter contract that allows creators to specify how often and how much they can withdraw from the raised funds, giving the option to pledgers to halt future withdraws if they collectively disagree with the creators actions in fulfilling the campaign. For example, letting a creator raise a years worth of salary but only being allowed to withdraw 1/12'th of it each month. Auto Refunding Campaigns Look into the option of automatic refunds for campaigns that fail to reach their goal. Examples of expanding FundMe Post-launch campaign updates for creators Archived campaign support Adding support for all new CashStarter improvements Some sort of pledger-only and creator interaction capability Creating an overall better user experience Tooling website While learning CashTokens I ended up building a little (ugly) website for personal use that had commonly-used interactions, such as: compiling contracts interacting with contracts displaying your wallets UTXOs creating new tokens/NFTs moving tokens/NFTs updating BCMR authHead moving BCMR authHead consolidating your wallet reclaiming your wallet (burns all tokens) I got sidetracked one day and started to build a prettier version with the beginnings of a UTXO drag-and-drop 'visual transaction creator', but shelved it quickly as I switched focus to getting Badgers.Cash ready for the BLISS conference. With a little time dedicated to it I could finish and release it for general use, and possibly use the finished product to help with... Tutorial Series I want to create an easy-to-approach video tutorial series that starts with installing the tools we need and goes right up to deploying fulling working apps like Badgers.Cash and FundMe.Cash. In this category I believe we only have Mathieau Geukens "MrZwets" Channel, but I want to go even more basic, basically a 'what I wanted when I first started'. An example of topics would be something like: Installing the basic tools (VSCode, dependencies, wallets, etc.) Create a basic website (React, Typescript) Cashscript & bitauth intro Build transactions with private keys Adding WalletConnect support Tokens overview (fungible, NFT, categoryID, capabilities, commitment) Create tokens (with tools and manually) Implicit burning Commitments and local state, limitations Simple example app walkthrough using local state (such as timelock) CashScript & bitauth overview Contracts overview (and contrast vs Solidity) Compiling a contract with cashc Debugging & OP codes introduction Example app walkthroughs Contracts sending to other addresses Multiple contracts in the same transaction Contracts interacting with each other Using NFT commitments in contracts Using contracts in unintended ways and preventing it Gotchas (VM limitations and quirks, like LE vs BE and minimal-encoding vs restricted byte fields) Stretch Goals An example of FundMe's capability to have stretch goals! If the 100 BCH goal is met, then each additional 0.5 BCH will equal the release of 1 more CasualBCH episode hosted by Sayoshi Nakamario and VinRadiant. Risks It's imperative that users verify that transactions look correct before approving them, such as checking the amounts and addresses. FundMe provides a way to interact with decentralized smart contracts by building transactions that are given to the user to approve. As with any web3 project, the worst-case scenario is that the project is compromised and the website code is maliciously changed to build transactions that the user didn't intend to happen. Wallets are independent of FundMe however, and even if malicious transactions are being built the wallet should show exactly what will happen to the user before approving the transaction. FundMe, CashStarter, and all my other projects are not audited. I am the only one who has worked on them. I am not a seasoned developer with decades of experience, and while I do my best to avoid it, there is always the chance that I mess up or won't be able to deliver on something I later learn is outside my capability.

title: Add BCH Node to StartOS Community Registry
description: Hello and thank you for reading. Start9 offers hardware and software with the goal of giving users sovereignty over their computing. They offer guides to build servers and install StartOS, their operating system, as well as offer pre-built servers. With StartOS, you can use private messengers, store pictures, videos, and passwords without the use of big tech companies like Google. Click the link to learn more https://start9.comOur goal is to use the StartOS community to offer a BCH node running software. Currently, Start9 is very BTC friendly, with respected individuals in the BTC community using StartOS to run their own BTC nodes. I would like to have the BCH node running software available for download, for visibility's sake and to prove their misguided conclusions of the difficulties of running a BCH node. We expect to have the project finished in a month and a half from the start date. Please check out the repository on Github

title: Let's Upgrade BCH Channel Captcha Bot
description: The Bitcoin Cash Channel Community has grown to over 3000 people! This is wonderful to see! The community is growing! But it also means that many free services on Telegram no longer work as we are classified as a "large" group. I would like to upgrade the captcha bot for BCH channel. But the PRO version of The Join Captcha Bot is not free.buymeacoffee.com/joincaptchabot/joincaptchabot-large-groupsI contacted the developer about accepting BCH but he declined. So I need a credit card to pay.It would cost 120 Euros or 130 USD or about 0.4 BCH for 2 years of service. For this I am asking for 0.4 BCH to cover the cost of a Visa or Mastercard gift card from https://buy.cakepay.com(if BCH tanks in the meantime I will cover the difference myself)If you are a whale please do not fund this in it's entirety. It is my hope that many people will fund this and try out fundme.cash in the process. I would very much rather see 100 tiny contributors than 1 big one. For that reasonI ask that you please LIMIT your contribution to a MAXIMUM 0.02BCH so that at least 20 people can participate.Thanks you.

title: Bitcoin Out Loud Video: The Road to CashTokens
description: My name is John Moriarty. I run the Bitcoin Out Loud YouTube channel, and I’ve done various jobs in the BCH space, including content production, copy writing/editing, and project management.Here’s the cost breakdown:-Final Storyboarding, Illustration, and Animation: $2000-Script writing, filming, production, management: $2000-Fundme.cash listing fee: 0.01 BCH-Fundme.cash 1.5% fee$2000+$2000 = $4000 //subtotal$4000 * 100/98.5 ~= $4060.91 //1.5% fundme.cash fee$4060.91/$332.39/BCH ~= 12.2173161 BCH // convert to BCH12.2173161 BCH + 0.01 BCH = 12.2273161 BCH //0.01 BCH listing feeHere's the script:On May 15th, 2023, miner-validated tokens were added to the Bitcoin Cash protocol. You can now mint custom assets on the BCH blockchain which can then be sent and received just like Bitcoin Cash itself.In addition to enabling the token use-cases you’re most likely familiar with like NFT art and Initial Coin Offerings, the upgrade also leverages tokens to enable decentralized applications rivaling the capabilities of blockchains like Ethereum.How’s that possible? Stick around.While Bitcoin may not be the first chain you think of when you hear terms like “smart contract” and “decentralized finance”, it’s important to remember that the Bitcoin network launched with its own “scripting language”. Using a predefined set of functions (called opcodes), users could make customized rules for what will make a transaction valid or invalid.Those opcodes included basic math operations like add and subtract, logical operations like IF and ELSE, cryptographic functions like hashes and signature checks, and some other more esoteric tools for interacting with Bitcoin’s “stack-based virtual machine”. Don’t worry if that last bit goes over your head, though. What’s important is that we recognize Bitcoin was conceived and released with programmability in mind.But Bitcoin smart contract development largely took a backseat when Ethereum was launched in 2015, and understandably so. While Bitcoin contracts were limited by the functions available in the scripting language, Ethereum smart contract developers could implement any logic they wanted.Ethereum does have its downsides, though. While the Bitcoin network only has to keep track of which coins haven’t been spent yet, the Ethereum network needs to keep track of an entire virtual computer, with various programs running simultaneously and potentially interacting with one another.That architecture makes scaling the Ethereum network to process more transactions very difficult. And when demand for transactions is higher than the network capacity, users are forced to pay high fees to outbid one another.Ironically, while Bitcoin’s architecture scales exceptionally well compared to Ethereum’s, a group of Bitcoin developers decided the Bitcoin network should only be allowed to process only a small number of transactions at a time. Disagreement about that direction led to the Bitcoin Cash fork in 2017.Since Bitcoin split into BTC and BCH, Bitcoin Cash has upgraded its scripting language several times. For example, many opcodes originally disabled in 2010 were re-enabled in 2018.Several new opcodes were added that year too, including OP_CHECKDATASIG, which allowed BCH smart contracts to use trusted sources of information from outside the blockchain. That data could include current exchange rates for futures contracts, or the status of a delivery for escrow contracts.One of Bitcoin Cash’s most important script upgrades came in 2022 with “transaction introspection”. Introspection opcodes allow smart contracts to restrict not just whether coins can be sent, but also how those coins can be sent. For example, you might keep your savings in a smart-contract that doesn’t let you withdraw more than 5% of the balance per day, unless your financial advisor co-signs the transaction.These upgrades enabled a surprising number of financial products and features, but there were still applications that would have been impractical or impossible to implement on Bitcoin Cash. There was still one piece of the decentralized finance puzzle missing, and that piece was, you guessed it, protocol-level tokens.With the addition of miner-validated tokens, Bitcoin Cash can now host much more complex financial products like decentralized exchanges (or DEXs), decentralized autonomous organizations (or DAOs), and even side-chain prediction markets, all without sacrificing its excellent scaling characteristics.The details are a little technical, but I’ll try to give you a general idea of how it works. Let’s shift back to Ethereum to start.Just like how programs on your computer can read data from your hard drives and interact with other programs, Ethereum transactions can read data from the Ethereum blockchain and interact directly with other smart contracts. A technical way to describe this setup is to say that Ethereum transactions have access to Ethereum’s “global state”. While that means processing transactions is computationally expensive, it makes complicated arrangements easier to implement.Bitcoin Cash transactions, on the other hand, are executed “atomically”, that is, independently from all other transactions, past and present. Without access to Bitcoin Cash’s global state, we need another way to represent and keep track of the various data structures used by complex smart contract applications. That’s where tokens come in.While you might usually think of tokens as representing “assets”, Bitcoin Cash’s implementation generalizes the concept. Bitcoin Cash tokens are defined by small commitments of data, and a category. The category allows Bitcoin Cash tokens to prove independently that they were issued by a given contract, and the data defines how the token interacts with the contract.By cleverly arranging and interconnecting multiple contracts that can issue and redeem tokens, we can create DEXs and DAOs whose current “state” is represented entirely by the balances of those contracts, the locations of their issued tokens, and those tokens’ properties.But in this case, interacting with a Defi application doesn’t require knowing about all the other tokens it issued. Your tokens carry the unforge-able data on how you can interact with the application, and that’s all you need. Instead of tracking state “globally”, Bitcoin Cash smart contracts track state “locally”.For more information, take a look at the CashTokens proposal.As a final note, it’s important to understand that you will NOT be able to copy and paste smart contracts from Ethereum to Bitcoin Cash, like you could from Ethereum to other “EVM” chains. While you may be able to build similar applications, the design process is completely different.Think of it like how both a carpenter and 3D printer can make a birdhouse, but giving a carpenter a list of coordinates, extrusion rates, and some plastic filament will get you about as far as leaving a 3D printer alone in a woodshop overnight.So it will take time to build the infrastructure that Ethereum users take for granted. What applications do you think will be the first to gain traction? Let me know in the comments, and be sure to subscribe for more videos like this one.

title: The Bitcoin Cash Podcast: Episodes 126 - 135
description: Episodes 126 - 135 of The Bitcoin Cash PodcastFunding requested: 35 BCH ($355 / BCH) Deliverable Episodes 126 - 135. The same show you already know and (hopefully) love. Notes FundMeAfter 5 successful Flipstarters, we're now live on FundMe.cash! Huge shoutout to Sayoshi Nakamario for pushing the state of the art in BCH crowdfunding tools (a critical element of the Podcast's success) forward. Flipstarter has been absolutely fantastic, my sincere thanks to everyone who was involved in its development & maintenance - the show wouldn't be where it is without it. My hope is that FundMe will be even better by opening up to a bigger pool of donors through the easier WalletConnect flow instead of the Flipstarter EC plugin which has been a known barrier to entry for some potential past contributors. There's also potential for contributor NFT distribution & other upgrades in the near future - so things are only going to get better. As usual, it's great to see the BCH ecosystem powering forward to deliver increasingly powerful & easy to use solutions. Visibility BCH momentum is really building & that's been very noticeable in running the Podcast. Recently, demand for guest slots has been higher than it ever has been by a huge margin. By the start of August, all slots for August, September & even into October were already scheduled (typically only 2 to 3 episodes would be scheduled in advance, rather than 8 or 9). This reflects both innovation within BCH & increased interest from outside. Internally, endemic projects are seeking help to get coverage & attention for their latest/upcoming exciting releases. The Podcast is more than happy to assist in this regard, it's very exciting to have an ecosystem thriving with so much productivity. Externally, the momentum in BCH is generating curiosity & engagement with the project. This is also great news & will help expose BCH to fresh audiences as a result. We're also seeing the return of "BCH is dead" FUD. After we defeated the previous round of that, we received a period of respite where haters felt timid to come back for more. However our increasing visibility is now bringing in a fresh wave of ignorant propagandised fools to give their 2 cents that BCH is dead, ironically only because it's doing better than ever and thus caught their attention. This probably won't be the last cycle of such FUD, but every time it recurs it becomes increasingly ridiculous & I'm pleased to see the community response has been to treat it as such. Outsiders notice & get curious. FeedbackListener feedback has been very positive for the recent episodes. I believe we are constantly delivering at a higher quality of discussion & I'm very glad the listener feedback seems to reflect that. Viewer metrics are not noticeably rising, but I'm not surprised given the relatively monotone BCH price action. Note to the community I have been particularly excited to see the emergence of public statistics from the BCH community. Selene users can be checked on daily, AFoG is putting out statistics, BCH Bull makes regular announcements on their progress, BCH Guru has a dashboard & other projects no doubt soon to follow. This data-driven approach allows for better self-reflection & iteration on upgrades - plus it sends a very, very strong public signal that the BCH community is taking itself seriously & making progress as a result. I'm also looking forward to the BCH Argentina conference in October. The Podcast will be in attendance & will do some on-the-ground coverage of the event - although it is not our intention to comprehensively cover the event or broadcast as much as possible like at BLISS. Attending is about being involved in what's happening, being informed on the local ecosystem for explaining on the Podcast, and we will make some ancillary social media posts & content to give some insight to the BCH community that can't make it but it won't be a full stream. Let's keep building Bitcoin Cash. Proof of Work I have aggregated previous delivery & community work (including previous 5 Flipstarter delivery & accountability) on a new page on the Bitcoin Cash Podcast website. See it here: https://bitcoincashpodcast.com/proof-of-work. Cost breakdown: 0.7 BCH topic research, episode hosting 0.7 BCH production & editing costs 0.1 BCH Thumbnail graphics & episode distribution 0.1 BCH Timestamps, links & episode summary 0.2 BCH Youtube Shorts creation & distribution 0.2 BCH Instagram & TikTok videos & management 2 BCH / episodex10 episodes = 20 BCH 5 BCH: Media materials for experimental prototype projects & additional videos such as ABLA explainer / BLISS interviews / music videos for increased BCH promotion & education. 5 BCH misc expenses: Ongoing costs such as website hosting & domain registration, Zoom subscription, supporting other community fundraisers, FAQ maintenance etc.4.5 BCH: Updates from BCH Argentina conference.0.5 BCH: Fundme.cash fee Contributor rewards:Donations of > 0.5 BCH will receive a shoutout at the end of each show (like the Patrons and Sponsors) unless your name/message is rude or slanderous or you ask me not to or it's otherwise unreadable for some reason. DisclaimerI will be completing the deliverables whether or not the Flipstarter gets funded, the continuation of the show & my assistance to the community is in no way contingent on this funding request. However, as per previous Flipstarter, I believe this is a valuable community service & get a lot of feedback in that regard. If funding stops I will eventually have to work harder on alternative monetisation strategies (possibly advertising, sponsored episodes) though. ContactAs always, for any questions, comments or suggestions I can be reached at jeremy@bitcoincashpodcast.com or on Telegram t.me/BitcoinCashPodcast.

title: Organize a roadshow of Bitcoin Cash live-events
description: Roadshow of Bitcoin Cash live-events in the Netherlands Funding requested: 25 BCH ($300/ BCH) Who we are We are Paul (also known from his Bitcoin Cash YouTube channel youtube.com/@rspl2024) and Willem (BCH Bliss Conference attendee – Bitcoin Cash YouTube channel youtube.com/@Bliss88-BitcoinCash, content following shortly), both from the Netherlands! What we want to do Organize a roadshow of various Bitcoin Cash live-events in the Netherlands. Goals of the roadshow Educate: Teach about the power of Bitcoin Cash and its potential to transform the financial landscape. Empower: Foster a vibrant community of Bitcoin Cash enthusiasts who are passionate about decentralization and financial sovereignty. Accelerate Adoption: Drive widespread adoption of Bitcoin Cash as a reliable and accessible digital currency. Where Region West (Amsterdam/Utrecht area) Region North (Groningen/Emmen area) Region South (Eindhoven area) Region East (Arnhem area) When The events will be held in Q4 (October, November, beginning of December) of 2024. At least 3 events will be held, the West and the North region will be covered for sure. Content events Part 1: Theory Blockchain Basics: A fundamental introduction to blockchain technology. What is Bitcoin Cash? A detailed explanation of Bitcoin Cash and its key features. Differences between Bitcoin Cash and Bitcoin: A comparison of the two cryptocurrencies, highlighting their unique characteristics. Economic Structures and Macroeconomic Changes: How Bitcoin Cash fits into the broader economic landscape and its potential impact on global finance. Why Bitcoin Cash? Security: Discussing the security measures in place for Bitcoin Cash. Wide Adoption: Highlighting the potential for widespread use and acceptance. Borderless: Explaining the global nature of Bitcoin Cash and its ability to facilitate cross-border transactions. Peer-to-Peer Electronic Cash: Demonstrating how Bitcoin Cash can be used for everyday transactions, such as paying for a haircut (with a video example). Why don't we hear more about Bitcoin Cash? Censorship: Discussing the alleged censorship of Bitcoin Cash by the Bitcoin Core community on social media. Corporate Takeover: Suggesting that Bitcoin Core has been influenced by financial institutions. Roger Ver's Book: Referencing a book by Roger Ver, a prominent figure in the Bitcoin Cash community. The Bitcoin Cash Community: Emphasizing that the community is not solely driven by financial gain, but also by a desire for a better world. Bitcoin Cash Ecosystem: Overview of various platforms and services built on Bitcoin Cash, including: Cauldron.quest (a decentralized exchange) BCH Bull (for forward contracts and DeFi) BCH Guru (online P2P gambling) Tapswap (for NFTs) Part 2: Demo This section focuses on showcasing practical applications of the platforms and services mentioned in Part 1. BCH Bull Demo: This will likely involve a live demonstration of how to use BCH Bull for features like forward contracts and DeFi functionalities within the Bitcoin Cash ecosystem. Cauldron.quest Demo: Expect a walkthrough of using Cauldron.quest, a decentralized exchange (DEX) built on Bitcoin Cash. BCH Guru Demo: This segment will likely showcase BCH Guru, a platform facilitating online peer-to-peer gambling using Bitcoin Cash. Tapswap Demo: This part will demonstrate Tapswap, a platform enabling users to interact with Non-Fungible Tokens (NFTs) on the Bitcoin Cash blockchain. Fundme.cash Demo: This part will demonstrate Fundme.cash, a crowdfunding website that uses decentralized smart contracts on Bitcoin Cash. Cost breakdown per event: 5 BCH hiring costs venue + basic consumption attendees (water, tea, coffee) 0.5 BCH transportation costs 0.5 BCH marketing costs 0.25 BCH BCH promotion materials (stickers, etc.) Disclaimer We will pursue a number of the anticipated live-event deliverables regardless of whether or not full fundme.cash funding is achieved & continuation is in no way contingent on this request. The event in the Region West (Amsterdam/Utrecht area) as well as the event in Region North (Groningen/Emmen area) will take place no matter what. That said, we believe this is a valuable community service that will help to boost BCH adoption which will benefit all involved in the community. Contact For any questions, comments or suggestions Paul can be reached at rspl382@protonmail.com or on Telegram t.me/rspl382. --------------------------- Roadshow de eventos en vivo de Bitcoin Cash en los Países Bajos Financiación solicitada: 25 BCH (300 USD/BCH) ¿Quiénes somos? ¡Somos Paul (conocido por su canal de YouTube sobre Bitcoin Cash: youtube.com/@rspl2024) y Willem (asistente a la Conferencia BCH Bliss – Canal de YouTube sobre Bitcoin Cash youtube.com/@Bliss88-BitcoinCash (contenido disponible próximamente), ¡ambos de los Países Bajos! Qué queremos hacer Organizar una serie de eventos presenciales sobre Bitcoin Cash en varias ciudades de los Países Bajos. Objetivos del roadshow Educar: Enseñar sobre el poder de Bitcoin Cash y su potencial para transformar el panorama financiero. Empoderar: Fomentar una comunidad vibrante de entusiastas de Bitcoin Cash apasionados por la descentralización y la soberanía financiera. Acelerar la adopción: Impulsar el uso generalizado de Bitcoin Cash como una moneda digital confiable y accesible. Lugares Región Oeste (área de Ámsterdam/Utrecht) Región Norte (área de Groningen/Emmen) Región Sur (área de Eindhoven) Región Este (área de Arnhem) Fechas Los eventos se llevarán a cabo en el cuarto trimestre (octubre, noviembre, principios de diciembre) de 2024. Se realizarán al menos 3 eventos, cubriéndose con seguridad las regiones Oeste y Norte. Contenido de los eventos Parte 1: Teoría Fundamentos de Blockchain: Introducción básica a la tecnología blockchain. ¿Qué es Bitcoin Cash? Una explicación detallada de Bitcoin Cash y sus características clave. Diferencias entre Bitcoin Cash y Bitcoin: Una comparación de las dos criptomonedas, destacando sus características únicas. Estructuras económicas y cambios macroeconómicos: Cómo Bitcoin Cash encaja en el panorama económico más amplio y su impacto potencial en las finanzas globales. ¿Por qué Bitcoin Cash? Seguridad: Discutidos de las medidas de seguridad implementadas para Bitcoin Cash. Amplia adopción: Destacando el potencial de uso y aceptación generalizados. Sin fronteras: Explicando la naturaleza global de Bitcoin Cash y su capacidad para facilitar transacciones transfronterizas. Efectivo electrónico peer-to-peer: Demostrando cómo se puede usar Bitcoin Cash para transacciones cotidianas, como pagar un corte de pelo (con un video de ejemplo). ¿Por qué no escuchamos más sobre Bitcoin Cash? Censura: Discutiendo la supuesta censura de Bitcoin Cash por parte de la comunidad de Bitcoin Core en las redes sociales. Toma de control corporativa: Sugiriendo que Bitcoin Core ha sido influenciado por instituciones financieras. El libro de Roger Ver: Se hace referencia a un libro de Roger Ver, una figura destacada en la comunidad de Bitcoin Cash. La comunidad de Bitcoin Cash: Enfatizando que la comunidad no solo está impulsada por las ganancias financieras, sino también por el deseo de un mundo mejor. Ecosistema de Bitcoin Cash: Descripción general de varias plataformas y servicios construidos sobre Bitcoin Cash, incluyendo: Cauldron.quest (un exchange descentralizado) BCH Bull (para contratos a futuro y DeFi) BCH Guru (juegos peer-to-peer en línea) Tapswap (para NFTs) Fundme.cash (para campañas de recaudación de fondos) Parte 2: Demo Esta sección se enfoca en mostrar aplicaciones prácticas de las plataformas y servicios mencionados en la Parte 1. Demostración de BCH Bull: Probablemente involucre una demostración en vivo de cómo usar BCH Bull para funciones como contratos a futuro y funcionalidades DeFi dentro del ecosistema de Bitcoin Cash. Demostración de Cauldron.quest: Espera un tutorial sobre cómo usar Cauldron.quest, un exchange descentralizado (DEX) construido sobre Bitcoin Cash. Demostración de BCH Guru: Es probable que este segmento muestre BCH Guru, una plataforma que facilita las apuestas peer-to-peer en línea utilizando Bitcoin Cash. Demostración de Tapswap: Esta parte demostrará Tapswap, una plataforma que permite a los usuarios interactuar con tokens no fungibles (NFT) en la cadena de bloques de Bitcoin Cash. Demostración de Fundme.cash: Esta parte demostrará Fundme.cash, un sitio web de crowdfunding que utiliza contratos inteligentes descentralizados en Bitcoin Cash. Desglose de costos por evento: 5 BCH costos de alquiler del lugar + consumo básico de los asistentes (agua, té, café) 0.5 BCH transporte 0.5 BCH marketing 0.25 BCH materiales de promoción Descargo de responsabilidad Llevaremos a cabo varios de los elementos previstos para los eventos presenciales independientemente de si se logra la financiación completa de Fundme.cash y la continuación no depende de ninguna manera de esta solicitud. El evento en la Región Oeste (área de Ámsterdam/Utrecht) así como el evento en la Región Norte (área de Groningen/Emmen) se realizarán de todas formas. Dicho esto, creemos que se trata de un servicio comunitario valioso que ayudará a impulsar la adopción de BCH, lo que beneficiará a todos los involucrados en la comunidad. Contacto Para cualquier pregunta, comentario o sugerencia, puedes contactar a Paul en rspl382@protonmail.com o en Telegram t.me/rspl382. A Willem se le puede contactar en tcm.cash88@gmail.com.

title: Learn Bitcoin Cash in Seconds
description: "The BitcoinCash in Seconds" Learn BitcoinCash in Seconds Funding Request: 2.63BCH ($334/BCH) Who I am? Hi, I'm CM Works a Graphic Artist and Short Form Video editor fromTwitter. I love to create something about BitcoinCash. In fact, almost 100% of my works are about Bitcoin Cash from fan arts to commissioned one from BitcoinCash enthusiasts. Apart from it, there are editing short-form video which is almost 100% about BitcoinCash too. Here I posted my BCH Related Arts: A combination of FanArts for BCH Projects and non-fanarts but still BCH Related. CM_WORKS Twitter CM_Works Instagram And Short Form Edits Works: Short-Form Works Instagram andShort-Form Works Instagram 2 What I want to do? To create a series of Bitcoin Cash Short-Form videos for Tiktok, Instagram and Youtube Shorts with a total of 22 Videos.Create Tiktok Page, Youtube Shorts and Instagram Reels page for the contentsGrow the Page Filled with Bitcoin Cash Contents Page Name "The BitcoinCash in Seconds" Goal of the Project Make BitcoinCash Known more through short-form contents Gather new BitcoinCashers Target Platforms Youtube Shorts Tiktok Instagram reels. Basis Nowadays, people spend most of their time in Tiktok and Reels. Even their source of knowledge and news. People loves videos with direct and concise content. Tiktok is the best source for viewers Instagram has the best source for account reach Youtube shorts is the best source for direct answers for searches Content Scripts Below is the script of the 22 Videos I want to create sizing 20-30 seconds: -- ### **Video 1: Introduction to Bitcoin Cash**(Finished) "Bitcoin Cash is a cryptocurrency designed to make transactions faster and more affordable. Created in 2017, it split from Bitcoin to address some of Bitcoin’s limitations. Bitcoin Cash offers larger block sizes, which means it can handle more transactions at once. This results in lower fees and quicker processing times compared to Bitcoin. If you're looking for a digital currency that's both efficient and cost-effective, Bitcoin Cash is worth considering."-- ### **Video 2: History and Origins**(Finished)"Bitcoin Cash was introduced in August 2017 following a split from Bitcoin. This split, known as a ‘fork’, aimed to tackle Bitcoin’s scalability issues. The developers wanted to enhance Bitcoin’s transaction capacity to handle more users and lower transaction fees. The result? Bitcoin Cash, with its larger block size, is designed to support a higher volume of transactions, making it a more scalable option for everyday use."-- ### **Video 3: Differences Between BCH and BTC**(Finished)"Bitcoin Cash and Bitcoin share a common origin but differ significantly in their approach. Bitcoin Cash features a larger block size of 32 MB, compared to Bitcoin’s 1 MB. This increase allows Bitcoin Cash to process more transactions per block, which means faster confirmations and lower fees. While Bitcoin focuses on store-of-value, Bitcoin Cash aims to be a practical currency for daily transactions, making it an appealing choice for frequent users."-- ### **Video 4: Benefits of Bitcoin Cash**(Finished)"Bitcoin Cash provides several notable benefits. First, its larger block size means lower transaction fees compared to Bitcoin, making it ideal for small transactions and micropayments. Second, Bitcoin Cash transactions are processed faster, so you won't have to wait as long for confirmations. This efficiency makes BCH a practical choice for everyday purchases and transactions. Overall, Bitcoin Cash combines speed, low costs, and usability."-- ### **Video 5: What is a Blockchain?**"A blockchain is a decentralized digital ledger that records transactions across many computers. This technology ensures that once a transaction is recorded, it cannot be altered or deleted. The ledger is divided into blocks, each containing a list of transactions, which are linked in chronological order. This system guarantees transparency and security, as all participants have access to the same information, making it difficult for anyone to tamper with the data."-- ### **Video 6: How BCH Transactions Are Processed**(Finished)"Processing a Bitcoin Cash transaction involves several steps. First, the transaction is broadcast to the network. Miners then verify the transaction by solving complex mathematical problems. Once verified, the transaction is added to a block on the blockchain. Thanks to Bitcoin Cash’s larger block size, this process is typically quicker than with other cryptocurrencies. As a result, transactions are confirmed faster and more efficiently."-- ### **Video 7: Scaling and Fees in BCH**"Bitcoin Cash handles scaling differently from Bitcoin by increasing the block size. This approach allows more transactions to be included in each block, which helps lower transaction fees. For example, while Bitcoin may experience high fees during busy periods, Bitcoin Cash maintains more stable and affordable fees. This scalability makes BCH a practical option for users who want to avoid high costs and slow processing times."-- ### **Video 8: Types of BCH Wallets**"When managing Bitcoin Cash, you have several wallet options. Software wallets are applications you can install on your phone or computer, offering ease of access. Hardware wallets are physical devices that provide enhanced security by keeping your private keys offline. Lastly, paper wallets involve printing your keys on paper, which can be a secure but less convenient option. Each type has its own advantages, so choose one based on your needs and preferences."-- ### **Video 9: How to Set Up a Software Wallet**"Setting up a software wallet for Bitcoin Cash is straightforward. Start by downloading a reputable wallet app from your app store or the wallet’s website. Follow the installation prompts and create a strong password to protect your wallet. It’s crucial to back up your recovery phrase, which is used to restore your wallet if needed. Once set up, you can start receiving and sending BCH easily using your software wallet."-- ### **Video 10: How to Set Up a Hardware Wallet**"To set up a hardware wallet, first connect the device to your computer using a USB cable. Follow the setup instructions provided by the manufacturer, which usually involve creating a PIN and writing down a recovery phrase. This recovery phrase is essential for restoring your wallet if it’s ever lost or damaged. Once set up, your hardware wallet will securely store your Bitcoin Cash offline, providing robust protection against theft."-- ### **Video 11: Securing Your Wallet**"Securing your Bitcoin Cash wallet involves a few key steps. Use a strong, unique password for your wallet to prevent unauthorized access. Enable two-factor authentication (2FA) if your wallet supports it, adding an extra layer of security. Regularly back up your wallet and recovery phrase, and store these backups in a safe place. By following these practices, you can significantly reduce the risk of losing your BCH or falling victim to fraud."-- ### **Video 12: Choosing an Exchange**"Choosing the right exchange is crucial when buying Bitcoin Cash. Look for exchanges like Coinbase, Binance, or Kraken, which are known for their reliability and user-friendly interfaces. Compare factors such as transaction fees, payment methods, and security features. Ensure the exchange you select supports Bitcoin Cash and offers adequate customer support. Doing your research helps ensure a smooth and secure buying experience."-- ### **Video 13: Buying BCH with Bank Transfer**"To buy Bitcoin Cash with a bank transfer, first log in to your exchange account. Navigate to the 'Buy' section and select Bitcoin Cash. Choose 'Bank Transfer' as your payment method. Enter the amount you wish to purchase and confirm the transaction. Bank transfers may take a few days to process, but once completed, your BCH will be added to your exchange account. Be sure to double-check all details before finalizing the purchase."-- ### **Video 14: Buying BCH with Credit Card**"Buying Bitcoin Cash with a credit card is quick and convenient. Start by logging into your exchange account and selecting Bitcoin Cash. Choose ‘Credit Card’ as your payment method. Enter your card details and the amount of BCH you want to purchase. After confirming the transaction, your BCH will be credited to your account almost instantly. Keep in mind that credit card purchases may involve higher fees compared to other methods."-- ### **Video 15: Tips for First-Time Buyers**"If you're new to buying Bitcoin Cash, here are some helpful tips. Start with a small amount to familiarize yourself with the process and minimize risk. Pay attention to transaction fees and exchange rates, as they can vary. Use secure payment methods and protect your personal information. Familiarize yourself with the exchange’s features and customer support options to ensure a smooth experience. These steps will help you make your first BCH purchase with confidence."-- ### **Video 16: Making a Payment with BCH**"Making a payment with Bitcoin Cash is simple. Open your wallet app and select the ‘Send’ option. Enter the recipient’s address and the amount of BCH you want to send. Double-check the details to ensure accuracy. Confirm the transaction and your BCH will be sent to the recipient. The process is quick, thanks to Bitcoin Cash’s efficient network, making it easy to use BCH for everyday payments."-- ### **Video 17: Merchants Accepting BCH**"Bitcoin Cash is increasingly accepted by various merchants. You can use BCH to pay for goods and services at both physical stores and online platforms. From restaurants to retail shops and e-commerce sites, more businesses are recognizing Bitcoin Cash. Check if your favorite merchants accept BCH and enjoy the convenience of using cryptocurrency in your daily transactions."-- ### **Video 18: Practical Tips for Using BCH**"When using Bitcoin Cash, there are a few practical tips to keep in mind. Always double-check the recipient’s address before sending any BCH to avoid mistakes. Keep track of your transactions and ensure you have enough BCH to cover any associated fees. By following these tips, you can effectively use BCH for various transactions and make the most out of its benefits."-- ### **Video 19: How BCH Transactions Work**"Bitcoin Cash transactions are processed through a series of steps. First, the transaction is broadcast to the BCH network. Miners then validate the transaction by solving complex puzzles. Once verified, it’s added to a block on the blockchain. Due to Bitcoin Cash’s larger block size, transactions are confirmed quickly and efficiently, making the process smooth and fast."-- ### **Video 20: BCH Fee Structure**"Bitcoin Cash offers a fee structure that is generally lower than Bitcoin’s. Fees are determined by the size of the transaction and current network demand. With Bitcoin Cash’s larger block size, fees are typically kept low, even during busy times. This makes BCH a cost-effective choice for users who want to avoid high transaction costs."-- ### **Video 21: Comparing BCH Fees to Other Cryptos**"Bitcoin Cash stands out for its low transaction fees compared to other cryptocurrencies. While Bitcoin may experience high fees during peak periods, BCH maintains more stable and affordable fees thanks to its larger block size. This affordability makes BCH a practical option for regular transactions and a strong competitor in the crypto space."-- ### **Video 22: Upcoming Developments**"Bitcoin Cash is set to undergo several exciting developments. These include updates aimed at enhancing scalability and introducing new features. Staying informed about these changes can help you understand how BCH will evolve and improve over time, making it a more robust cryptocurrency for the future."Sample WorksHere the 5 samples that I created from the script. 5 out of 22(in progress)Watch Them HereCost Break Down:1.97 BCH (22 Short Form Videos $30/Video)0.66 BCH (22 Thumbnails $10/thumbnail)Work Time Per Clip2-4 Hours of work Per Video(Animation, Graphic Editing/Designing, Effects,and sound effects application)Contacts:For suggestions, Comments, and questions please contact: contact.cmworks@gmail.com or t.me/CM_WorkzThank you!<3

title: BlockHack Hackathon - BCH Covenant Challenge
description: Support the Bitcoin Cash Covenant Challenge at Blockhack Fall Help Us Promote the Awareness and Adoption of Bitcoin Cash Covenants. Join us at the upcoming BlockHack Fall 2024, happening on November 8-10 in Toronto. This initiative aims to introduce the power of Bitcoin Cash covenants to a wider audience in the blockchain community, sparking interest and inspiring innovative ideas for real-world applications. What Are Bitcoin Cash Covenants? Bitcoin Cash covenants enable developers to program more complex conditions on transactions, opening up possibilities for applications like decentralized exchanges, token systems, smart contracts, and more! Despite their potential, many blockchain developers remain unaware of the advances happening in the Bitcoin Cash ecosystem. This challenge is designed to change that. Why Are We Raising Funds? All funds raised will go directly toward a prize pool for the Bitcoin Cash Covenant Challenge. The goal is to create a dedicated track for participants to explore the possibilities of covenant transactions, fostering the creation of new use cases and integrating covenants into broader blockchain solutions. The prize pool will reward the top three teams that come up with the most innovative and impactful ideas using Bitcoin Cash covenants. How Your Contribution Makes an Impact By contributing to this campaign, you are helping to: Raise Awareness: Spread knowledge of Bitcoin Cash covenants among the blockchain community. Promote Adoption: Encourage developers to experiment with covenant transactions and discover new ways to integrate them into diverse applications. Inspire Innovation: Create opportunities for teams to develop real-world solutions that leverage Bitcoin Cash's unique capabilities. Expected Outcomes The challenge aims to produce: Creative and practical projects that demonstrate the potential of Bitcoin Cash covenants. Broader adoption among blockchain developers who will bring new eyes and fresh perspectives to Bitcoin Cash. Increased recognition of Bitcoin Cash’s unique features within the blockchain and web3 communities. How Will the Funds Be Used? 100% of the funds will be allocated as prizes for the top three projects: 1st Place: 50% of the prize pool 2nd Place: 30% of the prize pool 3rd Place: 20% of the prize pool Your support will directly incentivize participants to think outside the box, ensuring that Bitcoin Cash covenants take center stage at this important event. Support the Future of Bitcoin Cash Development! Join us in making a difference at BlockHack Fall 2024 by contributing to the Bitcoin Cash Covenant Challenge. Let’s work together to show the blockchain world what’s possible with Bitcoin Cash and inspire the next wave of innovation.

title: Edmonton BCH Contract Learning
description: Hi!BCH now has the systems in place to more easily create apps and utilities, but we need more developers to actually create, own, and take care of those projects to provide a lasting value. In an effort to increase awareness, especially among new or prospective developers, I'm proposing to do my own small part by running info sessions in my local area (Edmonton, Canada). I'm aiming primarily to spark an interest in people that want to get into a space like this and may not know it even exists.The Current Plan (what this funding is for)Rent a meeting room for 4 hour sessions once a week for a month at different branches of the public library around the city of Edmonton, Canada.Advertise the free classes on eventbrite.caAdvertise the free classes at the local high schools, colleges, and universitiesThe Cost1.06 BCH ($530 CAD @ $500 CAD/BCH - Oct 28 2024)$400 CAD (4x $100 room rental)$130 CAD (roll-up retractable banner)Banner designI have a presentation made to help outline the capabilities of BCH vs BTC and EVM networks and how there are opportunities available in BCH for those interested.Some slide examples:Delivery?I do have some experience running 20+ person training classes in past jobs, so I know I can deliver. That said, public speaking has always been a major dislike of mine so I'm hoping starting small like this will ease me back into it and build up some confidence again since it's been quite awhile. And if there's one way to get me talking, it's starting a discussion about BCH...Future Plans?My hope is that this can become a more regular thing, and I can start doing free sessions at high schools/colleges/universities and other locations. I only just started working on this over the last few days so not much progress has been made, besides confirming that I can advertise any library-held sessions at the schools I've talked to so far. If the library sessions actually happen and I have something to show it being a 'real thing', rather than just a random guy talking about some 'blockchain' thing, then that wouldn't hurt the cause!I have done a little reaching out to educational non-profits in an attempt to get the library meeting room rental cost down 80%. So far no luck, but if it happens that would drastically extend how long the raised funds would last.I also do some long-distance trips to various places so while this funding won't be applied to it, doing sessions on my own outside of Edmonton could be a possibility later, and having a history of classes would help in lending some credibility there.Recordings?Not to start, maybe later. I get the value, but I also know it makes me irrationally nervous. Baby steps!Worst Case?At worst, nobody shows up and I waste travel time + 16 hours sitting in an empty meeting room working away on my laptop on <unannounced thing> :)Stretch Goals?I'm treating this as a proof-of-concept run so not hoping for any stretch goals, but if anyone insists then I'll say any extra funds will be somehow directly applied to this education purpose, with the default being every extra 0.2 BCH ($100 CAD) adds another class (room rental).

title: An Electron-Cash Plugin for Future BCH
description: Following on a successful 60 BCH flipstarter campaign in April '24, Future Bitcoin Cash has been developed, audited and funded on-chain since August. And an initial webapp was launched the following month to swap the tokens. It's all LIVE at: futurebitcoin.cash .The project enables locking BCH as Future CashTokens with incentives paid up front in BCH. The FBCH tokens can then be traded or sent to any BCH wallet with CashToken support. Some documentation, an archive of the original flipstarter, the audit and all the contract CashScript and BitcoinScript can found in the app.Webapps are great for reaching a lot of people. They target a low-cost, ubiquitous platform that almost everyone has in their pocket, but there are a number of drawbacks. An app as a webpage isn't great for a number of things, such as: high value transactions in a controlled environment, long running processes, or using a database with a lot of reliability or flexibility. Luckily, Bitcoin Cash has a well-established platform for more advanced feature integration, the Electron-Cash plugin system. Why do we need an Electron Cash Plugin if webapps are so great?In the case of FBCH, from a monetary perspective, it's somewhat irrelevant if 1000 people place 1 BCH or 1 person locks 1000 BCH. There's a lot of utility to be found catering to high net users in a platform they're accustomed to.So this campaign is to build out some "missing" features of FBCH as an Electron-Cash plugin: Sort coupons by expiration, rate, total yield, sat.Charting: historical charts of the prevailing rate.Market overview: a market-wide rate curve showing the prevailing coupon rate at different time scales.Automated coupon taking at user-defined rate.Auto-redemption of mature tokens.Builtin validation of FBCH series.Bootstrapped token metadata from static BCMR.Locally generated BCMR metadata for new series as minted. Stretch goal - A commission-free DeFi market: If the campaign exceeds the 30 BCH goal by 100% (60 BCH total), the deliverable will include an on-chain Defi system tailored to creating a liquid futures market with:Open ask-market style orders with partial fulfillment. Pricing token sales to the satoshi. No commissions.No centralized server or custom indexer. Why do we want a new kind of market? Currently, FBCH contracts allow swapping BCH and FBCH for the cost of network transaction fees alone. So to swap 10 BCH for FBCH costs about 550 sats, (a fee of 0.00005%). To swap just 0.1 BCH, cost the same sats, so it's about 0.005%.If a user then wants to sell their tokens, let's say 10 FBCH-01000000, there are two options currently: TapSwap (3.0% fee) and Cauldron (0.3% fee). If the seller wanted to liquidate their 10 FBCH-1e6 position, they could list it on Tapswap for something less than 9.7 BCH, and it will sell eventually, but the seller will eat 0.3 BCH in fees. While such that trade may be great for our good friend the market operator, it's not great for the seller. It's not great for price discovery or the liquidity of FBCH tokens. If someone has 1 FBCH in a series maturing in a week, the owner should be able to liquidate that position on an open market at a small discount, perhaps in the neighborhood of a 100k sat discount, but a 3% commission is 3M sats. And a Cauldron-style swap is a non-starter because it would not allow them to fully liquidate their position after the "constant product" price moved slightly and their tokens started selling for more than the locked BCH.From the FBCH Electron Plugin, it should be possible to list an "Ask" or offer to sell FBCH at a price to the sat. Anyone should be able to take FBCH from a seller's vault contract at that price, by paying BCH to the seller's address in the same transaction. This seller's vault contract would support partial fulfillment, meaning a seller could put 100 FBCH in a vault, to be taken all by 1 buyer, or in 1000 order of 0.1 FBCH each. Anyone will be able to derive the "market" contract from the categoryId of the token they wish to trade. All seller vaults and rates will be indexed by the NFT commitments sellers place on a market contract. TimelineThe initial plugin minimum viable app should take a few months. The market will be completed after the MVP plugin is complete.All products will be free and open source software. There will be no rent seeking or commission to use the swaping plugin or a market.

title: La Economía P2P: Episodios 110–144
description: Tu apoyo será clave para realizar una investigación exhaustiva y para impulsar campañas de concientización que fomenten la adopción de esta innovadora visión económica. Asimismo, hemos efectuado pagos al editor de videos utilizando Bitcoin Cash, reflejando nuestro compromiso con esta causa.Nuestros objetivos incluyen:Adquisición de luces para mejorar la calidad visual de nuestros episodios.Adquisición de micrófonos corbateros, lo que nos permitirá realizar entrevistas y grabar en distintos escenarios.Adquisición de una placa de sonido actualizada, para recibir invitados.Financiamiento de la producción de contenido de alta calidad, que abarca desde la investigación inicial hasta la creación de guiones, la edición de videos y la producción en general.Mejora significativa en la calidad de producción y edición para ofrecer una experiencia visual y auditiva excepcional.Continuar remunerando al editor de audio, reconociendo su valioso trabajo en el proyecto.Compensación justa para todo el equipo involucrado en la producción de los episodios, incluyendo creadores de contenido, investigadores, editores y otros colaboradores.Sostenimiento de los costos operativos necesarios como: alojamiento web, mantenimiento del sitio web y servicios de transmisión en línea, para garantizar una presencia constante y accesible en la web.Merchandising para repartir en encuentros de Argentina.Con tu apoyo, estaremos un paso más cerca de lograr nuestros objetivos y difundir el conocimiento sobre esta innovadora visión económica.Beneficios para donantesAgradecemos el aporte de todos los donantes. Para los que donen los 10 montos más grandes, incluiremos, previo contacto, un acceso de nivel 5 a la comunidad privada de La Economía P2P._________Your support will be key in conducting thorough research and driving awareness campaigns that promote the adoption of this innovative economic vision. Likewise, we have made payments to the video editor using Bitcoin Cash, reflecting our commitment to this cause.Our goals include:Acquiring lights to improve the visual quality of our episodes.Acquiring lapel microphones, which will allow us to conduct interviews and record in different settings.Acquiring an updated sound card to host guests.Funding the production of high-quality content, from initial research to scriptwriting, video editing, and overall production.Significantly improving production and editing quality to deliver an exceptional visual and auditory experience.Continuing to compensate the audio editor, recognizing their valuable work on the project.Fair compensation for all team members involved in the production of episodes, including content creators, researchers, editors, and other collaborators.Covering necessary operational costs such as web hosting, website maintenance, and online streaming services to ensure a constant and accessible web presence.Merchandising for distribution at meetups in Argentina.With your support, we’ll be one step closer to achieving our goals and spreading knowledge about this innovative economic vision.Benefits for DonorsWe appreciate the contribution of all donors. For the top 10 largest donations, we will include, after contacting them, level 5 access to the private community of La Economía P2P.__________Más información:Sitio web: https://laeconomiap2p.com/Canal de Youtube: https://www.youtube.com/@LaEconomiaP2P/Canal de Telegram: https://t.me/LaEconomiaP2PX: https://twitter.com/LaEconomiaP2P

title: Do you think Cheapy made a difference?
description: Sometimes I think to myself that I am not doing enough despite spending the majority of my waking hours working on BCH stuff. Doing regular audits of BCHF activities puts it in perspective a bit and I start to feel the opposite and think yeah.. I have actually done quite a bit.BCH has no boss, no king, no pre-mine booty to distribute, no dictator. There is no one to hire staff to get jobs done. There is no one to pay a salary. The way the community decides the way forward is by supporting the things they like and conversely not supporting the things they do not. Support can be shown many ways and one of the ways of showing support is financially. Most commonly fund raisers have been for developers. Wallets and infrastructure and algorithms that the lay person is incapable of providing. It is relatively easy to see and understand the value they add if they deliver what it is they set out to do. The thing is created and becomes available.But what about all the little non developer things that fall through the cracks? A functioning society also needs “someone” to pick up the trash, fight forest fires, someone to send out reminders about upcoming events and someone to gather and collect all the tasks that someone has not yet done. BCH needs someones to do many little things too. That is what I have chosen to do as I think it is where I have been, and can be, the most effective and needs the most attention.This campaign is probably a bit different than the typical BCH fund raiser. Typically people have some plan and they lay it out and then ask for funds in advance and people hope that they deliver at some point in the future. This campaign however is the opposite. I have done many things and paid for the expenses out of my own pocket, dedicated a boatload of time and now I’m asking the community if they think it is has been valuable to the ecosystem and if so has that work and effort been worth paying for after the fact.Originally I was going to try to think of ways to make the BCHF self sustaining with various income streams and services (as you can read about here) but after asking around it seems that we are still too small to warrant paid services that I can offer. Also, after consulting with a lawyer and an accountant, currently BCH is too small to warrant the massive cost and burden of setting up an NPO. This leaves me in a bit of a quandary.I did a survey on Twitter and Telegram: In both surveys “Direct Donation” was selected as the preferred option. Unfortunatelydespite the donation address being included on bitcoincashfoundation.org and on the news posts/videos, my rough estimate shows that the total donations (depending on the current BCH price) would not even cover the expenses I have paid myself (not a single sat has moved from donations while I have been contemplating the future of the project) let alone the vast amount of time and effort I have put in. Seeing as Bitcoin Cash works on proof of work, I am asking the community to look at my proof of work and decide if my efforts have been worth making.Q. What did BCH already get?What You Are Already Getting:Management of the Coinmarketcap.com Bitcoin Cash account with over 200k followers.The weekly BCH news in text published on 12 different platforms (over 70 weeks!).The weekly BCH news video published on 14 different platforms.Audio “podcast” version of the news on Spotify.The BCH devs and builders Telegram channel with moderation.Of the 6 telegram mods, 52% of ALL moderation activities (banning bots/removing spam etc.) in https://t.me/bchchannel since March were done by me, as well as creation and co-ordination of its mod channel and log bot as well as the creation of almost all bot filters.The BCHF rosebot federation (If anyone wants to join the BCHF telegram federation and you use Rosebot you can just paste this in your chat "/joinfed 1b2d268b-cc41-404f-b446-1a5712a6fc58").The creation of and moderation of https://t.me/bchpricechannelThe creation of and moderation of https://t.me/BCHMEMESbcash.lolThe creation of and moderation The BCHF Discord to co-ordinate the BCH swarm and provide a place for people to post post job ads, newbies to get their questions answered, as well as groups for focused discussions as needed.Moderation of the Flipstarter, r/Cashtokens, Telegrams.Being a contact point via the BCHF where various people and news organizations have been able to reach out to to get up to date and timely information regarding BCH.Outreach to major stakeholders regarding upgrading the 2024 BCH upgrade (around 300 contacts) and again when there was a bug fix.Discover.cash (with General Protocols).“Bitcoin Cash Accepted Here” stickers shipped to almost anywhere in the world FOR FREE. (since launch we have shipped 40 stickers to 9 countries.BitcoinCash logos and other relevant media uploaded to 4 stock image websites.Media Archive.Flipwatch.org Phase I.Transparency and accountability.Q. What do you HOPE to deliver in the future?What I Hope to Do:ALL OF THE ABOVE! And…………………..Expanded outreach!Flipwatch phase II with in depth reviews with data open for all.Revival of BitcoinCash Network Discussions. (A video series of talks with people all over the ecosystem).Tutorial video series. (How to use cashtokens/an offline wallet/wallet comparison etc.)Media Kit.Tagging and organizing the meme archive at bcash.lol .Wallet/tools directory. (currently it is a bit hard to find all the things developers need).Another Fulcrum Server.Another Chaingraph Server.The “Conference Crew” (I hope to get BCH represented at generally crypto conferences).The “Mod Squad”(a team of mods available for BCH projects to hire).A BCH logo “Lora” for Ai image generation.A “No Middle Man” woocommerce plugin so shops can accept BCH without needing external services.Asset reclamation. (“@BCH” “@BitcoinCash” etc. across social media).A BCH timeline/history (Alex has already created and amazing graphic timeline. I mean a in depth written/filmed history by the parties involved.)BCH Stats page (BCH energy/defi.None of the above is guaranteed. But things I would like to expand into if time allows.Q. In the survey I said I want to give a direct donation! I don’t want to use FUNDME! I understand. Feel free to send donations to this address. Any funds donated to this address will be added to the fundme campaign on your behalf.bitcoincash:qpz6w3fkwfhj02ed7mkktyfksfvymcmwk5dfvmsfd9Q. If this is not funded does that mean you will stop doing what you do?A. No, but I certainly wont be expanding things. Depending on the realities of needing to earn an income via other means my time will be divided between BCH and a means of generating an income. That may mean needing to scale back or adjust the amount of time I dedicate to BCH to some degree.Q. Is this a fundraiser to fund all those things you hope to do? Is this a promise to get those things done? A. No, I hope to work on these things and expand the BCHF’s scope but as there are a lot of unknowns and moving pieces. I do not promise that any of them will get done. If this campaign succeeds you can be sure next year around this time there will be another and you should judge whether or not the activities I have done between then and now are worth the asking price of those things I have done in the interim.Q. Why should we pay for things we already got for free?A. If you think what I have done over the past 2 years has added value to the space and helped BCH become what you want it to be and you want to see my work continue or even MORE of it. Or you think my activities help raise the value of BCH and therefore your stack/bag and you want to see it continue or even MORE of it.Q. How many hours do you REALLY spend on this stuff?For a while I was attempting to keep track of my time. However I found it gets really murky. I have Telegram/Discord open all day everyday. While I am awake I spend most of my day at the PC and monitor every message that comes in across all the BCH groups I mod. Is it fair to measure time I am watching cat videos on reddit (with an eye on TG on my other screen)? As I already pointed out, out of the 6 BCH channel mods, 52% of mod actions are done by me and according to combot statistics for BCH channel I am also often the #1 poster in the group or at least in the top 3 depending on the week. (obviously a lot of posts are banter but I also try to help as much as I can by pointing people in the right direction. That is also just 1 of the many groups I participate in.The other work I do however takes vastly more dedicated and focused time. The news takes many hours to research, write, edit and post across all 12 platforms every week as well as then filming, editing, post-processing and posting the news video every week. During the annual upgrade I researched and verified the contact information and status of more than 300 businesses and individuals who may need to be informed about BCH’s annual upgrade and reached out to them. I repeated the process and hounded many of those contacts when there was a bug fix for BCHN later in the year as well which took probably hundreds of hours in total including follow up. Not to mention all the other outreach such as contacting various business about incorrect BCH logos/descriptions and all the things in the “What You Are Already Getting” section. I am sorry I can not give you an exact number but most of my waking hours are BCH focused or adjacent.Q. What happened to this plan: ?A. That is still a possible future for the BCHF when the BCH community is large enough to warrant and such activities. Once the ecosystem as reached critical mass, the BCHF will reconsider the options to become and NPO or engage in more self sustaining activities. All the activities of the BCHF will help towards making that possible future a reality.BCH’s Foundation Isn’t Built Alone—Let’s Lay the Next Block Together. In a decentralized world, where peer-to-peer electronic cash aims to break down barriers and empower individuals, each person’s contribution matters. I really believe that BCH is now more aligned and focused than we have ever been and we are making real headway. By supporting efforts like mine for the common good, you're not just funding one person—you’re helping build and sustain an entire ecosystem committed to financial empowerment and freedom for the whole world. Every step we take together brings us closer to a reality where digital cash works, peer-to-peer, for everyone, everywhere, and no one is left behind. Change doesn't happen overnight, nor by the efforts of a single entity; it happens when passionate communities like the BCH community unites to make the world a better, and freer place.I believe in, and am committed to, BCH’s potential to revolutionize not only the way we transact but the way we collaborate, innovate, and thrive without reliance on a central authority.Your support is more than just a donation. It’s a vote of approval for my ongoing efforts to help build something that truly makes a difference.Thank you for considering my work as part of that mission, and if you are reading this thank you for helping make BCH money for everyone with all the efforts you make as well.Let’s continue to make history together. A special shout out to those of you I asked to proof read this and give me the feedback I needed to hear.

title: The Bitcoin Cash Podcast: Episodes 136 - 145
description: # Flipstarter: Episodes 136 - 145 of The Bitcoin Cash Podcast### Funding requested: 18 BCH (~$600 / BCH)## Deliverable- Episodes 136 - 145. The same show you already know and (hopefully) love.## Notes### FundMePrevious campaign was a successful transition to FundMe, happy to be back on the platform. Thanks again to Sayoshi. Progress is clear on having a convenient central point to assess desired community needs plus a record of past fundraises. I think a great future innovation would be some kind of Patreon-style subscription payments where users could load a balance one time and have it distribute to chosen creators on a recurring basis with the ability to revoke their subscription to any creator if delivery wasn't matching expectations. I'm sure we'll get there.### VisibilityThere's a number of facts contributing to the slow & steady continuing increase of Podcast reach (now approaching 10k Twitter followers & 2.5k Youtube subscribers).The traction of major projects, particularly with the rising amount of BCH DeFi, is growing. BCH Bull & Cauldron are stand outs in this regard.Price is rising in USD terms while holding steady or even creeping up slightly in BTC terms. As ever, this is fantastic for our global & crypto industry mindshare.BCH is dead FUD has essentially died (once again) & is now being followed by a fresh round of "maybe the bcashers were right?" This is no doubt also feeding into a general rise in visibility of the Podcast. We'll be winning over converts that contribute to the next round of hushed whispers that lead to increased visibility & more "bCaSH is DeAd, I CaN KeEp iGnOrInG It!" cope.## Badgers.cashI discovered that BADGER token, obtainable only by provably time-locking BCH on-chain, has traded as high as #10 on Cauldron. Speculative liquidity thus created a **native BCH yield** for time-locking BCH. This is an incredible community innovation, as it allows anyone to yield on their BCH by provably HODLing (boosting price, liquidity & thus marketing reach) and it also allows anyone to generate goodwill in the community by provably purchasing & burning BADGER. It's always been possible to "donate" to all BCH holders by burning BCH itself, but that's psychologically unappealing due to the limited supply of BCH. BADGER avoids that, is fun, generates more transactions & usage of CashTokens tech.In order to boost Badger TVL, what is needed is some kind of non-speculative demand to buy (& ideally burn) BADGER tokens. My idea for this is that community projects can show goodwill to the community by provably burning & I'm going to start with the Podcast. As an experiment, 2.7% (0.5 BCH) of the desired raised funds will be used to purchase Badger on Cauldron.quest and provably burn it. Readers are more than welcome to frontrun this by purchasing BADGER, which achieves somewhat the same effect! If this caught on as a community initiative by aligned projects, it would encourage more people to lock BCH, boosting the price and bringing more resources to the community to run even more projects (which can burn more BADGER). Raising BCH on-chain TVL is one of the few ways to defeat naked short selling, Tether inflation or other manipulation of the BCH price so I am very interested in new & potentially compelling ways to encourage this.We'll see if funders are enthusiastic about this experiment &/or if Badger TVL continues to grow. If it does, maybe I'll continue it as a regular part of the Flipstarter. As of time of writing (Monday 9th December 2024), Badger has 65 BCH TVL in 44 locks & BADGER is $0.0091 with $731 TVL & $1288 30d volume.## Note to the communityAlong with The BCH Podcast & General Protocol's Spaces, a big shoutout to Paul for his continuing great work on his Youtube channel & also Fiendish for his new Fiendish & Friends weekly Space which is very promising.Let's keep building Bitcoin Cash.## Proof of WorkI have aggregated previous delivery & community work (including previous FundMe + 5x Flipstarter delivery & accountability) on a dedicated page on the Bitcoin Cash Podcast website. See it here: https://bitcoincashpodcast.com/proof-of-work.### Cost breakdown:- 0.6 BCH topic research, episode hosting- 0.6 BCH production & editing costs- 0.1 BCH Thumbnail graphics & episode distribution- 0.1 BCH Timestamps, links & episode summary- 0.1 BCH Youtube Shorts creation & distribution- 0.1 BCH Instagram & TikTok videos & management1.6 BCH / episodex10 episodes = 16 BCH0.5 BCH: BADGER burn.1 BCH misc expenses: Ongoing costs such as website hosting & domain registration, Zoom subscription, supporting other community fundraisers, FAQ maintenance etc.0.5 BCH: Fundme.cash fee## Contributor rewards:Donations of > 0.5 BCH will receive a shoutout at the end of each show (like the Patrons and Sponsors) unless your name/message is rude or slanderous or you ask me not to or it's otherwise unreadable for some reason.## DisclaimerI will be completing the deliverables whether or not the Flipstarter gets funded, the continuation of the show & my assistance to the community is in no way contingent on this funding request. However, as per previous Flipstarter, I believe this is a valuable community service & get a lot of feedback in that regard. If funding stops I will eventually have to work harder on alternative monetisation strategies (possibly advertising, sponsored episodes) though.## ContactAs always, for any questions, comments or suggestions I can be reached at jeremy@bitcoincashpodcast.com or on Telegram https://t.me/BitcoinCashPodcast.

title: Support a 27-year old PhD Student
description: Support a Dedicated 27-Year-Old PhD Student in Achieving Their Academic Goals I am currently pursuing a PhD in Chemistry in Budapest, but the doctoral scholarship I receive is unfortunately very modest—approximately 140,000 HUF per month (around $360). This amount is insufficient to cover basic living expenses.To make matters more challenging, I had to repay a previous scholarship, despite dedicating 1.5 years of work to it. Unfortunately, no publication resulted from this research, which led to the repayment obligation. I am seeking financial assistance to replace the support I lost, which provided essential funding for my living costs.The scholarship amounted to 200,000 HUF ($510) per month for 18 months, totaling $9,180. While I don't expect to raise the full amount, even a small contribution would be a tremendous help.I do have a small investment in Bitcoin Cash (BCH)—four coins purchased at an average price of $360 each. However, I would prefer not to sell this investment at the moment.I am asking for help to address this $9,180 debt, and I assure you that every dollar received will go toward repaying this obligation. Additionally, I aim to continue my educational activities at the university, where I teach for only a minimal stipend, reflecting the low compensation for such work in Hungary.I sincerely thank you in advance for your support. At the end of the campaign, I am willing to provide proof of repayment for the first $2,000 installment to anyone who requests it. Can be contacted on Telegram at @Hobby0101

title: Support High-Quality Bitcoin Cash Content.
description: Help fund content that educates, inspires, and grows the BCH ecosystem!In December 2024, I launched an initiative to create high-quality content that invites and educates more people about Bitcoin Cash (BCH). My goal is to make BCH more accessible, understandable, and widely adopted.To make this possible, I need to raise 1 BCH per month for 12 months—a total of 12 BCH—to dedicate myself full-time to researching, writing, and publishing BCH-focused content across multiple platforms.So far, the support from the community and fans of my work has been exceptional. Thanks to contributions, my content has:✅ Reached over 100,000+ views on Read.Cash✅ Gained 9,000+ views last month alone on Publish0x✅ Expanded to 4+ platforms where BCH discussions happenWhy This Campaign MattersBitcoin Cash isn’t just a payment tool—it’s a path to financial freedom. But too many people still don’t understand its power. Worse, misinformation about Bitcoin (BTC) dominates, while BCH’s superior technology and usability go unnoticed.🔹 Did you know BCH has DeFi through CashTokens?🔹 Did you know ZKP roll-ups are coming in May with the Velma Upgrade?🔹 Did you know BTC has been hijacked and can no longer function as digital cash?These are critical facts that people need to know, and content is the bridge that brings them into the BCH ecosystem. The more we educate, the faster Bitcoin Cash adoption grows.Why I Need 1 BCH MonthlyAs a full-time BCH content creator, all my earnings go toward sustaining this mission. Researching, writing, and engaging with the community takes time—time that could otherwise be spent on other work. Raising 1 BCH per month ensures that I can:🚀 Continue creating high-quality BCH content without interruption📢 Reach even more people through multiple platforms📚 Cover essential topics like BCH adoption, use cases, and ecosystem updatesHow You Can SupportIf you believe in Bitcoin Cash and want to see more people learning about it, I invite you to:💚 Contribute to this campaign (every BCH counts!)🔄 Share this campaign with others who support BCH education📢 Engage with and promote BCH content to spread awarenessExclusive Perks for Contributors (Optional).As a supporter, you’ll receive:⭐ Mentions at the end of my articles🔗 Direct links to your website, brand, or project📢 Custom content to help promote your workJoin the Movement – Let’s Build the BCH Future Together!This isn’t just about me—it’s about growing Bitcoin Cash adoption and awareness worldwide. If we don’t tell people the truth, who will?Together, we can break the misinformation cycle, empower people with financial knowledge, and make BCH the global currency it was meant to be.💰 Contribute now and be part of the movement!Follow my work and explore more BCH content:📌 X | Reddit | Read.Cash | Publish0x | Grill | RealBitcoin.Cash | Bastyon | Telegram | email: alberdioni8406@gmail.com | Direct tips/donations: https://tipb.ch/alberdioni8406

title: Murray Store: A Market Way
description: Hello, my beloved guardians of liberty and monetary freedom!I have a project to put Bitcoin Cash in everyone's pockets and get people to start using it. I also think it is essential to spread and solidify the ideas of freedom.It's a simple way. Let me show you my vision.Please read, it's important. At the bottom, you can find my contact so you can ask me any questions.WHO I AM AND WHAT I DOMy name is Alan Mendilaharzu, and I'm from Argentina.I'm 36 years old, and I've been working on the revolution of freedom since 2020. It has been a long journey until I discovered Bitcoin Cash.
