And when I did, I found not only the best weapon to protect freedom, but also an amazing community with the same values.I have sympathy for Javier Milei, since I first saw him on TV in 2018 screaming at a left-wing 'journalist.' At that time, everyone around me was saying, 'He is a crazy bad guy,' but I visualized that he was exactly what our country needed, and said it. And time has proven that I was right.I was learning about the financial system on my own since my early 20s to understand how to protect and grow my money, and, in short, I discovered the robbery by central banks. Here, in the government indoctrination system, they brainwash you with the idea that the state is needed and good for everything, and libertarianism was virtually nonexistent. So, it was not easy.In my case, I have always felt rejection against the state, but it was more visceral than conscious.I don't remember if I reached libertarianism through Milei or another influencer, but what I can tell you is that I became radical because of the totalitarian fake pandemic Covid quarantine.In 2020, I became a very active freedom activist and got to know many people, institutions, books, movies, and great referents I am in contact with.My objective was to put the ideas of freedom in the street and in the hearts of the people.With a group of young libertarians, we were the first people to go to and organize rallies with the Gadsden flag. We were 'Pibes Libertarios' ('Young Libertarians,' yes, I was the oldest). We needed to explain to people what that represents at the time. The far left, who was in power, laughed at us, and the false right (the less left-wing opposition) attacked us. But we continued, even though the world was against us.I founded the 'Nueva Centro Derecha' or 'NCD' ('New Center Right,' because we didn't want to be confused with Nazis). When we created that organization, being right-wing was 'politically incorrect.'Later, I founded 'Unión y Libertad' or 'UnLi' ('Union and Liberty) to protest against the 'sanitary' Covid restrictions.The hardest part of that was uniting people with different reasons under the same cause.With time, we can see the results of our fight: we put the first anarcho-capitalist president in the history of humanity. And we are changing public opinion. But that is only the first step. I see it as pure propaganda, not real change.What we really need is, as Rothbard said, that most of the people identify with libertarianism.With the NCD, in coordination with other groups, we organized the largest protest with millions of people moving across different parts of the country. It took place when it was forbidden to leave your house. You can search for November 8, 2020.https://www.instagram.com/p/CHYDjlXANpXWe also organized the first public appearance of the right-wing influencer Tipito Enojado, when it was forbidden to have more than three people gathered. We had legal problems, but luckily, great libertarian lawyers helped us.https://www.instagram.com/p/CLAAf_WA5OzHere you can see, we made the first political approach between the two most influential right-wing figures in Argentina, Javier Milei and Patricia Bullrich, when all their factions hated each other.But now, they work together and make a great team, mostly thanks to the NCD that planted that seed.https://www.instagram.com/tv/CL9UsRcguA7Here is a picture of how the libertarian movement was not so long ago here. We were the first (in the streets).https://www.instagram.com/p/CIuhOoZASDGWith UnLi, we organized several manifestations against Sanitarian Tyranny, this is one of the biggest.https://www.instagram.com/p/CY1O5MJu4WL/?img_index=5I also founded "Juntadas Libertarias" ("Libertarian meetings"), which were about drinking beer, discussing ideas, and having good times, to bring libertarianism into the real world, and expand and strengthen the decentralized network. The guys from BitcoinCash Argentina came to one of those meetings, and that's how I met them.We held the biggest libertarian non-political meeting, where they were able to talk about BCH to a hundred new people. One BTC maxi friend of mine there got very angry lol.https://www.instagram.com/p/ClolBpzuZ7H/?img_index=4Later, they did a very successful BCH Experience in the same place. I invited a lot of people too.https://www.instagram.com/reel/CvaM9_7LBi8This was a small glimpse of what I did. There was much more and a lot of work behind this.I also added a friend's bar to accept BCH.https://www.instagram.com/reel/DDIGnMMxSrvAnd we accept BCH with another friend who does moving.https://www.instagram.com/p/C_WjCTTvuEbThis is one of the early meetings with Pibes Libertarios.THE IDEAWhat matters is that I generated a giant impact without any resources. I'm a regular person with a regular job who reacted to socialism and worked for his freedom.Until 2022, we had a great libertarian community growing, but ironically, when Milei won, the movement got poisoned by politics. Now we have a lot of Mileists, which is good (for now), but not so many libertarians. We need the ideas to transcend and every individual to adopt them in their soul.It was a long way to get here, and there is more to do. To continue and make that change a reality, I need some support for the next step.In 2020, I envisioned a freedom revolution gestated in three steps:1. Milei as president, like a key player, who will impact the entire world.2. Liberate people's minds from the state parasite.3. I don't know what or how it will occur, but people will have monetary freedom in the process.At this point, what is needed is to free people from the state's brain chains. For that, we need them to understand how they are robbed, not only economically, but also in their freedom and life.And the biggest scam of all is central banks, worldwide.Also, what BCH really needs is more people using it. We have an excellent currency and many tools, but we need more adoption.THE PROJECTIn these years, I have accumulated a lot of knowledge, friends, and contacts.Since some time ago, I have wanted to start a brand that people who identify with freedom use proudly, and make liberty propaganda with it.It's about libertarian articles, but not necessarily so explicit.Yes, there will be some Milei shirts for those who want them, but I want to put products on the market with phrases and images of Rothbard, Mises, Ayn Rand, Milton Friedman, just as an example.I want to see people using libertarian apparel normally, everywhere.I wish people feel and know that they are part of and contributing to a free world by using these products.This is when Murray Store is born.MURRAY will be a brand that sells a variety of products such as caps, t-shirts, pins, souvenirs, keychains, magnets, mugs, coffee cups, pens, bottle openers, collapsible cups, mates, fitness gloves, kitchen aprons, mouse pads, cushions, seed pillows, and more, and contribute to expanding freedom.For that reason, the most important part is to promote freedom money. With a 10% discount in BCH and 5% cashback in BCH for those who pay with other methods, a lot of people will, and have the incentive to, join Bitcoin Cash.Also, 10% of the earnings will go to libertarian social activism of all types. That can range from small things like putting pamphlets on the street with liberty ideas, to organizing events, charity, supporting educational institutions, all aimed at expanding freedom ideas and the use of BCH. People who buy these products will know that they are helping to create a freer world.When clients who receive BitcoinCash for the first time ask what to do with it, they can be directed to other shops and websites to spend it.Some concept examples: I think that if this business has even a small success, others will join BCH payments.WHAT I HAVEI have good suppliers.Friends and contacts who can help me with mass publicity on social media. I leave you a list of some of them with a reference link:Pibes Libertarioshttps://www.instagram.com/pibeslibertariosDanannhttps://youtube.com/@danannoficialAgustín Etchebarnehttps://x.com/aetchebarneLilia Lemoinehttps://www.instagram.com/lilialemoineMartín Krausehttps://x.com/martinkrauseLos Herederos de Alberdihttps://youtube.com/@losherederosdealberdiLu Palavecinohttps://www.instagram.com/lu.palavecino1Marilúhttps://x.com/marilonguiTipito Enojadohttps://youtube.com/@tipitoenojadoAlejandro Bonggiovanihttps://x.com/alejobongioGustavo Lazzarihttps://x.com/lachaAlejandro Fargosihttps://x.com/fargosiJuani Fernándezhttps://x.com/juanif17Santiago Oríahttps://x.com/Santiago_OriaNicolás Marquezhttps://x.com/NickyMarquez1Álvaro Zicarellihttps://x.com/alzicarelliAlso, I have direct conversations with Javier Milei for years, but they are not very frequent now since he became President. Totally understood, given the context.And plenty more, that are relevant but hard for you to understand.But more importantly, I have the ability to grow ideas, organize, create movements, and reach people's minds.THE WAYThe plan is to make a lot of efficient publicity, give incentives to share, like a referral program, and gain ground in the market. Selling at an affordable price, in massive amounts.I expect to generate a great impact and for BCH to enter in an indirect way. Subtle, almost subliminal. But powerful.We are lions fighting for freedom, but most people will need guidance at first.With this, it doesn't mean that everyone will become anarcho-capitalist, but BCH will expand exponentially, and I hope a lot of people will join the movement for freedom money.WHAT IS NEEDEDA website and social networks need to be created, I have the knowledge of how to do it. And a lot of time is needed for selling, managing, and logistics.For this purpose, I need some basic tools to work: A basic smartphone for receiving contacts, communications, and social networks.Example:https://tiendaonline.movistar.com.ar/samsung-galaxy-a35-128gb-5g.htmlA functional laptop for work and creating good designs.Example:https://www.mercadolibre.com.ar/notebook-lenovo-intel-core-i5-1235u-16gb-ram-256gb-ssd-pantalla-156-full-hd-windows-11/p/MLA44159616Some stock to quickly cover initial demand.And at the beginning, it will be necessary to sacrifice earnings, so I need financing to fully dedicate my time to this for the first three months.THE GOALWhat I can promise is that BitcoinCash will reach a lot of people.But it will depend on the community to absorb them. I think we are ready. BCH is ready. Bitcoin is ready.Initially, it will be mostly for Argentina, but I wish to reach the world.My biggest dream will be to grow, have international commerce, and pay employees with BitcoinCash.FINAL DATAOnce that I have the tools, I will innaugurate and start promoting in 3 weeks. I established the amount of BCH for this campaign higher than what I calculated, because of the variations in prices and unexpected expenses.I will be publishing the progress on my Twitter:https://x.com/AlanM1989bch_This is my contact, so you can ask any questions or request more information. I listen to every suggestion.Telegram:t.me/AlanmbchAll my social networks:https://linktr.ee/alan.mendilaharzuI wrote this by hand and later corrected it with ChatGPT. I hope I expressed myself correctly in English.I pray that you like the project and can help me. I will be extraordinarily grateful.Thank you for reading.

title: RealBitcoin.Cash - BCH Community Server on Lemmy
description: Hi,this is CashDragon. I am running a Lemmy instance for the Bitcoin Cash community on Lemmy and this campaign is to cover the hosting, maintenance, and administration costs for the year of 2025. The reason for this is to broaden the reach of Bitcoin Cash related topics as well as decrease reliance on centralized big-tech owned platforms.You can visit the site @ https://realbitcoin.cash/If you have questions please join the Matrix BCH chatroom space @ https://matrix.to/#/#bitcoincashspace:matrix.org---------------------Lemmy is a decentralized by federation Reddit style platform which interconnects many instances to create a large collection of communities.Find out more about Lemmy and the fediverse here: https://join-lemmy.org/

title: CryptoBakery: Simple BCH, in Spanish and for all.
description: What is CryptoBakery?It is an adoption project for Bitcoin Cash with the aim of massifying Satoshi Nakamoto's original idea by disseminating content in digital and alternative media, always focused on educating, entertaining and informing, dedicated to Spanish-speaking people who are not coiners and confused by BTC. .We massify the concepts of the Bitcoin Cash ecosystem, we take it down from the pedestal of developers and academics and - without distorting it - we turn it into a mass consumer product, easily digestible and suitable for all audiences.Our visionTo be the main multiplatform media leader in Spanish-speaking content dedicated to the adoption of Bitcoin Cash and its ecosystem.Our valuesWe make the complex simple.We have demonstrated the possibility of acquiring knowledge in a fun way while establishing an environment of coexistence and peace through digital platforms.Responsibility - Truthfulness - Quality - Honesty - Prudence - Ethics - Commitment - Integrity - Innovation - Perseverance - Seriousness - IdentityAction Plan for CryptoBakeryCryptoBakery has the vision of becoming the leading digital medium in Spanish-speaking content, for this we will start with two fundamental strategies: Build a digital media network for the dissemination of the BCH ecosystem for Spanish-speaking people and make the Bitcoin Cash ecosystem known to the Spanish-speaking population. The language we will use will be colloquial, close and pleasant; respecting and without distorting the central ideas of the Bitcoin Cash developers. Our general objective is to be an introductory medium for new non-coin users and/or users of other cryptocurrencies who are confused about Satoshi Nakamoto's original idea distorted by the current BTC community.Strategy: Build a digital media network to disseminate the BCH ecosystem for Spanish-Speaking people.Goals:Create Website and its content.Profiles on Instagram, Twitter (X) and Tiktok. And its content.Tactics:Website development through the WIX service: fast and intuitive.Creation of content strategy and content calendar for Instagram and Tiktok profiles.Humanize and replicate content through Twitter (X).Strategy: Make Bitcoin Cash known to the Spanish-speaking population.Goals:Reach 10,000 followers on Instagram in a range of 4 to 6 months.Reach 10,000 followers on Tiktok in a range of 4 to 6 months.Reach 20,000 visits to our website in a range of 4 to 6 months.Tactics:Publication of advertisements in digital media.Optimize social profiles to generate traffic to the Website.Creation, editing and assembly and dissemination of creative, educational and informative content.What I have.I started this project with all my heart and soul. My dream and main source of inspiration has been my bakery, however, it's no longer operational due to various issues I've already discussed on my Read Cash profile. Therefore, I've decided to dedicate myself to promoting the digital networks of other entrepreneurs, including my beloved Bitcoin Cash community. We need to continue expanding our reach in all languages.I've been able to move forward with the development of the website and the content strategy for the Instagram profile. The TikTok profile is under construction and will focus on sharing clips from the English-speaking BCH community with Spanish subtitles and videos with information on new Bitcoin Cash applications.Website: https://holdbch.wixsite.com/cryptobakeryInstagram: https://instagram.com/cryptobakeryve?igshid=NzZlODBkYWE4Ng==X: https://twitter.com/CryptoBakeryVe?t=KQP8FWvcQvRGJNM5e-njgA&s=09About me and my history: https://read.cash/@ramonoropeza/my-life-in-bitcoin-cash-38d8357aWebsite viewPreview of the Future Instagram Feed (Content calendar is ready)It would be great to have your support for this!8.5 BCH Equipment needed to create quality content.8.5 BCH Ad Payments, Domain and Site Hosting, and Professional Development Courses.4 BCH Memberships to Content Creation Resources and Apps4 BCH Emergency and Contingency Funds.Total: 25 BCH.I am very excited to be able to contribute directly to the BCH ecosystem and be able to work for everyone. If you would like to support this cause, please let us know through fundme.cash. Grateful for your support, thank you very much.More About Cryptobakery Proyect: https://read.cash/@ramonoropeza/cryptobakery-triggering-mass-adoption-of-bitcoin-cash-through-small-businesses-9fec92acAbout me and my history: https://read.cash/@ramonoropeza/my-life-in-bitcoin-cash-38d8357aContact: https://twitter.com/CryptoBakeryVe?t=KQP8FWvcQvRGJNM5e-njgA&s=09 Telegram: @monorevolutionramonos17@gmail.com

title: The Bitcoin Cash Podcast: Episodes 146 - 155
description: Episodes 146 - 155 of The Bitcoin Cash Podcast Funding requested: 45 BCH (~$320 / BCH) Deliverable Episodes 146 - 155. The same show you already know and (hopefully) love. bitcoincashpodcast.com Notes FundMe Very happy to be doing my third FundMe campaign. All the previous campaigns have gone really smoothly with the help of this tool. And if the early work on OpenCashDAO (github.com/OpenCashDAO) is any indication, maybe in a few months we'll have even more advanced funding tools! Visibility Podcast visibility & clout continues to rise. And viewer feedback has been that some of the recent episodes have been the best ever. Badgers.cash My last round of buring BADGER was a really fun experiment, so I have allocated some funds in the FundMe to once again do a provable burn. Haven't managed to make this a community meme yet, but it's not been a big focus.-- Explanation of Badgers belowI discovered that BADGER token, obtainable only by provably time-locking BCH on-chain, has traded as high as #10 on Cauldron. Speculative liquidity thus created a **native BCH yield** for time-locking BCH. This is an incredible community innovation, as it allows anyone to yield on their BCH by provably HODLing (boosting price, liquidity & thus marketing reach) and it also allows anyone to generate goodwill in the community by provably purchasing & burning BADGER. It's always been possible to "donate" to all BCH holders by burning BCH itself, but that's psychologically unappealing due to the limited supply of BCH. BADGER avoids that, is fun, generates more transactions & usage of CashTokens tech.In order to boost Badger TVL, what is needed is some kind of non-speculative demand to buy (& ideally burn) BADGER tokens. My idea for this is that community projects can show goodwill to the community by provably burning & I'm going to start with the Podcast. As an experiment, 2.7% (0.5 BCH) of the desired raised funds will be used to purchase Badger on Cauldron.quest and provably burn it. Readers are more than welcome to frontrun this by purchasing BADGER, which achieves somewhat the same effect! If this caught on as a community initiative by aligned projects, it would encourage more people to lock BCH, boosting the price and bringing more resources to the community to run even more projects (which can burn more BADGER). Raising BCH on-chain TVL is one of the few ways to defeat naked short selling, Tether inflation or other manipulation of the BCH price so I am very interested in new & potentially compelling ways to encourage this. Note to the community A huge thank you from me to The Bitcoin Cash Foundation, Casual BCH, General Protocol's Spaces, Paul, Fiendish & Friends and everyone else doing great work promoting BCH! It really truly is a team effort & I love having so much regular BCH content to consume beyond my own show.Let's keep building Bitcoin Cash. Proof of Work I have aggregated previous delivery & community work (including previous 2x FundMes + 5x Flipstarter delivery & accountability) on a dedicated page on the Bitcoin Cash Podcast website. See it here: bitcoincashpodcast.com/proof-of-work. Cost breakdown: 1.1 BCH topic research, episode hosting 1.1 BCH production & editing costs 0.3 BCH Thumbnail graphics & episode distribution 0.3 BCH Timestamps, links & episode summary 0.3 BCH Youtube Shorts creation & distribution 0.2 BCH Instagram & TikTok videos & management 3.3 BCH / episode x10 episodes = 33 BCH 1 BCH: BADGER burn. 5 BCH: Accounting costs. 5 BCH misc expenses: Ongoing costs such as website hosting & domain registration, Zoom subscription, supporting other community fundraisers, FAQ maintenance etc. 1 BCH: Fundme.cash fee Contributor rewards: Donations of > 0.5 BCH will receive a shoutout at the end of each show (like the Patrons and Sponsors) unless your name/message is rude or slanderous or you ask me not to or it's otherwise unreadable for some reason. Disclaimer I will be completing the deliverables whether or not the Flipstarter gets funded, the continuation of the show & my assistance to the community is in no way contingent on this funding request. However, as per previous Flipstarter, I believe this is a valuable community service & get a lot of feedback in that regard. If funding stops I will eventually have to work harder on alternative monetisation strategies (possibly advertising, sponsored episodes) though. Contact As always, for any questions, comments or suggestions I can be reached at jeremy@bitcoincashpodcast.com or on Telegram t.me/BitcoinCashPodcast.

title: bitcoin - Pacific Northwest
description: Support Bitcoin Cash Adoption in Spokane—Fund a Paper Wallet, Spark Real-World Use!Bitcoin Cash (BCH) is an incredible tool for fast, low-cost transactions, and we believe the best way to drive adoption is by putting BCH directly into the hands of local businesses. That’s why we’re launching a campaign to distribute funded Bitcoin Cash paper wallets to businesses in Spokane—giving them not just an introduction to BCH, but real motivation to start accepting it.Why Paper Wallets?A Bitcoin Cash paper wallet is a simple, tangible way for newcomers to experience cryptocurrency without needing technical knowledge. It contains a QR code with a preloaded BCH balance, making it easy for a business owner to scan and start using immediately. By receiving a funded wallet, they’re not just learning about BCH—they’re becoming invested in it.How Your Support HelpsYour donation directly funds these paper wallets, each loaded with a small amount of Bitcoin Cash. These wallets will be handed out to local businesses, coffee shops, and entrepreneurs, giving them a reason to explore BCH as a real payment option. Many businesses hesitate to adopt crypto because they’ve never used it—this campaign removes that hesitation by giving them a head start.The Ripple EffectWhen a business receives a funded Bitcoin Cash wallet, they’re naturally inclined to learn how to use it, accept it, and even spend it. This creates a growing network of BCH users in Spokane, strengthening its role as a local currency and making BCH more useful for everyone. Your contribution doesn’t just support adoption—it kickstarts a movement.Every dollar you donate goes directly toward loading wallets that will be placed in the hands of real businesses. Help us turn Spokane into a Bitcoin Cash-friendly city—one paper wallet at a time!Donate now and be a part of real BCH adoption in Spokane!Facebook group:https://www.facebook.com/groups/1637045700507484bitcoincashpnw@gmail.com

title: BitcoinCash Nigeria Q2 2025
description: 2024 was a transformative year for BitcoinCash adoption, particularly across the Global South. Bitcoincash Nigeria played a pivotal role, positioning BitcoinCash strategically within key ecosystems through numerous impactful initiatives. Our efforts focused on laying strong foundations, enhancing BCH representation within the crypto community, and pioneering strategies that attracted new users. As we step into 2025, we are committed to intensifying these efforts—not only in Nigeria but across the Global South. Adoption remains a marathon, and our goal is to solidify BitcoinCash as the premier global peer-to-peer electronic cash solution. We aim to raise 50 BCH (300$/Bch )through this Fund Me campaign to fund strategic activities planned for Q2 2025. With community support, we will accelerate our mission, driving transformative change and significantly broadening the reach of BitcoinCash. BitcoinCash Nigeria: Key Activities in 2024 Internships and Educational Initiatives: BitcoinCash Nigeria / Zapit Student Internship Program Details 1 | Details 2 Community Engagement and Webinars: BitcoinCash Virtual Meetup Details BCH HUB Partnership with Women Tech Makers (International Women's Day) Details BitcoinCash Webinar on CashTokens Details BCH4GOOD Humanitarian Outreach to Hospitals Details CashToken Meetup Details BitcoinCash Webinar: BCH BULL Details Strategic Workshops and Discussions: BCH HUB at Digital Ecosystem Country Assessment Workshop Details AMA Session Zapit - P2P Exchange (Nigerian Payment Options) Details X Space Session: BitcoinCash in Africa Details BitcoinPizza Day Celebration Details Free Roger Campaign Details 1 | Details 2 Community and Sports Initiatives: BitcoinCash Community Football League Details 1 | Details 2 Sponsoring WADAAC Community Engagement Details 1 | Details 2 X Space - BitcoinCash Nigeria & Zano Collaboration Details Tech Festivals and Outreach: BCH HUB at Arewa Tech Fest Details 1 | Details 2 BitcoinCash Giveaway (Bitcoin.com) – Over 5000 New Wallets Details 1 | Details 2 Hijacking Bitcoin Book Giveaway Details Policy and Governance: BCH HUB: Policy Dialogue on Enhancing Nigeria Digital Innovation Ecosystem Details BCH HUB at ISN Annual Gathering 2024 Details BCH HUB at E-governance Bill Stakeholder Engagement Adamawa Details Major Events and Milestones: BCH FESTIVAL Nigeria 2024 Details BCH HUB at ISN Annual Gathering 2024 Details 1 | Details 2 BitcoinCash ATM Launch at BCH HUB Partnerships: BCH HUB Partners with the Federal Ministry of Communications, Innovation and Digital Economy, Nigeria (Mentor Hub for National Girls in ICT Competition) BCH HUB Partners with 3MTT (Applied Learning Cluster) 1. BCH BUILD Hackathon Unleashing Peer-to-Peer Innovation in Nigeria (April 21 – May 31) Overview: The BCH BUILD Hackathon is designed to discover and nurture the next generation of innovators who will leverage Bitcoin Cash to solve real-world problems. Hackathons are critical in onboarding fresh talent, stimulating creative ideas, and accelerating the development of practical, impactful blockchain solutions that cater specifically to the needs of Nigeria and the wider Global South. Understanding the Initiative: The Bitcoin Cash Ecosystem is a vibrant community of builders. Over the years, we've witnessed remarkable platforms, innovative builds, and groundbreaking ideas emerge from this ecosystem. Hackathons serve as powerful catalysts, incentivizing and onboarding new talent into our vibrant community. Drawing inspiration from the groundbreaking success of the 2021 Coin Party Hackathon—which generated transformative ideas—we are now channeling our efforts towards the Global South. This hackathon aims to onboard emerging talent from Africa and beyond, empowering them to build the next generation of revolutionary Bitcoin Cash platforms and applications. Throughout the past year, we've engaged extensively with developer and techpreneur communities across Africa, recognizing their drive, creativity, and technical expertise. This event is not merely a competition; it is a movement to highlight the limitless potential of peer-to-peer electronic cash and foster a new era of digital innovation. Why Bitcoin Cash? With low fees, rapid transactions, and powerful smart contract capabilities, BCH is ideal for building solutions that advance financial inclusion, empower local businesses, and drive digital entertainment and education. Participation Incentives: $5,000 in BCH prizes for top projects $50 Participation Bursary & Exclusive Swag for all participants Get rewarded just for taking part. Mentorship from top developers in the ecosystem. Opportunities to network with Nigeria’s leading tech talent, designers, marketers, and business strategists. Key Themes: Financial Inclusion & Microfinance Local E-Commerce & Marketplaces Games & Entertainment Education & Skills Development Agricultural & Supply Chain Traceability Schedule: April 21–28: Team Formation & Ideation May 1: Opening Ceremony & Kickoff May 1–28: Project Development & Mentorship May 29: Project Submission Deadline May 31: Presentations & Awards Ceremony Special Awards: Grand Winner – BCH Nigeria All-Stars Prize Community Impact Award Best User Experience Award Innovation in Peer-to-Peer Payments Award Entertainment Excellence Award 2. BitcoinCash Trade Fair Seamless Commerce and Merchant Adoption (June 8) Overview: The BitcoinCash Trade Fair aims to directly tackle the hesitation merchants face in adopting cryptocurrency by showcasing real-world use cases of BCH in everyday commerce. Through interactive demonstrations, practical education, and engaging activities, we intend to create a strong case for businesses to embrace digital currencies, thus fostering a more inclusive, digitally empowered economy in Nigeria. Why This Event Matters: For too long, businesses have hesitated to adopt cryptocurrency. Through continuous engagement and education, Bitcoincash Nigeria has laid the groundwork for a more digitally inclusive economy. The BitcoinCash Trade Fair is a bold, innovative step to further incentivize merchant adoption, equip business owners with practical solutions, and ultimately transform the future of commerce in Nigeria. Highlights: Live Transactions: Real-time BCH commerce demonstrations Merchant Booths: Interactive displays and direct BCH transactions BCH Swap Booth: Instant fiat-to-BCH conversions Marketing & POS Solutions: Hands-on demonstrations of crypto POS solutions Interactive Games & Competitions: Engagement activities, substantial discounts, and prizes Expert Panels: Discussions on future trends in crypto and digital commerce This event is strategically designed to incentivize merchant adoption, resolve practical business concerns, and pave the way for widespread crypto integration in Nigeria. 3. Additional Community Engagement and Partnerships Overview: Community engagement and strategic partnerships form the backbone of sustainable adoption. Our participation in prominent events and strategic collaborations with influential organizations ensures sustained visibility for Bitcoin Cash, driving adoption at both grassroots and institutional levels. Bitcoincash Nigeria is deeply committed to driving BCH adoption and raising awareness throughout the Nigerian ecosystem. We actively participate in high-impact events and meetups while forging strategic partnerships with brands and community organizations. This multifaceted approach not only strengthens BCH representation in crucial spaces but also helps push adoption to every corner of the country. Over the coming months, we are dedicated to expanding our presence by engaging in a variety of activities that spotlight Bitcoin Cash’s transformative potential. Through these efforts, Bitcoincash Nigeria aims to build a stronger, more connected community, ensuring that BCH remains at the forefront of digital innovation and commerce in Nigeria. Upcoming Events (Q2 2025): Women Tech Makers Meetup – May Google I/O with GDG Community – May Arewa Tech Fest – May 20-22 Stellar West Africa Conference – May BitcoinPizza Day – May 22 ISN Annual Summit 2025 BCH Hangouts – April, May, June Open Source Africa – August 14-16 Through these strategic activities, we aim to build a more inclusive, empowered, and digitally advanced economy powered by BitcoinCash. Together, let's continue driving innovation, community empowerment, and widespread adoption of BitcoinCash!

title: Vox.cash - An integrated collection of small dapps
description: This is a fundraiser for a collection of small BCH apps in an integrated wallet. Collectively, the hope is, if a number of these "safe" ideas are integrated into a wallet and handed to a novice user, they might have more bitcoin (cash) the next time you meet up. These "apps" could live in a walled garden and eventually graduate out to other wallets. Having several small apps integrated into one can often be more than the sum of the parts. There's a lot of things that can be done by utilizing small functions together that can't be done with isolated apps.These are the mini apps: - Chat(s)- An index database- Subscriptions- Fungible Token Mining- Limit Order Dexes- Auctions- Time locks (re: Hodl & Last Will)- Yield(s) (FBCH & Badgers)- Irrevocable trusts (re:unspent.cash)- Integrated AccountingThe end product will be deployed in a unified platform with integrated web wallet. This app is going to be like a refresh of member or memo with CashTokens, but without the need for a custom indexer or specialized "backend" server. There's going to be less emphasis on the social media aspect and more focus on basic economic access and utility.All of the "mini-apps" listed below will be small on-chain contracts. Some ideas are completely new and some are updated versions of earlier projects. It may seem like a lot of work, but the smaller the contract the easier things are to build, audit, and debug. This work is expected take about a year to build out. Most of the contracts are near completion and through testing. There is a budget of about 20 BCH for audits. There are people in the world who want to take everyone's Bitcoin Cash to get rid of it as a currency. These people REALLY DO NOT IT LIKE when people save BCH―especially with defi contracts. There's been threats, harassment and ultimatums with this work, including by some people claiming to further the original bitcoin p2p project.Please consider utilizing the pseudonymous properties of Bitcoin Cash if making a donation to avoid a DM from those folks.For developers and bot programmers, most of these utilities will also ship as stateless libauth template "mixin" functions for any web wallet, meaning app developers can adopt a single component, or follow along with their idea on the same template and mainnet-js. The core functionality will also be an open software software library and command line utility, if someone wants to automate or integrate some component into their project or workflow.Who am I? I develop software under the username 2qx (github/gitlab). I was a founding developer on mainnet-js; mainnet was originally suppose to have a number of verbs to closely integrate with BCH defi in 2020. In December 2022, I raised 60 BCH to further develop unspent.app, which was about six mini-app contracts (without a wallet or tokens). In 2023, unspent was reduced into just perpetuities as unspent.cash. In 2024, I raised 60 BCH to build futurebitcoin.cash, a recursive time-locking market using fungible CashToken series. Contact info: I'm available on reddit as u/2q_x and telegram as @a2qx_username 0. We need to have a chat. Right now, the places where the original idea of bitcoin can be freely discussed are in a fairly precarious state. One of the main platforms has recently announced a ban on the use of any cryptocurrency (except their preferred currencies), and it's based in a jurisdiction that is hostile to free thought and free speech. Another (older) platform is a now a publicly traded company with a bot/troll problem. And being based in the United States, it too is now in a jurisdiction increasingly hostile to free speech and thought. And finally, there is the platform where bot engagement trolled a dull eccentric individual into bidding for the platform before he realized much of the engagement he was basing his decision on was driven by trolls and bots. It no longer seems like an app where many smart folks disseminate ideas. In short, we need a public group chat that's not vicariously controlled by a Russian actor, and where the ability to talk about and use the real bitcoin can never be rugged. So we're going to have a public group chat on-chain, utilizing NFT commitments as the storage medium. Messages will cost about 250 sats per byte, and old messages will get burned up to feed a future saving economy. With this chan, any message can be censored by anyone for 2500 sats per byte, and it will be possible to see which messages got burned early for those interested. Different channels for different topics will be supported. This app is a marketplace of ideas.1. A reliable key-value database.Similar to a group chat, just storing a little bit of data announcing the existence of a defi contract can be a significant pain point for many new small dapp projects. Currently, many dapps have built custom crawlers to index data stored on-chain using what are called "op_return" messages. Many apps are relying on public instances of chaingraph, which is software that is expensive and non-trivial to run. There are also performance and scaling issues that arise with these indexers which often hampers the user experience in dapps.So the second mini-app will be a highly distributed key-value database. Small index is a contract that will be able to store references and records to chat groups, auctions, trade offers and new long-running contracts. Data can be read from the highly reliable and scalable SPV index (fulcrum, electrumX or rostrum). Reading the records for a key will be as reliable as looking up the token balance of an address.Records will be limited to 40-bytes (or the current NFT commitment length) and cost 1 sat per block to store. Miners will collect storage fees to clean up old data. This app is a market for data storage.2. SubscriptionsRegardless of whether this particular flipstarter is successful, looking forward, it will be better for the ecosystem to have a more predictable funding model available. So there will be an ugly "subscribe" button in the app, that does nothing but hide the button for people that subscribed. The subscription contract will allow anyone to create subscriptions paid out in any fungible token. Users will be able to cancel or withdraw their subscription balance early with an NFT baton held in their wallet. The subscriptions will be announced using the key-value small database from the previous step. Miners (or any enterprising party), will be able to collect a few sats for executing the regular installments. Easily creating subscriptions for content creators, app developers, podcasters and mods will be available in the mini-app. The app should enable predictable baseline revenue for many voices and creators across the Bitcoin Cash ecosystem.There will be no tiers or rent-seeking features for this specific project. Although other people may build apps utilizing gated subscription features, there's too much to do and too little time to try to milk people over sats, so, for this app, the subscription will only ever just hide an ugly subscribe button.This app will create a market mechanism for future support.3. Token MiningNovice users want to mine. So we're going to let them mine fungible tokens that can then be traded for sats. Bitcoin Cash previously had a minable SLP token called Mistcoin, which was a project that saw immediate spontaneous adoption and a healthy secondary market. Additionally, an early iteration of Unspent Phi had a minable contract paying sats, which saw immediate uptake. So we're going to have a multi-threaded contract paying out tokens according to some half-life with an adjustable difficulty target stored in mutable NFT commitment―because we effing can. Eventually, if it gets big enough, the average of the difficulty targets across the mining threads can be utilized as a proxy for a highly decentralized price oracle. That price oracle can then be utilized by other contracts to drive more stable coin markets. This app is a market to trade energy for tokens.4. A Dex fit for a stablecoinUsers want stability. And the detractors of the idea of bitcoin often cite volatility (relative to fiat) as the first major risk to deter adoption among novice users. While automated market algorithms can provide liquidity across a very robust price range, we need markets to trade stable assets with a fair amount of depth at very specific prices as well. We need limit order markets. By storing limit order data in the commitment of an NFT, it will be possible for anyone to list tokens for sale on a contract, and then have the contract allow anyone to trade at the pre-defined order price. This app is similar to open ask limit orders on memo or tapswap, but it will allow partial orders and be bidirectional. Someone could fund a contract with sats to buy a token at a predefined price, and/or sell the same token at a higher price from using the same contract. Many users will be able to list their spreads for a particular token, and bots will be able to arbitrage across many contracts and dexes. Catdex has no commissions.This is a market for a stable coin. 5. Dutch auctions for token price discovery. If someone sells a bunch of tokens, often the only record or price history is the initial token sale. Without active and liquid markets, there is no data to dissuade the "investor" that they're actually just throwing their money away on collectibles. Without liquid markets, collectibles can absorb liquidity, attention and mind-share, often without much merit or underlying utility to give value back to the user. On other chains, the value of NFT sets can eventually be estimated by records of on-chain English auctions. But auctions could also be very efficiently held on Bitcoin Cash for any type of token by using the age of the unspent output in a Dutch auction. The auction app will allow anyone to announce a dutch auction, and fund their contract with any number of fungible tokens (or NFTs) to be disposed of on their behalf. Anyone will be able to clearly see the current price given the starting price and the age of the unspent output. Anyone will be able to purchase the tokens in a transaction paying the consignor the current ask. Bots and traders will be able to arbitrage or trade tokens at auction with the same token listed as a limit order (or AMM threads). Users may cancel their lots by buying back their own tokens. Bidders can be fairly assured that lots will sell to someone. And the process of price discovery can be a reference to speculators of collectibles. Auctions will have no listing commissions or buyers premiums―only fees paid to miners. This is a market to liquidate tokens.6 & 7, Absolute and relative time locks. Hodl-style and rolling time locks have been around for ten years. We've had these apps in the Electrom Cash as plugins. Users can lock coins until a certain time, or have their coins pay out to another party if not touched for some period of time. These ideas will be refreshed to handle locking CashTokens as well. Both of the contracts can be made to "auto-complete" via anyone-can-spend transactions, by announcing a record of the lock using the small key-store database (#1 above). These contracts market the idea of locking coins.8 & 9, Users want reliable yields.We have two dapps on Bitcoin Cash paying a solid yield without risking user capital on highly-manipulated markets. Users can stake coins for Badgers; or they can take coupons for staking Futures. In both cases, users aren't risking the loss of their capital to fiat manipulation or wild tokenomics. Both of these time-locked staking apps are perfect for a walled garden. They'll both have integrated support without any need to connect a wallet to an external integration.This are yield bearing markets to lock coins.10. Last irrevocable trust. An ultra-predictable stream of revenue is a wonderful thing. For almost three years now, the number of contracts streaming monthly payments to users has been growing, and they'll continue to grow with the fourth (more streamlined) version of the contract. Unspent.cash perpetuities will benefit greatly from direct wallet integration, as well as better historical tabulations now available from mainnet-js. The next iteration will accommodate fees and payouts running below the current dust limit and propagation fee threshold.On-chain irrevocable trusts are a market in time for users of Bitcoin Cash that know what they have and what it's worth.Bonus accounting.Blockchains are great at keeping a kind of accounting ledger. Given a standard set of contracts, it's fairly feasible to map out what happened to user funds in a clear and deterministic way without storing notes or account nicknames on the blockchain. There is a rich set of tooling for plain text accounting that can let users manage and plan their income and expenditures in a predictable way. If we give people tools to make, earn and save money, it then makes sense to let them plan how to spend it. When all the mini apps are done, it'd be nice to have some limited accounting and be able to export the data upstream.

title: Education for Argentina
description: Hello, BCH Community!My name is Lucas, I’m from Argentina, and a father of two.I run a YouTube channel called Lecturas Libertarias: 🔗 https://www.youtube.com/@LecturasLibertariasI’m a coherent libertarian (not a Milei follower), an anarchist, a defender of individual liberty, and a critic of political systems.On my channel, I teach the core foundations of coherent libertarian philosophy: 📚 Praxeology, natural law, agorism, and counter-economicsHere are some examples of my work:https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=zyRTIAnqnOA&t=134shttps://www.youtube.com/watch?v=v_U72kwEjhY&t=403shttps://www.youtube.com/watch?v=_Mk8NReGfx8&t=625sI also spoke out loudly during the $Libra situation, and I’ve made several videos explaining why BTC failed as a libertarian project:https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=iSQjYz9K1ls&t=2shttps://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Vtsvuv4x8DY (100K views)https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=0AikbMrZzkk&t=321shttps://www.youtube.com/watch?v=C0wiBv6r-Vw&t=1003sWhy this campaign?If you're familiar with Argentina, you know how bad things are: 🚫 Heavy taxation 💸 Constant inflation 🔒 Currency controls (a.k.a. “el cepo”) 🧾 Forced payment systems and financial repressionThese problems extend to all of Latin America.But after years of content creation, I’ve realized something: People don’t understand the root of the problem.They suffer the consequences… …but they don’t understand that the State, fiat money, monetary policy, and representative democracy are the actual causes.My goal is to educate people on the root problem, so they can later understand the real solution: BCH.The focus is simple: Problem → Reaction → SolutionThe BCH community has the tools to offer real solutions to real problems, especially for small merchants and entrepreneurs.So my goal is to dedicate myself full-time to this project and create high-quality content that:Teaches libertarian economic concepts in clear, simple termsIdentifies the everyday struggles of merchants (withheld funds, overtaxation, frozen accounts, unreliable services like PayPal or MercadoPago)Explains why these problems existAnd shows how BCH can solve themWhat I want to create:🎯 3 long-form YouTube videos per week (in Spanish and English) 🎯 4 short-form videos for Instagram and social media, also in both languages 🎯 A new dedicated Instagram account for this content 🎯 Printable guides and infographics (PDFs) in English and Spanish, to be shared via WhatsApp, Telegram, etc. 🎯 Custom content (graphics, videos, infographics) for recurring contributorsWhy am I asking for support?To cover operational costs and allow me to focus 100% on creating content to support BCH adoptionTo upgrade my hardware and buy a laptop to work more efficientlyI have no interest in fighting BTC communities. That’s a waste of energy. The average user already has deep-rooted misconceptions about crypto, and breaking through those biases using BTC is extremely difficult.Instead, I focus on education, real-world issues, and practical BCH solutions.With years of experience in YouTube strategy, content creation, graphic design, and voice-over, I know how to communicate clearly, keep viewers engaged, and make complex ideas easy to digest.By receiving monthly support in BCH, I’ll be able to fund the project, use BCH directly, and promote its adoption through action.I also plan to cover tools like Moria, BCH.BULL, and others that can help people and businesses use BCH today.I’m ready to get started right now.With your help, I can launch this content and make an impact from day one.Thank you for your time and support.Lucas📺 YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/@LecturasLibertarias 🐦 Twitter: https://x.com/LucasDiDodo1 💬 Telegram: t.me/LucasDidodo

title: CriptoCatia Podcast
description: What is the CriptoCatia Podcast about?Several months ago, I traveled to Argentina to attend the BCH Conference, after which I decided to stay in this country, since the political instability in Venezuela does not allow me to continue working actively on the Adoption of the BCH, and now I can tell you without putting my integrity at risk, that I and my team received several threats from some political groups of the governing party, since the UCV, being so politically important, anyone who carries out any activity at this University is branded as an opponent of the current government.My only political position is that in my country there can be opportunities and freedom for all Venezuelans and since I started talking about Bitcoin Cash my intention was to show people a type of free and safe money that could benefit them and give more empowerment to the masses who suffer from the banking system and current governments.Since I won't be in Venezuela for some time, I've decided to start a project called “CriptoCatia Podcast” from where I can continue contributing to promoting the use of BCH with a Podcast dedicated to talking about the Adoption of Bitcoin Cash in Venezuela and other Latin American countries.This Podcast will have a very different theme than what is normally seen in this type of projects, since our main script will be to show those cases of BCH Adoption that are carried out in each country, since when I was in Venezuela working on this, I could notice that nobody highlighted our work or gave it value, only the community and other people who, thanks to social media algorithms, could see our contribution.And that's not all, in this Podcast we will have direct contact with students and merchants who use BCH and thus we will learn about their experiences, there will be interviews with Adoption teams from different countries, we will also be able to do live surveys and learn about opinions on the new applications and projects that are coming out in this new year and also learn about their experiences with the applications that are already available.In this way, more Spanish-speaking people and people from Latin America will be able to learn about them and give them the use they deserve, since they are applications of the highest quality and level compared to those of other ecosystems, where their users suffer from their high transaction costs and some other flaws in their blockchains.We will be doing episodes where we will talk about specific topics about the crypto world and the entire battle that is currently taking place against the seizure of BTC and the arbitrary detention of Roger Ver, as well as other topics that may arise in the coming weeks and months.What will the funds we raise be used for?Starting a podcast on your own is extremely expensive, especially when you only want to offer the community the best, because they deserve it.I want to clarify for the community that the funds requested in this campaign will be used solely to purchase the necessary tools for the podcast. Production and editing costs will be part of your contribution to support this first phase. We want to start this project as soon as possible. These are the tools we need to get started:2 Microphones 2 Headphones 1 Audio interface 1 Camera 1 Laptop We will also need microphone stands and a stand for recording videos.We'll also cover hosting costs, Zoom subscriptions, tips for students, and merchants who submit their videos to us and pay with Bitcoin Cash.Why support this campaign?You should support this campaign because with this podcast, we can reach more people through social media. We'll use a formula that will have a huge impact: connecting with the audience and making them the protagonists of this podcast. We'll invite important people from the community, but for us, it's more important that the local person tells their neighbor how great it is to pay with BCH, fee-free and quickly.We already have a YouTube channel that's gaining followers daily without even publishing the first episode. https://youtube.com/@criptocatiapodcastWe also have a Telegram group to gather feedback from the public, and as I mentioned before, they will be the real stars of this Podcast.https://t.me/CriptoCatiaPodcastThe success of this campaign is very important to us in order to finally get this project moving. If you have any questions, I'm on social media as HamphernyR, where you can check out the progress of our work. Without further ado, I'll leave you with this gif from the CriptoCatia Podcast.https://x.com/HamphernyR/status/1904991253407637540?t=SreWuBkU_qfeTvfjctGauA&s=19Thank you for supporting us and long live electronic cash.

title: Selene Wallet 2025
description: Your Favorite Bitcoin Cash Wallet Selene Wallet is a self-custodial, open source, BCH-only mobile wallet that emphasizes a simple and accessible user experience without compromising on features that power users expect. We designed Selene Wallet with the goal of allowing anybody to go from zero-to-BCH-user as seamlessly as possible. Once a new BCH user is onboarded, the wallet can teach them what they need to know along the way! Only have 60 seconds to teach someone about Bitcoin Cash? Point them to Selene Wallet. The app downloads in seconds, and you can send them some BCH the moment they open the app for the first time. They’ll receive it instantly, and be amazed at the incredible speed and low barrier to entry! We aim to deliver a BCH experience that FEELS good, not just for seasoned BCH veterans, but also for people who are experiencing cryptocurrency for the first time. Our long-term vision for Selene Wallet is to provide the necessary tools that allow BCH to grow into the global reserve currency. First, 10,000,000 daily active users, then the whole world! Fundraiser Target: 424 BCH ($152,640 USD) Assumed BCH Price: $360 USD/BCH 2024 Retrospective and Review Overall, we're pleased with our results in 2024. Since releasing the Stats feature last July, there have been over 3600 unique devices that have opened Selene Wallet, with about 100 daily and 1000 monthly average users. We've done very little marketing or promotion overall - most of our users are gained by word of mouth. We see these stats as signs that we're on the right track, and we're excited for our next phase of growth. Let's review our 2024 deliverables: What did we deliver? Biometric/PIN authentication BIP70 Stats Coin Control CashTokens User manual (some) i18n improvements (but not the full scope of what we wanted) In addition, we accomplished some milestones that were not part of last year's Flipstarter targets: Availablity as a web wallet Critical performance, reliability, and scalability improvements UI improvements in response to user feedback Support for CashStamps/wallet sweep (in lieu of Shareable Link) What did we NOT deliver? We were unable to meet some of our promised deliverables within the 12 month timeframe: CashConnect/WalletConnect CashConnect and WalletConnect are the next priority items on our roadmap, now that we have support for CashTokens. We are aiming for WalletConnect support in time for BCH BLISS, with CashConnect support soon after. Dark Mode We are currently working with a design team to improve our color palette, which includes a Dark Mode palette. This will likely be finished and implemented within the next 30 days. Point of Sale (now "Vendor Mode") We've been focusing on making the user-focused wallet experience as complete as we can before pivoting to merchant-focused features. Meanwhile, Paytaca has competitively positioned themselves as the leader in BCH merchant adoption. We believe directly competing with their POS offering at this time is a redundant use of BCH community resources. We still have "Vendor Mode" on the roadmap, which is just a vendor-focused receive screen, and not a comprehensive POS implementation. Maps An excellent map implementation is a massive undertaking that requires tight coordination between many ecosystem participants. Veteran BCHers are well aware of "the Map problem." Entries go out of date, or are too highly localized, etc. Paytaca is able to solve the stale data problem via integration with their POS software, and they have the resources to support their local users. Our map integration would initially depend on Paytaca's. Overall, achieving our goals for a Map feature will take disproportionate time and effort compared to the anticipated benefits. However, we're still highly interested in growing the BCH Map ecosystem in the long term. Maps will remain a long-term roadmap target, but with no definitive timeframe for completion at this time. Shareable Link Somewhat obviated by our inclusion of CashStamps functionality. Truthfully, I (Kallisti) don't know how to do it without doing additional research; I've done enough research to know what I need to learn, but have prioritized other things instead of attempting an implementation. It's just a matter of spending the time on it, or paying someone to implement it for us. i18n improvements Our translation coverage has been improving over time, and we've made a few improvements like setting the default currency by locale. Ultimately, we want to set up a better platform for handling translator contributions and translation strings. We will soon be doing backend and infrastructure development for upcoming (unannounced) Selene products. We intend on exploring i18n platform options at that time. 2025 Roadmap and Deliverables For the next 12 months, we are targeting the following deliverables: CashConnect / WalletConnect Dark Mode Vendor Mode CashFusion Improved transaction history Improved stats dashboard Support for more fiat currencies To-Be-Announced Secret Project We are also opportunistically* targeting these deliverables: Additional in-app utilities, such as address management, message signing, currency conversion, and more Continued improvement of our user manual; in-app help text Improvements and expansion of our website Integrations with other BCH products, such as BCH Bull, Moria USD, AfoG, etc. Improved i18n/translations platform Shareable Link Maps *We say "opportunistically" because we don't want to over-promise and under-deliver, but also want to be transparent about our current goals and focus areas. Monetization Strategies We don't intend to survive on fundraisers indefinitely. As Selene Wallet becomes more mature and stable, we are able to build more products and services under the Selene brand. In the short term, we're considering an optional "Donation Mode" in the wallet that will allow you to send a microdonation to the Selene team and your Electrum server provider every N transactions. We anticipate many users would happily enable this mode to support BCH developers, and this can provide a revenue stream that scales with user growth. Some observation and user feedback suggests that we should consider a NFT sale. We have plans to develop a Selene-focused token ecosystem in the future, but the details have not yet been finalized. As our userbase grows, so does our brand. We have new products in mind that uses our brand as leverage, but we are not ready to publicly reveal more at this time. Interested contributors may contact the developers directly for more information. Some of our future plans require expansion of our server infrastructure, which on their own can also provide potential sources of revenue, such as file hosting and data services. Breakdown of Costs Baseline Operating Costs: $26,604 / year (74 BCH) At a baseline, we have a minimum annual expenditure of $26,604 (~74 BCH). We must pay these expenses in order to operate. Administrative: $21,697 / year Michigan LLC Registration: $250 / year Business Mailing Address & Phone: $348 / year Apple Developer License: $99 / year Building costs (Kallisti Home/Office): $21,000 / year Infrastructure: $4907 / year Server costs: $1591 / year Domain names: $254 / year bch.ninja, kallisti.cash, kallisti.io, selene.cash, selene.technology, selenewallet.com, xulu.tech Email (2 domains): $50 / year xulu.tech, kallisti.io Internet and Cellular: $1440 / year Energy Costs: $1572 / year ($131/mo average) Travel: 13 BCH ($4810) We must consider the expenditures involved with traveling to various conferences and events. Last year, we anticipated travel funding to come from Kallisti's discretionary income. Budgeting constraints toward the end of 2024 unfortunately prohibited attendance for the BCH Argentina conference. In order to continue attending key events, this year we will directly allocate funds for travel. We are confident that our attendance is delivering positive impact and visibility for the Selene and BCH brands. At the BTC conference in Nashville, Tennessee last year, Kallisti was able to give everyone on-the-ground insight into the conference, as well as interview with Fox News, giving an excellent promotion opportunity for Selene and BCH. We followed up with Steve Thurmond, who strategically teamed up with Kallisti in Nashville to make sure BCH had maximum impact at the conference. Likewise, this month Kallisti and Jeremy attended the BTC OP_NEXT conference in Tysons, Virginia, which brought immediate visibility to Selene and BCH from some of Bitcoin (BTC)'s most influential and respected community members. Our goal is to continue representing Bitcoin Cash positively by having a physical preference in Bitcoin and crypto spaces. BCH BLISS: $1934 Ticket: $150 Air Travel: ~$1200 (1 passenger, economy, round trip) Hotel: ~$584 (at venue, 4 nights) BTC Las Vegas: $1586 Ticket: Free Open Source Pass Air Travel: ~$200 (1 passenger, economy, round trip) Hotel: ~$1386 (at venue, 4 nights) OP_NEXT: $1290 Ticket: $250 Air Travel: ~$700 (1 passenger, economy, round trip) Hotel: $340 (3 nights) At the time of writing, there is not any other major travel anticipated. If there is another conference in Argentina, we'd like to consider attending, as Argentina has our 2nd-largest userbase by country. We will likely run an additional small fundraiser if this is the case. There are other US/Canada events, such as Ryan Giffin's Florida meetup, and Jerry's Toronto meetup that we would like to attend if able. Developer Compensation: 333.5 BCH ($120,060) Kallisti - full time development, user support, infrastructure management, business management, engineering management, product management: 194 BCH ($69,840) The median income for experienced software engineers in Kallisti's area is $101,342 Subtracting the Baseline Operating Costs and Travel Costs, $69,928 (~194 BCH) remains in discretionary income for Kallisti This is the opportunity cost of working full-time on a project I love, and is also the opportunity cost for the community to keep me from seeking a traditional development job in my city. Jeremy - part time development, user support, business management, marketing management, 'investor' relations, product management: 97 BCH ($34,920) We price Jeremy's contributions at roughly half the amount of Kallisti's, reflecting his capacity for involvement while also working on the Bitcoin Cash Podcast and BCH Bliss, which have their own funding outside of Selene. Jeremy provides critical support for Kallisti, sharing equal responsibility in business and product development, and having done most of the outreach for our additional development and funding contributors. This round of funding is intended to position Jeremy for full-time work exclusively in the BCH ecosystem. Budget for additional developers, paid on a per-ticket basis: 32.5 BCH ($11,700) We consider the cost it would take to pay one developer for 6 weeks of work, using the same rate as Kallisti. $101,342 / 52 weeks = $1948.88 / week; $11,693.28 for 6 weeks (~32.5 BCH) We already have developers on standby who have contributed excellent work. We would like to continue working with them. One-time commission for CashFusion development: 10 BCH ($3600) We have a developer on standby who has the qualifying expertise to properly implement CashFusion. We have negotiated a 10 BCH payment for completion of the CashFusion feature. Infrastructure Expansion: ~$1300 / year (~3.5 BCH) Additional high speed block storage for Chaingraph: ~$1200/year (~3.333 BCH) (1TB @ ~$100/TB/month) Additional object storage for IPFS and other services: ~$100/year (~0.3 BCH) (1TB @ ~$9/TB/month) Kallisti has been hosting critical and reliable BCH infrastructure since 2021 at bch.ninja. Electrum provider for Selene Wallet, Electron Cash, Stack Wallet, Mainnet-JS (Cashonize, Tapswap, et al), etc. One of three chipnet miners Performant, high-availability Mainnet and Chipnet blockchain explorers (sickpig explorers) High-reliability p2p peer for other BCH nodes (Fast) Storage is biggest resource need We want to expand to provide Chaingraph, IPFS, and other services Sale of digital services and infrastructure access is a potential revenue generator Progress Tracking and Feedback Our GitLab milestone and issue trackers can be used to follow our progress: Development board: https://git.xulu.tech/selene.cash/selene-wallet/-/boards/2 Feedback board: https://git.xulu.tech/selene.cash/selene-wallet/-/boards/4 Issue tracker: https://git.xulu.tech/selene.cash/selene-wallet/-/issues/ Milestone tracker: https://git.xulu.tech/selene.cash/selene-wallet/-/milestones Updates will always be published to our Twitter: https://twitter.com/SeleneWallet If you’d like to share your thoughts about Selene Wallet with us, send an email to feedback@selene.cash or talk to us on Telegram at https://t.me/SeleneWallet! Try Selene Wallet Today! Website: https://selene.cash Web Wallet: https://app.selene.cash Google Play Apple App Store Direct Android APK Download Special Thanks We'd like to extend our appreciation and gratitude to the BCH community for supporting us and enjoying Selene Wallet. In addition, we'd specifically like to thank a few people for their contributions in 2024: Jim Hamill Mateus Dal Bianco Josh (@MrJPE) Gustavo _minisatoshi Jason Dreyzehner Calin Culianu Mathieu Geukens The Paytaca Team General Protocols Steve Thurmond Jett Scythe

title: Bitcoin Out Loud Video: The CHIP Process
description: My name is John Moriarty. I run the Bitcoin Out Loud YouTube channel, and I’ve done various jobs in the BCH space, including content production, copy writing/editing, and project management.I’m running this fundraiser to fund the production of an explainer video about Bitcoin Cash’s CHIP Process.Here’s the cost breakdown:-Final Storyboarding, Design, and Animation: $1500-Script writing, voiceover, production, management: $2500-Fundme.cash listing fee: 0.01 BCH-Fundme.cash 1.5% fee$1500+$2500 = $4000 //subtotal$4000 * 100/98.5 ~= $4060.91 //1.5% fundme.cash fee$4060.91/$354.43 BCH ~= 11.4575797 BCH // convert to BCH11.4575797 BCH + 0.01 BCH = 11.5575797 BCH //0.01 BCH listing feeHere’s the script:Bitcoin was designed to be a decentralized digital currency, a payment network where there’s no one person or group of people in charge.Being decentralized gives Bitcoin various advantages over centralized systems.But decentralization also makes coordination much more difficult. Without a CEO or committee to direct it, how can the network reach consensus on any decisions that need to be made? For example, how could the Bitcoin network decide whether or not to implement a proposed update?*INTRO*The history of Bitcoin and later Bitcoin Cash is a prime example of how difficult reaching decentralized consensus can be. The so-called Scaling Debate dramatically split the Bitcoin blockchain in two, even when a plan for scaling Bitcoin existed from the beginning.While agreeing on the need to increase the blocksize limit was a good starting point, that didn’t solve decentralized consensus, and Bitcoin Cash would undergo 2 more chain splits, one in 2018 and one in 2020.These splits hurt confidence in BCH’s long term viability, and shrunk the community. But important lessons were learned in the process.Maybe the most important lesson was that, without some sort of established process, the result isn’t just chaos; it’s much worse. Bad actors can end up in influential positions and then stall good proposals indefinitely or rush bad proposals through, leading to contentious chain splits.So what is the CHIP process?Short for Cash Improvement Proposal, the CHIP process is a framework designed to help decentralized communities like Bitcoin Cash reach consensus on a proposed change while avoiding chain splits and other damaging outcomes.Since its introduction, every successful Bitcoin Cash protocol upgrade has been proposed as a CHIP.But it’s important to note that the CHIP process is not the “official” consensus process for Bitcoin Cash. Nothing can be official for a truly decentralized network. It’s just one method that has had success so far.The CHIP process centers around a public, living document maintained by an explicit owner (or owners). That document is called… a CHIP.A CHIP includes much more than a technical specification, although that’s there too. In total there are 12 sections.The Motivation and Benefits section describes how the upgrade or change will practically benefit stakeholders. What need is there that this change fulfills, or what problem needs addressing?The Technical Description and Specification sections will have things like protocol formats, parameters, and workflows.The Costs and Risks sections recognize how any stakeholders could be negatively impacted by the proposed change. Who exactly will need to react to the change and what are the associated costs in the long term?The Current Implementations and Tests Cases sections cover where the proposal is already implemented, and the evidence that the change works as expected.The Evaluation of Alternatives section is where other possible solutions should be listed and addressed. What about options that are more powerful but less tested, or solutions that are less powerful but more widely understood? What about doing nothing? This section is pivotal because it provides a starting point for discussion around sources of conflict.The Security Considerations section should address risks specifically related to security. Does the proposal touch consensus-critical code? Could it create a vulnerability to denial of service attacks?Finally, the List of Major Stakeholders section broadly recognizes which groups of network participants are stakeholders for the proposed change while the Statements section shares the feedback gathered from specific stakeholders.By requiring public documentation of stakeholder positions, the CHIP process acknowledges conflict as a necessary part of reaching robust consensus. The goal is to air out disagreements in a structured way, so that they can be examined and resolved, rather than letting them fester behind closed doors.Lack of clear schedules can lead to prolonged conflicts, so there is a suggested schedule covered in the CHIP specification. In most cases, the status of the CHIP document should make it overwhelmingly clear whether it has reached consensus at least 6 months before the date of the proposed change.A CHIP that hasn’t gained enough support by that time can choose to update their timeline and continue campaigning for a later upgrade. A CHIP that has gained enough support will already have all major stakeholders onboard and prepared to implement the change.The CHIP Process is not without limitations. Because a CHIP is largely defined by the CHIP owner, it's crucial for stakeholders to critically evaluate the quality of each CHIP. Is the stakeholder list comprehensive? Are the risks and alternatives adequately addressed? The strength of the process relies on the informed judgment of its participants.It’s also worth pointing out that the CHIP process isn’t a decision making process. There is no vote, and no one can be forced to run or not run software. Instead, the CHIP process is a consensus building process. It’s a practical template built to make reaching consensus easier and less costly.Where the CHIP process excels is in providing a clear framework—a path to navigate the challenges of decentralized coordination, with stability as a core goal.

title: La Economía P2P: Episodes 145–174
description: In this campaign, our goal is to continue creating quality content for La Economía P2P. We publish weekly episodes where we explore real opportunities in a peer-to-peer economy based on Bitcoin Cash, focusing on the technical aspects of the technology along with its philosophy and the vision of Satoshi Nakamoto.Thanks to the generous support of the community, we no longer need to acquire equipment. We have a solid technical infrastructure.Today, the main challenge is to sustain and scale the project:Sustain weekly production, which requires time, coordination, research, editing, and ongoing distribution.Scale to new audiences, taking advantage of the fact that YouTube now dubs our episodes into English, opening us to a global audience.Increase the number of videos on the channel: summaries of all episodes in both English and Spanish, potentially expanding to other languages.📚 What does this campaign fund?With your support, we will cover the following key expenses:ProductionProfessional video and audio editing, including weekly work with freelancers.Coordination, research, scriptwriting, and graphic design costs for each episode.DistributionContent distribution on platforms like YouTube, Telegram, Podcast, and social media.Creation of clips and subtitles to share more accessible and viral content.Hosting, maintenance, and improvements to the website.Community and reciprocityRewards for donors and members of the private community.A small fund of 0.5 BCH to support other BCH community projects as a sign of reciprocity.🌍 International ReachEnglish dubbing of all episodesThanks to YouTube’s new dubbing tool, our episodes are now being heard in English without needing to produce separate versions. This feature allows us to expand international outreach, making our channel a meeting point for spreading the idea of peer-to-peer electronic cash.Episode summariesWe know many want to learn about P2P electronic cash and its advantages, but busy schedules often make it difficult to follow full episodes. That’s why we’ll offer interactive summaries that present the key concepts in a concise and accessible way.We will produce these summaries for each podcast episode, which will increase user retention and expand our reach to new people interested in the technology.⚠️ What if it’s not funded?Each episode requires multiple hours of work: reading, writing, coordination, recording, editing, and publishing.If we don’t reach the goal, we will reduce the frequency to one every 2 or 3 weeks in order to keep the project sustainable alongside our job responsibilities.Your contribution makes the difference between a consistent, active, and professional channel, or one that is more sporadic and limited.🎁 Benefits for donorsWe appreciate every contribution.The top 3 donors will receive, after contact, Level 5 access to La Economía P2P’s private community, with exclusive content, participation in meetups, and more.______En esta campaña, nuestro objetivo es seguir creando contenido de calidad para La Economía P2P. Publicamos episodios de forma semanal, donde exploramos las oportunidades concretas de una economía entre pares basada en Bitcoin Cash y hacemos foco en las características técnicas de la tecnología, junto con su filosofía y la visión de Satoshi Nakamoto.Gracias al generoso apoyo de la comunidad, ya no necesitamos adquirir equipos. Contamos con una infraestructura técnica sólida.Hoy, el desafío principal es sostener y escalar el proyecto:Sostener la producción semanal que requiere tiempo, coordinación, investigación, edición y distribución constante.Escalar a nuevas audiencias, aprovechando que YouTube ahora dobla nuestros episodios al inglés, abriéndonos a un público global.Incrementar la cantidad de videos en el canal: resúmenes todos los episodios en inglés y español, potencialmente expandiendo a otros idiomas.📚 ¿Qué financia esta campaña?Con tu apoyo, vamos a cubrir los siguientes gastos clave:ProducciónEdición profesional de video y audio, incluyendo trabajo semanal con freelancers.Costos de coordinación, investigación, guión y diseño gráfico de cada episodio.DistribuciónDistribución de contenido a plataformas como YouTube, Telegram, Podcast y redes sociales.Creación de clips y subtítulos para difundir contenido más accesible y viral.Hosting, mantenimiento y mejoras del sitio web.Comunidad y reciprocidadRecompensas para donantes y miembros de la comunidad privada.Pequeño fondo de 0.5 BCH para apoyar otros proyectos comunitarios BCH como señal de reciprocidad.🌍 Alcance internacionalDoblaje al inglés de todos los episodiosGracias a la nueva herramienta de doblaje de YouTube, nuestros episodios comienzan a escucharse en inglés sin necesidad de producir versiones separadas. Esta función nos permite ampliar la difusión internacional, lo que convierte nuestro canal en un punto de encuentro para difundir la idea del efectivo electrónico peer-to-peer.Resúmenes de los episodiosSabemos que muchos desean conocer el efectivo electrónico P2P y sus ventajas, pero a menudo las agendas ocupadas dificultan que sigan episodios completos. Por eso vamos a ofrecer resúmenes interactivos que presentan los conceptos clave de forma concisa y accesible.Vamos a producir estos resúmenes de cada episodio del pódcast, lo que incrementará la retención de los usuarios y expandirá el alcance a nuevas personas interesadas en la tecnología.⚠️ ¿Qué pasa si no se financia?Cada episodio requiere múltiples horas de trabajo: lectura, escritura, coordinación, grabación, edición y publicación.Si no alcanzamos el objetivo, vamos a reducir la frecuencia a uno cada 2 o 3 semanas, para poder sostener el proyecto de forma compatible con nuestras responsabilidades laborales.Tu aporte hace la diferencia entre un canal constante, activo y profesional, o uno más esporádico y limitado.🎁 Beneficios para donantesAgradecemos cada contribución.Los 3 donantes más importantes recibirán, previo contacto, acceso de Nivel 5 a la comunidad privada de La Economía P2P, con contenido exclusivo, participación en encuentros y más.____Join us in this new phase. Help us continue producing free, educational, and decentralized content.📺 YouTube Channel: @LaEconomiaP2P🌐 Website: https://laeconomiap2p.com✉️ Telegram: https://t.me/LaEconomiaP2P🐦 X: https://twitter.com/LaEconomiaP2P
