title: Bitcoin Cash Nigeria at Open Source Festival 2025!
description: 🚀 Support Bitcoin Cash Nigeria at Open Source Festival 2025!Theme: Beyond Borders | Date: August 14–16, 2025 📍 Lagos, Nigeria🌍 Why This Event MattersOpen Source Festival is Africa’s largest celebration of open-source technology, collaboration, and community. It brings together thousands of developers, designers, students, and organizations who believe in the power of open systems.With the 2025 theme “Beyond Borders,” this year’s event focuses on cross-community innovation, global contribution, and building across tech ecosystems — and that’s exactly why Bitcoin Cash Nigeria must be there.Bitcoin Cash is built on open-source principles — permissionless, decentralized, and designed for everyday utility. This event gives us the perfect platform to onboard developers into the Bitcoin Cash ecosystem, showcase open-source tools, and connect with key stakeholders.💡 Our Goals This YearFor OSCAF 2025, we’re taking our presence to the next level with:🛠️ A dedicated Bitcoin Cash Smart Contract Workshop🎁 Onboarding new users with wallets, CashTokens, and BCH giveaways🧠 Spreading awareness on Bitcoin Cash as peer-to-peer money and open infrastructure🛍️ Sharing free BCH-branded swag, stickers, and learning kits🤝 Networking with developers, designers, and communities to explore integration opportunities🛠️ Bitcoin Cash Workshop: CashScript ArenaWe’re hosting a hands-on CashScript Arena Workshop to introduce open-source developers to smart contract development on Bitcoin Cash.Smart contracts can feel complex—especially for beginners—so we’re flipping the script: learn by hacking. Using the CashScript Arena, participants will break intentionally vulnerable contracts on the BCH mainnet to claim bounties and gain firsthand experience of how BCH smart contracts work.This session is perfect for curious developers, Web3 builders, and open-source contributors new to BCH. To support their journey, we’ll include beginner-friendly tutorials from Bitcoin Cash University and MrZwets’ YouTube channel, making the workshop accessible to all skill levels.Complete beginners are welcome — by the end of this session, participants will have practical knowledge, useful tools, and a launchpad to start building real-world apps with CashScript.📜 Our Legacy at Open Source FestivalBitcoin Cash Nigeria has been part of Open Source Festival since 2020. We've grown from participants to community contributors:2020Our first OSCAF experienceReflections on open-source inspiration2022Shared insights at the eventPanel contributions and BCH onboarding2023Expanded BCH awareness and partnershipsHands-on session with 50+ participants onboardedIn 2025, we want to make our biggest impact yet — and we need your support to make that happen.💸 Budget Breakdown — Target: 5 BCH (~$2,500)ItemDescriptionEstimate✈️ Travel & AccommodationFlights + local transport + hotel stay for team$1,000🛠️ Workshop & Learning CashScript Arena setup, refreshments, materials $1,000🎁 Swag & Onboarding PacksStickers, shirts, paper wallets, Cashstamps giveaways, $500🙌 How You Can HelpSupport our campaign by donating to help cover logistics, resources, and outreach materials. Every satoshi helps us empower the next wave of Bitcoin Cash builders in Africa.All these efforts are part of a bigger vision that we’ll be announcing soon to the community. We’re building a global model to onboard a new generation of builders into the Bitcoin Cash ecosystem. Let’s go beyond borders — together.

title: BCH Content Hub
description: 📢 BCH Hub – The Bitcoin Cash Content HubIntroductionBCH Hub is designed to solve a core problem in our community: quality content about Bitcoin Cash is scattered across dozens of platforms, making it difficult to find, follow, and share.BCH Hub provides a accessible space where the best videos, articles, podcasts, and tutorials are curated, organized, and easy to access.This hub saves time, reduces noise, and ensures the community always has a reliable place to stay informed and inspired.🌍 Why Bitcoin Cash?Low fees: send transactions for fractions of a cent.Speed: near-instant confirmations.Global and borderless: available to anyone with a phone.Financial sovereignty: you control your keys, with no intermediaries.BCH is the perfect tool for daily payments, but it needs visibility, education, and trust. That’s what BCH Hub provides.🚀 Our Solution: BCH HubA curated aggregator: we collect and organize the best BCH articles, podcasts, and videos.Accessible education: clear guides and tutorials for beginners and advanced users.Real-time updates: fresh content delivered through API integrations and trusted sources.Smart indexing: content categorized so you can quickly find what you need.Instead of noise and fragmentation, a single reliable hub for all things Bitcoin Cash.🎯 Expected ImpactWith your support, we can:Launch the first public web platform with active BCH content curation.Make the platform and educational materials accessible in multiple languages.Integrate login with Wallet Connect using BCH walletsMaintain a content curation process and user support through the platform form, and expand into an audiovisual repository with conferences, debates, and tutorials.Deploy the platform on a secure server and configure both backend and frontendEvery contribution helps give Bitcoin Cash more global visibility.🏆 Donor Rewards$5 – Public thank you on the BCH Hub website.$20 – Early access to premium features (exclusive newsletter, advanced filters).$50 – Exclusive digital merch (wallpapers, BCH Hub banners).$90 – Indexing of your channel and content on the site$300+ – Recognition as a “Founding Sponsor” on the homepage.🌱 RoadmapWeb development – finalize the platform and integrations.Initial text curation – index the first 100 articles and 50 key videos.Community marketing – promote BCH Hub across Telegram, Reddit, and Twitter/X.Educational expansion – add content in English, Spanish, and other languages.🤝 Join the MovementBCH Hub is not just a project — it’s a tool for the entire community. It continues the cultural effort to spread the ideas of freedom and true Bitcoin: peer-to-peer money.With your help, we can make sure Bitcoin Cash has a trusted, accessible, global information hub.📌 Every donation brings more people closer to the vision of peer-to-peer electronic cash.👉 Contribute today on fundme.cash and be part of the future of BCH.🔗 Website: bchhubmini.vercel.app (test site)💚 Donate: fundme.cash💬 Contact: email cryptomindersyt@gmail.com | Telegram t.me/+OvqF4keZcTYwMmNh🐦 Twitter/X: @cryptomindersyt

title: BitcoinCashOG: BCH Marketing & Community Growth
description: BitcoinCashOG: BCH Marketing & Community Growth Funding requested 9 BCH (~ $600 / BCH) Deliverables: Note: December was corrected to November 3 Months work (September-November 2025) Bitcoin Cash Marketing via BitcoinCashOG on x.com 3 months X Premium Plus subscription: $140 78 hours ($70 p/h) September-December 2025 1 hour per day Monday-Saturday Hello, Bitcoin Cash community! Fiendish Crypto here, your dedicated BCH enthusiast known in the space for my passion in promoting peer-to-peer digital cash. You may have seen me: Host BCH 2022 in St Kitts & Nevis, Present at BCH Bliss 2024 with Making The Impossible A Reality Present at BCH Bliss 2025 with Breaking into the Mainstream Run podcasts The BCH Hangout and Fiendish & Friends Currently working with @BitcoinCashOG on X.com to serve as: A central positive hub for BCH engagement, education, and outreach. Amplification of BCH's voice, share high-signal content, attract new users by focusing on adoption stories, tech updates, and community vibes. Why This Campaign? Bitcoin Cash is the best money that the world has ever created and has some of the best developers and engineers in the space. There’s just one, tiny, but important challenge to overcome! Getting people educated about this amazing technology and then using it! Despite having a market cap of over 10 billion dollars, Bitcoin Cash has a total marketing budget of a rounding error of a rounding error … marketing and outreach can be improved tremendously! This is a big challenge, and the running and growing of BitcoinCashOG on X.com is an important first step to tackle this. Social media is a great way to reach millions or even billions of people , and Bitcoin Cash can benefit from a prominent, easily discoverable presence on X.com, just like other decentralised cryptos with large, highly followed accounts. When large accounts, such as news outlets or crypto exchanges, tweet about Monero, Dash, Bitcoin, Litecoin etc, they often use @links to the largest handles representing those coins. This allows X users to simply click the account hyperlink and hit follow on that account to stay in the loop with the latest, greatest news from that coin and its community. Bitcoin Cash is currently the odd one out. BCH never has any link to any account. People are lazy, they likely won’t even hit the search bar. Even if they do, they will not find any large BCH account to follow, and therefore probably wont follow anyone in the space nor get any news on their feed. Worse still, they may likely conclude that BCH is a dead project with no people in it! We can solve this and supercharge Bitcoin Cash’s reach to ensure that whenever Bitcoin Cash is mentioned in a social media post, it links to a: Well curated Good vibes only PG 13 Non-political Non-exclusionary Beacon of pure BCH goodness aka, @BitcoinCashOG! This project aims to change the perception of BCH to be a lively, thriving ecosystem with boundless opportunity. The strategy relies on community buy in and support! Renaming the account is reliant on enough community buy in proof, which will take time and some strategic manoeuvres. Goals and Vision: Grow the Account Milestone 1: 100 followers - end of week 2 (Completed 10th August 2025 / 11 days ahead of schedule 🎯) Milestone 2: 1000 followers - November 2025 Milestone 3: 5000 Followers Rename account to “Bitcoin Cash” Milestone 4: 10,000 followers - February 2026 Milestone 5: 50,000 followers - August 2026 Milestone 6: 100,000 followers - January 2027 Content Strategy: 3-4 posts/day, themes include: Merchant Monday:(adoption focus), Mid-Week Geek Out:(education), Feel-Good Friday:(engagement), and neutral, informative updates. All content is positive, fact-based, and community-driven. These themes will morph and adapt depending on feedback and growth based on data analysis: Expand Outreach : Increase BCH Spaces and Podcasts to spark discussions, collaborations, and cross-community engagement. As we grow, allocate time and budget for attending crypto conferences to represent BCH and boosting educational posts on X for wider visibility. Community Benefits : Provide a clean, positive, high signal channel where anyone can learn about BCH's potential as everyday digital cash. Amplify user stories, dev projects, and real-world uses to inspire more adoption. Account Guidelines : The account has clear guidelines to ensure that it remains an overwhelmingly positive force for the Bitcoin Cash Community and network. BitcoinCashOG Guidelines Posts from BitcoinCashOG are designed for maximum positive impact for the network and community, that hold the community and the network’s best interests at heart. To achieve this there are some guidelines to help keep posts, content, and frequency on point. 3-4 posts per day Replies to other posts are not limited in number Posts spread out where possible to cover 24 hours No profanity No pornographic posts No overly political posts Although Bitcoin Cash is inherently political so will be a balance No talking of price No opinions on protocol changes Posting of proposed changes to keep people informed is ok, but posts should be handled neutrally. No aggressive language or attacking / antagonistic behaviour. Will use humble humour and facts as a retort. No supporting any flipstarter/fundme. Reporting of successful campaigns is ok Ideas on general themes on days (at least one post will match the theme on that day) Thank you so much for reading and for any and all donations! Have some questions? Reach out on @BitcoinCashOG on X.com or via the BitcoinCashOG telegram group and we’ll be happy all questions and get you as excited as us! Let’s do this 💪

title: Freest Money for the Freest People of Liberland
description: From September 4–6, 2025, I’ll be at Liberpulco in Liberland anarchapulco.com, a gathering dedicated to freedom, community, and innovation.My mission: to onboard as many people as possible to Bitcoin Cash (BCH) by distributing $10–15 / BCH 0.02 coupons created on stamps.cash. These coupons give newcomers their first hands-on experience with peer-to-peer electronic cash — spendable right away.Why this matters:Liberland is positioning itself as the world’s freest nation.Last November, I personally completed the 3D aerial mapping of all 7 km² of Liberland, giving me a close connection to its land and people.I have strong relations with President Vít Jedlička, and I want to help make Liberland the first true Bitcoin Cash country.Fun fact: Justin Sun, Tron (TRX) founder was recently made Minister of Finance in Liberland. Wouldn’t it be poetic justice to spread BCH adoption while he sits on his greedy bags of coins?About me:I’m David K, an ETH Zürich–educated architect and founder of Archventure LLC, a company specializing in Drone Inspection, 3D Surveying, 3D Printing, and DJI Enterprise solutions. My work blends technology and architecture — building bridges between the physical and digital worlds.I’m funding the first batch of coupons with the help of my swiss BCH-mentor-friend and myself. We are paying for 20 Coupons ourselves. With your support, we can print and distribute many more BCH coupons, onboarding hundreds of new users to the world’s freest money.Let’s give the freest money to the freest people — and put BCH at the heart of Liberland’s future.

title: Blockchain Tech Workshops
description: Introduction The Philippines is one of the fastest-growing markets for digital payments, but blockchain education is still limited to theory. Few students get the chance to work hands-on with real blockchain tools.Bitcoin Cash offers something unique: fast, low-cost, permissionless transactions and programmable money. By teaching CashTokens and CashScript, we are giving young developers the skills to create practical blockchain applications for payments, commerce, and beyond.This isn’t just about training—it’s about building capacity in communities where blockchain adoption can make the biggest difference. Workshop Content Each workshop runs for 2 full days and is designed to combine fundamentals, hands-on practice, and collaborative building. By the end of the workshop, participants will not just understand blockchain concepts—they will have built working prototypes that showcase Bitcoin Cash utility. Day 1: Foundations & Smart Contracts Morning Session: Introduction Blockchain Fundamentals: Understanding distributed ledgers and consensus Cryptography Basics: Public/private keys, digital signatures, and hashing Bitcoin Cash: History, features, and advantages UTXO Model: Understanding unspent transaction outputs Smart Contracts: Introduction to programmable money Afternoon Session: Cashscript Development Cashscript Language: Syntax, operators, and data types Contract Structure: Input/output validation and conditions Practical Examples: Building basic smart contracts Testing & Deployment: Tools and best practices Day 2: Framework Integration & Group Projects Morning Session: Quasar Framework Quasar Overview: Introduction to the framework Cashscript Integration: Using smart contracts in Quasar applications Frontend Development: Building user interfaces for smart contracts State Management: Handling contract interactions and wallet connections Afternoon Session: Group Projects Team Formation: Students self-organize into groups Project Selection: Choose from provided application templates or propose custom ideas Development Time: Hands-on building with instructor support Presentations: Groups showcase their applications All workshop content is published in a public repository: 🔗 github.com/paytaca/cashscript-workshop This ensures that the materials are not only used in our 12 schools but can also serve as a reference and toolkit for organizers worldwide. Anyone who wants to conduct similar workshops can freely access, adapt, and build upon these resources—expanding the reach and impact of Bitcoin Cash education far beyond the Philippines. Who We Are The workshops are organized by Paytaca. We are a Philippine-based Bitcoin Cash wallet and ecosystem builder, together with SciBiz Informatics, a blockchain and AI startup studio. Paytaca has onboarded hundreds of merchants, rolled out point-of-sale systems, and sponsored tech community events across the Visayas. SciBiz Informatics builds solutions ranging from payment hubs to tokenized platforms. With our track record and grassroots presence, we are well-positioned to deliver credible and impactful blockchain education. Meet the Trainers Our workshops are designed and conducted by the same developers who have been building real Bitcoin Cash applications. They’re not just teaching theory—they’re sharing battle-tested experience from projects already in use today. 👨‍💻 Joemar TagannaCEO & CTO of Paytaca, CTO of PurelyPeer, founder of SciBiz Informatics 👨‍💻 Jeff Khirvy Kyell SarmenDeveloper behind Paytaca Marketplace, StableHedge, and the AnyHedge integration in Paytaca. 👨‍💻 Ronaldo RamanoCreator of CashTokens Studio and developer of Multisig Wallet support in Paytaca. 👨‍💻 Jethro AlbanoEngineer of the backend and smart contracts powering PurelyPeer, a BCH-powered geolocation game. 👨‍💻 Edcel Marie BaliteDeveloper of the Paytaca P2P Exchange, bringing peer-to-peer crypto trading to grassroots communities. Participation Over the past three weeks, we have personally reached out to colleges and universities in Tacloban, Ormoc, and Cebu. The response has been overwhelming—12 institutions have already confirmed their participation in our Blockchain Tech Workshops. This means the program is fully greenlit; all that remains is execution. Tacloban & Ormoc Leg Eastern Visayas State University (EVSU Tacloban) – Sept. 9–10, 2025 (Tue–Wed) Western Leyte College (WLC Ormoc) – Sept. 16–17, 2025 (Tue–Wed) STI Ormoc – Sept. 18–19, 2025 (Thu–Fri) ACLC Tacloban – Sept. 22–23, 2025 (Mon–Tue) EVSU Ormoc – Sept. 25–26, 2025 (Thu–Fri) ACLC Ormoc – Sept. 29–30, 2025 (Mon–Tue) University of the Philippines (UP Tacloban) – Oct. 1, 2025 (Wed) Cebu Leg Asian College of Technology (ACT) – Oct. 2–3, 2025 (Thu–Fri) University of Cebu (UC Main) – Oct. 7–8, 2025 (Tue–Wed) University of San Jose-Recoletos (USJR) – Oct. 9–10, 2025 (Thu–Fri) University of the Visayas (UV) – Oct. 17–18, 2025 (Fri–Sat) University of Southern Philippines Foundation (USPF) – Oct. 23–24, 2025 (Thu–Fri) University of Cebu (UC Banilad) – Participating, schedule to be finalized University of Cebu (UC Lapu-Lapu & Mandaue) – Participating, schedule to be finalized Budget & Use of Funds Our trainer team, along with our marketing team, will be on the ground full-time, conducting two-day workshops in each school. To make this possible, we are seeking $11,000, allocated as follows: 1. Travel & Accommodation – $4,200 Transport for trainers between schools and cities Two months’ accommodation in Ormoc and Cebu for the trainer team 2. Training Materials – $1,000 Printed certificates, handouts, and reference sheets Student kits (USBs, pens, notebooks) Fund for test exercises in mainnet 3. Meals for Trainers – $2,000 Meals and snacks for trainers and marketing team 4. Swag & Incentives – $1,000 T-shirts, stickers, lanyards for participants Small prizes for group work and project demos 5. Trainer Allowance & Honoraria – $1,400 Allowance for trainers and marketing team 6. Contingency Fund – $1,400 Buffer for unexpected costs such as extra transport, supplies, or technical issues Any excess from the funds from significant BCH Stretch Goals Should the BCH price significantly increase during the campaign, any excess funds will be put to good use by extending the program’s impact. Specifically, we will channel these resources into: 1. Student Hackathon – A follow-up event where workshop participants can compete in building Bitcoin Cash-powered applications. 2. On-Site Marketing Activities – Visibility efforts around campuses and communities to raise awareness and adoption of Bitcoin Cash. 3. Cashback Promos with Paytaca Merchants – Incentives for students and local communities to try using BCH in real transactions, reinforcing what they’ve learned.These stretch goals will make the workshops more than a training initiative—they’ll become a catalyst for a wider BCH adoption movement in the Philippines. Impact of Your Support Your contribution will: Train hundreds of students on practical blockchain development Build a new wave of grassroots innovators ready to use and expand Bitcoin Cash Strengthen the BCH ecosystem in communities where Paytaca already has traction Lay the groundwork for future hackathons, startups, and open-source projects built on BCH Together, we are not just funding a workshop—we are investing in the future builders of decentralized technologies powered by Bitcoin Cash. Call to Action Help us spark a movement of young blockchain innovators in the Philippines. By backing these workshops, you’re fueling both education and adoption of Bitcoin Cash at the grassroots. 🔗 Donate today and share this campaign with the BCH community. Together, we can grow Bitcoin Cash adoption from the ground up.

title: BitCoinCash CAMPUS CONNECCT
description: Hello BitcoinCash Community, I’m Dr FLEXA community builder, Educator, and a professional Photographer, I’ve been actively involved in BitcoinCash community for the past 5 years building, connecting ,educating local community about P2P Cash, Digital Currency and promoting adoption particularly BitcoinCash.PAST ACTIVITIESSome of my past activities include onboarding merchants, creating contents and representing BitcoinCash in Web3 and blockchain Conferences/meet ups in Nigeria , I’ve also contributed and participated in various BitcoinCash trends, contests and build a strong community both IRL and on social media..Community engagement Meetupshttps://x.com/drflex6/status/1854559812513669201?s=46Blockchain Presentationshttps://x.com/drflex6/status/1854293254985916606?s=46The Annual BitcoinCash festivalhttps://x.com/drflex6/status/1866190342845841626?s=46CashToken Competitionhttps://x.com/drflex6/status/1853582255907635514?s=46CashToken Community meet up’shttps://x.com/drflex6/status/1837675050259861863?s=46TUTORIALhttps://x.com/drflex6/status/1772128649921081679?s=46WHY AM I CREATING THIS CAMPAIGN ?This program represents a strategic investment in Africa's blockchain future. By supporting BCH education in Nigerian universities, funders will help create the next generation of cryptocurrency advocates, developers, and entrepreneurs in Africa's most populous nationProject developers• Demonstrate BCH's utility for peer-to-peer transactions in the Nigerian context• Foster innovation in blockchain technology among Nigerian youthTarget UniversitiesPhase 1 (Months 1-3): 2 universities including:• University of ILORIN• Modibbo Adama University of TechnologyPhase 2 (Months 3-6): 2-3 universities across all geopolitical zonesProgram ActivitiesMonthly Meetups• 2-hour demonstrationsSpecial Events• Quarterly hackathons focusing on BCH applications• BCH Merchant Day- connecting students with local businesses• Annual conference bringing together all university chaptersDigital Engagement• WhatsApp and Telegram groups for ongoing support• Social media campaigns showcasing student projects• Online resource library in English and local languages•Budget Breakdown (6 months)Core Expenses• Event Coordination & Logistics: $700• Venue rentals, refreshments, materials• Student Incentives & Giveaways: $400• BCH wallets with small amounts, branded materials• Guest Speaker Fees & Travel: $500• Marketing & Promotion: $300• Program Coordination Staff: $500• Technology & Platform Costs: $250• Hackathon & Competition Prizes: $1000Total Funding Request: $3150 (605/BCH )Expected OutcomesShort-term (3 months)• Establish active BCH communities in 2 universities• 200+ students onboarded to BCH wallets• 10+ student projects/presentations completed• 20+ local merchants accepting BCH paymentsLong-term• Self-sustaining student-led BCH communities• 100+ student developers familiar with BCH development• 10+ innovative BCH applications developed by students• Network of 200+ BCH ambassadors across NigeriaSustainability Plan• Train student leaders to continue meetups independently• Establish partnerships with university tech clubs• Create revenue streams through consultation and development services• Connect with Nigerian BCH merchant network for ongoing supportWhy Nigeria?. Largest economy in Africa with growing tech sector• High mobile phone and internet penetration among youth• Existing challenges with traditional banking create demand for alternatives• Strong entrepreneurial culture among university students• English language advantage for global BCH community integrationThis program represents a strategic investment in Africa's blockchain future. By supporting BCHeducation in Nigerian universities, funders will help create the next generation of cryptocurrencyadvocates, developers, and entrepreneurs in Africa's most populous nation.For more information or to discuss this proposal, please contact DR Flex viahttps://x.com/drflex6?s=21

title: The Bitcoincash Wallets Show
description: Bitcoincash Wallet Show : From ASGARD to ZapitThe Bitcoincash Wallet show is set to launch. I will explore wallets to give old and new users of Bitcoincash information on various wallets.The free market needs help.Here we are 16 years of Bitcoin and there is still more use of PayPal than the many alt-coins that provide good liquidity and low fees. It is clear the free market by itself doesn’t make the best thing become the new standard. There is market inertia. Users that use anything that has wide acceptance get advantages of those network effects. Yet those of us who wish to move on to something superior do not get those advantages.Wallet software use just like money has inertia. What the purpose of the show is to show and tell users on which features wallets have and why these features are important.I will seek out a good camera and microphone, as well as a single purpose computer for testing and an iPhone for testing. I will get hosting which should be enough for ten episodes before my donations start coming in.

title: Funding the Faucet
description: Current Gamebugs.cash is a browser-based multiplayer strategy game that doubles as a BCH faucet. You can play it in chrome browser directly on your phone or PC at bugs.cash.The game currently supports up to 8 players per instance. BCH drops appear inside the game world, and players can collect them. If you make it to “Monster Mode,” you can cash out your accumulated BCH.I launched the game in March, and since then I’ve been steadily improving it: fixing bugs, refining “Monster Mode,” and adding items that make the game replayable and fun. Some players have already proven to be very loyal, showing consistent activity. Even with little advertising so far, there’s already a small but dedicated community, and with more exposure I believe the player base can grow significantly.bugs.cash works as both entertainment and education: players outside the crypto space can encounter BCH in a fun and approachable way, while the faucet helps spread awareness and incentivizes exploration of the broader BCH ecosystem.Funding the FaucetThe purpose of this campaign is simple: funding the faucet.Every BCH donated here goes directly to the faucet reserve address. I will steadily load funds into the faucet at a rate of 0.05–0.2 BCH per week until the reserve is used up. This ensures a steady flow of rewards for players, rather than one-time large injections. The faucet address to which the funds will be loaded is bitcoincash:qphh2t0dlx0hhfrcsw39xrrd9ywmswy8fgl5ztl4zf Any additional funds raised will simply also go towards the faucet.Funding the faucet serves two purposes:Player rewards – keeping the game exciting and worthwhile for players who invest time into it.Advertising – free BCH attracts players, spreading awareness of both the game and Bitcoin Cash itself.So in practice, faucet funding works as both community outreach and organic transaction generation on the BCH network.I’ve funded the faucet myself up to now and received some direct contributions from community members, but this structured campaign makes it easier to sustain momentum.Future Plans (not part of this fundme campaign)Long-term, simply giving away BCH isn’t sustainable. To address this, I plan to gradually integrate CashTokens into gameplay.Here are some of the ideas on the roadmap:Pokemon-style gameplay - The ability to convert game items into tokens to trade with other players (as cashtokens on external dapps) or to release back into the game world.bugs.cash CashToken drops – earnable items that might hold value because of the “proof of play” required to obtain them. I could also drop discount tokens for third party projects.Equippable CashTokens – for example, equipping a “BCH Guru” token might grant +2 health when equipped.Advertising on more platforms – The small playerbase has helped a lot with testing ideas and gameplay and getting the game running smoothly. In future I plan on focussing more on advertising the gameProof of Workbugs.cash is live, running, and already distributing BCH to players. Here are ways to verify my ongoing work and involvement in the BCH community:🎥 Podcast – I was featured on The Bitcoin Cash Podcast: Watch here🐦 Twitter – My personal account with BCH updates and game progress: @kreeflewer🎮 Play bugs.cash – https://bugs.cash💬 Discord community – Join here📜 Patch Notes – Patch Notes🔗 Track faucet activity – Blockchair linkI also attended BCHBliss in 2025 and met some awesome people there.ThanksThanks to the following players for donating to the game:"sakuracash","jabber","neutron","worldofbch","devin","cptami","hampher","cashdragon" Special Thanks to thebitcoincashpodcast and afifthofgaming in assisting with initial launch and testing

description: Current Gamebugs.cash is a browser-based multiplayer strategy game that doubles as a BCH faucet. You can play it in chrome browser directly on your phone or PC at bugs.cash.The game currently supports up to 8 players per instance. BCH drops appear inside the game world, and players can collect them. I will steadily load funds into the faucet at a rate of 0.04–0.08 BCH per week until the reserve is used up. This will make these funds last atleast 1 year and up to two years. This ensures a steady flow of rewards for players, rather than one-time large injections. The faucet address to which the funds will be loaded is bitcoincash:qphh2t0dlx0hhfrcsw39xrrd9ywmswy8fgl5ztl4zf. Note that the payouts per score (rating) have been tweaked after launch, it was way to high at the start. The faucet also does not necessarily have funds at a given time. So earnings would be higher if the faucet was funded. Also note that unclaimed earnings are automatically recycled to the faucet at the end of the month, so not all earnings are claimed.The above chart shows average player activity during a week (since March) counted in 1 hour buckets. Only players are counted that reached monster mode. Monster mode usually takes at least half an hour to activate. I plan on having a happy hour with higher payouts on Saturdays at 6PM UTC, encouraging players to play together at the same time.The above chart shows number of onchain transactions generated by the faucet address per block. The chart starts at 1 March and ends at the start of October. 8448 onchain transactions generated in this period. see address: bitcoincash:qphh2t0dlx0hhfrcsw39xrrd9ywmswy8fgl5ztl4zfsee the following link for more charts: wiki charts Future Plans (not part of this fundme campaign)Long-term, simply giving away BCH isn’t sustainable. title: mainnet_pat 2026
description: Hey everyone, I would like to announce a FundMe.cash campaign to fund a whole year of my full-time work for the BCH community. I've decided to make this fundraiser time-based instead of project-based because there are a lot of projects which will benefit from my contributions, and running several smaller funding campaigns is not really viable; see the project breakdown below.Project breakdownMajor projectsHD Wallet implementation in mainnet-js, integration into CashonizeHD Wallet support in WC2 specification, implementation in the @bch-wc2 NPM package, integration into Cashonize, upgrade of WC2 interfaces for better error handling - The current BCH WC2 spec is very specific about the transaction – the transaction signing request must define the exact transaction shape; the wallet may not alter it to add any new inputs or outputs, just perform the signing. This approach turned out to be a great start for BCH dApps, but it narrows the freedom of UTXO spending choice of the wallet, making the requesting dApp only know the UTXOs of the connected address. This approach fails miserably in the case of an HD Wallet – a client shall not know all HD addresses of wallets but only the specified connected addresses. I propose to revisit the spec and add support for partial transaction signing requests, where additional P2PKH inputs will be provided by the wallet, both non-HD and HD with subaddresses, opaque to the dApp. - Furthermore, the current spec returns to the client a `undefined` result for any underlying wallet errors associated with signing or any other internal handling. The updated spec aims to define a new result object which indicates the error code and message, corresponding to the most common signing failure cases, to enhance the Wallet-dApp interaction.Exploratory work on CashScript enhancements to support: - MAST contracts and QuantumRoot contracts * I have authored MAST support in NexScript (link 1, link 2), a clone of CashScript on Nexa, and it is possible to port this work back to BCH, with new modern features as functions, bitwise ops and higher VM limits. * I also want to explore the implementation of QuantumRoot contracts described by Jason. - Contracts with global state kept in sidecar token UTXOs * Having a lot of experience in CashScripts compiler modification from my work on Nexa's NexScript and adding the debugging functionality to CashScript, I am confident I will be able to extend the CashScript compiler to support Solidity-like syntax to define contract state variables. The values of these variables will be stored in token NFT commitments and provided in sidecar inputs to be spent together with this contract. Such a syntax will bring BCH's CashScript closer to the EVM world's Solidity, with the potential to attract more developer attention and to port existing EVM projects and innovate on our blockchain. - Further development of the testing suite to become a standalone package similar to Hardhat * This CashScript add-on I have authored grew mature and independent enough to be extracted from the repository. It is already used in two of my recent projects – the SushiBar staking contract and the Olando community project – and has received many updates. It is now time to create a templating project from it, similar to Hardhat for EVM.I expect major projects to take up to 60% of the budget.Minor projectsOngoing maintenance and improvements of mainnet-js: restructure packages to make including the lib as a dependency more lightweight, native BIP39 support using libauth, encrypted storage of wallet secrets, support DS-proofs for incoming payments, support SIGHASH_UTXOS signing, BIP21 URI scheme payment protocol, updating documentation, and other issues from the backlog.Better token support in Sickpig's explorer: add full token metadata support, and improve the address page to list all tokens as a summary.Implement the x402 spec for micropayments in BCH and CashTokens. Develop a gating proxy service which enforces the payment scheme, an example application which makes use of this service and implements the user-facing UI for conducting such payments using a built-in wallet and a WC2-connected wallet.Implementation of CashFusion for CashTokens. We have discussed the details with Jonald, and he is excited to work on this task together with me.4byte.directory-like web index of BCH contracts. It will both help the wallets to identify the P2SH contract spends and the P2S contract locks. The latter is also very handy because P2S locks will not have an address in our usual sense.Contributions to Cashonize and its community forks: CC-Wallet (built-in cauldron support) and Olando-Wallet.Support for watch-wallets, support for importing WIF and hex-encoded private keysSupport internationalizationImplement the improvements from new mainnet-js developmentsI expect minor projects to take up to 30% of the budget, including the compensation for delegated tasks and collaboration with other developers.Ongoing supportThere are many projects of mine which require maintenance, support and enhancements. I do want to have time to respond to community requests and keep them cool and nice and provide the means to interact with them on API or development level. Or better to be embeddable in other projects. Specifically, among those are the TapSwap.cashNFT marketplace and the BCHPUMP.cash CashTokens launchpad, which could become wallet-embeddable software.I am running many community-enhancing servers – bchn.pat.mn, fulcrum.pat.mn, explorer.pat.mn, blockbook.pat.mn, chaingraph.pat.mn, ipfs-bch.pat.mn, bcmr.pat.mn and others. This campaign will help these being further maintained and server costs covered for the entire 2026.I expect the support work and maintenance costs to take about 10% of the budget.Would love to doThere is always some cool work around waiting to be done, and I will be happy to pick it up if budget allows.Contributions to BCHN - `/someone` is needed in supporting CEX handling of CashTokens, and I have a good contribution history to that repo. - Improvements of RPC calls and their hardening, including whitelisting the call methods, akin to those used in my proxy service bch.pat.mn - Improvements to web-specific CORS handlingContinue work on Subsquid integration; more detailsCampaign objectiveWith this fundraiser, I seek to raise 500 BCH to fund the long-term work on the features and new projects described above. With my vast experience in BCH development and knowledge of the projects I will be working with, I am confident that my contributions will be very valuable for the community.Who am I?I am pat, a hobby Bitcoiner since 2011 and a Bitcoin Cash developer since 2020.My most recent and notable contributions were in the field of CashTokens and BCH infrastructure: I added CashTokens support to the BlockBook indexer, implemented atomic swaps between Monero and Bitcoin Cash, created BCHPUMP.cash and xPUMP staking, and added debugging functionality to CashScript. I've pioneered the WalletConnectV2 support in BCH for the Cashonize wallet, prepared mainnet-js for the May '23 upgrade, created Paytaca Connect, developed the dApps for Emerald DAO and BitCats Heroes Club, consulted BCH Guru developers, and launched TapSwap.I have contributed to many BCH projects: BCHN, Fulcrum, Electron-Cash, LibAuth, CashScript, gs++, SLPDB and others.In October 2021, I teamed up with Kasumi and became the Lead Dev for MistSwap, bringing analytics, governance, sideshift integration, LNS, SmartSafe, Sonar.Cash and much more to the MistSwap and smartBCH network.I've started my way working for read.cash and later switched to help with mainnet.cash – the main library used by noise.cash. I've brought numerous features to mainnet.cash, the most notable being SLP, smartBCH and cashtokens support.I am most active on GitHub and Telegram.

title: Konk: Your Bitcoin Cash Marketplace
description: The Mission: A Peer to Peer Bitcoin Cash Marketplace for a Freer WorldIn a world where freedom is being throttled at an alarming rate, Konk is a tool being built for people who want an escape ramp, to trade freely without asking for permission or fear of being censored or debanked, supported and influenced by the whole Bitcoin Cash and wider freedom-advocacy community. If there was ever a time for BCH to show what it can really do in offering an alternative to the current dystopian creep, it is now! If there was ever a product that can solve the immediate problems that people are facing of controlled access to food, essentials and services, it is a decentralised P2P marketplace that uses the soundest version of Peer to Peer Electronic Cash for the world. We need the BCH version of XMR Bazaar….but better!Konk will become an uncensorable P2P online marketplace, powered by Bitcoin Cash. Anyone will be able to buy or sell using BCH as payment. No banks, no gatekeepers and no KYC.I’m building it from the ground up, alone(currently), as I believe it is a missing tool in our permissionless eco-system that will ultimately give people back their freedoms: - A real marketplace where BCH can be earned and spent directly- A platform designed for everyday trade, not speculation- A practical step toward a circular economy that works outside the systemKonk isn’t just for those already deep in crypto. It’s for the millions looking for a way out of the collapsing fiat system, who just need the tools to start. These people will be a huge target market as we move rapidly into this new era of totalitarian digital control and for this reason, Konk will have strong focus on being newcomer-friendly and familiar. Something that feels as easy to use as Amazon or Ebay, doesn’t scare people with overly doom-mongering messaging and plenty of Bitcoin Cash educational material + news targeted towards people finding the BCH community and crypto for the first time.In addition to trade via delivery networks within nation borders and internationally, Konk will have features and functions that specifically help users to trade locally for food and other goods which will provide support for local producers and sellers who are currently feeling the squeeze of regulation from government overreach. Connecting local communities again will create solid, localised pockets of trade using Bitcoin Cash which can then be connected over time with other communities, developing a robustness that can withstand any future restrictions such as the ones we saw with Covid lockdowns etc.The website url can be found at the end of this FundMe campaign for you to follow the current status of the build but I invite you to read through the full campaign first. Why Konk Is Critical for Bitcoin CashWe all know that real adoption matters - and not just at merchant point-of-sale.Konk directly addresses two of the most frequently asked questions in the BCH space:1. Where can I buy things with my BCH?2. How can I earn BCH without using a centralised, KYC exchange?Konk answers both:✅ You can buy anything listed on Konk using BCH✅ You can earn BCH by selling goods and services locally or globallyHowever, this is not a “Build it and they will come” project. I’m not so naïve as to think that we build a tool and people will find it and use it, especially when it comes to crypto. I will be actively marketing to sellers in a strategic way to get them onboard.Furthermore, should Konk achieve it’s goal of becoming a “killer app”, it will also help secure the future of BCH and the freedom of everyone by bringing in fresh liquidity from new bch users and providing high on-chain transaction volume for the time when the block subsidy ends. BTC was neutered and turned its back on scaling. BCH still has a chance, but we must create the tools that drive serious demand. Konk is one of those tools. Konk’s marketThere will be 4 target groups initially with some crossover between them; People already in crypto/BCH: These users already understand how crypto works as well as the need for it as an alternative to the current financial system. This target group will naturally and organically see much of the online marketing for Konk and give it a go. It is our job to make it more attractive(ux) and useful to use than alternatives.Local producers/sellers: This group of users will be local producers of food or goods, as well as people who have items to sell from their home. The idea is to provide a direct, local link between producers and buyers that doesn’t involve large companies or stores. Facebook marketplace has become popular for P2P trade and it’s an area that Konk will look to capitalise on also. I will be organising local BCH educational meetups and seller’s markets local to myself to promote both Konk and the use of BCH and will then be sharing templates for these activities for anyone else to replicate in their own local communities.Online Small Business Owners: There are many online small business owners who have amazing products and are trying to cut through the noise on apps such as Instagram, Facebook, TikTok, YouTube to get their products seen to be able to launch or grow but many struggle to break through and their dream falls away. Konk will provide an additional market for them to advertise their products on, to a dedicated and loyal community who loves BCH-accepting businesses. I will be actively contacting these types of business daily to get them hooked and selling on Konk. Once the first start up businesses become successful because they sold on Konk using Bitcoin Cash, it will trigger a snowball of interested sellers.Current users of traditional “big” online marketplaces: If you go on Reddit and read the comments on subreddits such as Amazon or Ebay, many sellers are now leaving or giving up due to a variety of reasons from; restrictions getting tighter, having to go through additional kyc checks to ever-increasing fees and their products not being seen by the algorithm if they don’t pay to promote their products. I intend to survey these users to understand their pain points and figure out a) how to address them through Konk and b) how to make a transition to selling (and buying) on Konk as easy and attractive as possible. I believe this active communication with other marketplace users will garner strong signups and usage from Amazon and Ebay refugees.Who’s Building Konk?I go by NeonDaThal and I’m a lifelong entrepreneur and agorist, having over 15 years launching and managing businesses predominantly in the hospitality sector. I am a Bitcoin Cash maximilist and believe it’s the best chance at freeing money, and therefore the world, that we have. The more quality BCH-based businesses and apps that we have available, the more adoptees we will see arriving. I’m attempting to do my part and bring some of these apps to the market. One of my current projects is bitcoincashgloballotto.comI’ve always been passionate about the science of business and marketing and have never been a coder. But I knew that Konk could open up the pathway to increasing adoption among regular people so I taught myself to code out of necessity as trying to find the right developers was taking too long. I understood that this needed to be built now.This is my contribution to increasing trade through BCH with a focus on bringing new people in to the space.Now, I’m ready to finish the build and launch Konk publicly. But I need your help to shape how the tool develops.Where Konk is at now and why I’m rebuilding Konk from scratchKonk already existed once. I built an initial version using Wordpress and plugins which was called Agora Connect and I later rebranded to Konk. It worked well for testing and it gave me invaluable hands-on experience building a marketplace ux.But it didn’t take long to hit limits: performance issues, lack of flexibility, and too much reliance on third-party systems. I realised that if Konk was to be truly permissionless, resilient and built for the long term, it would need to be re-imagined as a standalone app from the ground up. So that’s where I stopped, started to learn to code from scratch and am now building the app using React and Next.js.Freedom and censorship-resistance matter way more than convenience and the goal is to ensure that Konk can’t be switched off and will continue to develop with that ethos at the forefront of development.The app is currently being built and isn’t yet finished but instead of working silently behind the scenes, I’ve decided to announce it now and “build in public” as I think doing so with input from the community will be interesting, fun and will actually shape the direction of Konk’s development in a more aligned way. Support, ideas and ongoing feedback will mean a better final product. This new version of Konk is being rebuilt with intent, transparency, and hopefully with the whole community behind it. And that’s exactly why it will work.The goal is to become the best decentralised marketplace out there – for regular people, without permission from any bank or government.Features you can currently check out live on the app:Homepage: https://konk.market/ - dynamic hero slider to showcase BCH projects(will eventually be paid ads ran here but only BCH aligned businesses/projects. Currently shows different media types for projects randomly chosen)Cart: https://konk.market/cartProduct Page: https://konk.market/K007 (calculated delivery time, dynamic “deliver to”, dynamic quantity selector, dynamic BCH price always shown, Local delivery map feature if set by sellerProduct Search: Select “Products” in search bar and hit search to see mock products search resultsCurrency change: change currency of all product prices by hovering over “EN” menu item in the header next to the search bar. Base currency is BCH and all currencies (currently) change via CoinGecko apiAd banners and space throughout the appThe app is currently optimised for desktop view and some pages or elements may not appear correctly in mobile. If you see something that needs fixing or layout that’s not looking good, tell me!"Why This Means So Much to Me"The road to building Konk hasn’t been easy. Over the last few years, I’ve faced some personal challenges; from losing my job to helping my mother recover from a serious stroke. I’ve taken on part-time work to make ends meet while teaching myself to code and bringing this vision to life.My wife has supported me more than words can say, and I owe it to her and to everyone who believes in permissionless trade, voluntaryism and freedom to make Konk an overwhelming success.I’m not saying this to gain sympathy. I share it to be transparent and to affirm my deep commitment that this isn’t a passing project or startup gamble, it’s my life’s work. I believe Konk and Bitcoin Cash are tools that the world needs more than ever and regardless of the difficulties or length of time, it will be built.Funds soughtFunds sought: 45 BCHI will not be looking to find investors at any time. This is my commitment. This needs to be a tool for the community without undue outside influence. It’s also for this reason that I seek to fundraise for this project through FundMe.The funds will allow me to:· ✅ Finish core development and UI polish· ✅ Pay for infrastructure (hosting, node, security) for 6 months· ✅ Cover my time to work full-time on completion, marketing and scaling for 6 months· ✅ Hire dev to help finalize BCH payment gateway· ✅ Launch Konk faster and stronger — while momentum is building for BCHAll contributors will receive lifetime fee-free listings on Konk and their account set with “Ambassador” status.How Konk WorksAll product’s will be free to list and when an item is sold on Konk, the BCH goes directly and instantly to the seller’s wallet, minus a small, flat 5% fee. No holding period, no 3rd party, just instantly peer to peer.Konk will rely on a reputation system rather than a moderated escrow system which still suffers from issues and risk of abuse as a result of 3rd party moderation. The reputation system will be clear and will function with the same ethos that people employ when dealing and trading with people in real life; to begin with, only buying from trusted sellers and later, based on them having a high reputation score.Compared to others(UK sellers mid-2025):Amazon (UK)Individual seller: £0.75 per item sold (no monthly fee)Professional seller: £25/month (ex VAT) subscriptionReferral fees: generally 8–15% of item price, depending on categoryDigital Services Tax: additional ~2% applied to referral‑based fees for UK sales eBay (UK)Insertion fee: first 250 fixed‑price listings per month free, then ~£0.35/listing Final value fee (private sellers): 12.8% up to £5,000; 3% on portion above £5,000; plus a £0.30–£0.40 order fee depending on order size Buyer Protection fee (from July 2025): added cost to buyers—not sellers. For sellers, payouts now delayed until delivery confirmation EtsyListing fee: $0.20 (≈£0.16) per item, valid for 4 months Transaction fee: 6.5% of the total sale value (including shipping)Mandatory offsite advertising fee: 12% (under $10K annual sales) or 15% (over $10K), applies to entire purchase if buyer clicks promotional ad Why Konk Is a Better Choice5% flat fee vs. Amazon’s 8–15% + possible extra taxes, eBay’s 12.8% + insertion and buyer fees, or Etsy’s layered charges.Instant payout to your BCH wallet, no holds, no KYC, no waiting.Simple, transparent fee system with no hidden costs or subscription tiers.Sellers keep 95%, every time, and on your own terms.Revenue: How will we sustain Konk long-term?Revenue will come from the flat 5% fee when an item is sold but also from advertising in a variety of strategic parts of the app:Banner ads (a range of banner ad space is already available and users will be able to advertise their products, business, media etc and target them to desired demographics)Media carousels (as on the homepage, there will be media carousels throughout the app where users will be able to advertise using more dynamic media such as video, gifs, audio, podcasts etc)“Featured” product listings (when uploading product listings, sellers will be able to pay a “promote this listing” fee which will ensure the listing appears at the top of search results in the “featured” section for the promoted period.I want to prove that businesses can survive and thrive solely on Bitcoin Cash without the need to convert back to fiat, to encourage others to follow suit.Features Being Built in this FundMeInstant 0-conf BCH payments to sellersFamiliar marketplace interface (think Amazon + eBay)Seller profiles, reviews, account dashboardsReputation-based systemLocal, national & international shipping optionsSearch functionality for products, users, articles/blogsWishlist & Buy Again functionalityFee-free accounts for all contributorsBoosted listings & strategic ad space for BCH projectsNo KYC: just an email to get startedFuture VisionI don’t want Konk to stop at only a basic online marketplace. The vision for Konk is that it will grow and evolve to incorporate additional functionality that will benefit people way beyond a place to buy and sell goods. Future roadmap for Konk includes:Services (language, design, plumbing, tutoring, etc.)Rentals (holiday lets, rooms, property listings)Jobs board, gigs, and freelance workCommunity content, articles, and BCH builder hubsMobile appsZKP privacy (to be researched and discussed with other devs)Konk NFT for use by sellers for loyalty rewards, discounts etcKonk walletNFT Product listingsNFT ReputationsReusable delivery addressesNFC smartcard integration for easier BCH spendingAnd as always, we promise to not require KYC at any point.Resilience & ResistanceIf Konk succeeds, resistance should be expected. The app is being built with safeguarding plans in mind including robust hosting, encrypted backups and alternative deployments.It’s very important for Konk to be seen as friendly by the masses so there will be some restriction on products that are allowed to be sold dependent on region(but we will not allow these to be enforced by any authority in that region). All restricted items will be as a result of a careful risk assessment done by Konk and based on perceived public perception. A future plan to address this and for those that need the functionality, will be the launch of an onion web version of the app which will be less restrictive.A truly free marketplace that can’t be censored is the mission, without asking for permission.Why Your Support MattersI’m just one person but I’m all in on BCH. I’m utterly convinced that BCH is the best chance we have to free people from the system that has oppressed them for centuries and continues to tighten its grip.Your contribution does more than help build Konk. It:Strengthens the real-world utility of BCHHelps build tools for actual freedomSupports a builder committed to this missionGives you and the community a platform that pays itself forwardThis isn’t just a project worth funding, it’s a tool that makes Bitcoin Cash stronger and the world freer. "Nobody's coming to save you" can be modified for us to: "Nobody's coming to build the tools for us", we're going to have to build them ourselves.This FundMe not being funded won’t stop the project and I will keep building it with the same passion and focus as always. A successful FundMe just ensures that launch happens much sooner, giving people a genuine escape hatch to make money or buy what they need without having to submit to government-dictated restrictions to their lives that are happening right now. I hope you’ll be part of making it real.Web app: konk.market Follow the journey, be first to see the features evolve and be part of shaping the Konk story by following the Konk social media accounts: X.com InstagramInstagram Hot ProductsTelegramContact me: X.com: @NeonDaThal Telegram: @NeonDaThal

title: Bitcoin Cash at More Events 🌍🚀
description: Overview / SummaryThroughout Q3 (June, July, August), the Bitcoin Cash (BCH) community has been highly represented at top-tier events across Africa not just attending, but actively speaking, exhibiting, and building partnerships that put Bitcoin Cash on the global tech and crypto map.With your support, BCH has shown up strongly at major conferences such as the Africa Stablecoin Conference, GITEX Africa, and Open Source Festival Africa, among others — connecting with thousands of builders, developers, founders, and policymakers.As we close out the year, we want to keep that momentum going through November and December, ensuring Bitcoin Cash continues to show up, engage, and lead conversations around financial freedom, blockchain adoption, and open innovation.Digital Nigeria International Conference: 11th -13th November https://x.com/Nurain_Dev/status/1849810278914867204 https://x.com/bch_nigeria/status/1719630079326671236ISN Annual Gathering 2025: 19th -21st November DevFest Yola 2025: 29th November 💡 Bitcoin Cash at ISN Annual Gathering 2025The BCH Hub has been a proud member of the Innovation Support Network (ISN) since 2023 — a network that connects innovation and tech hubs across Nigeria and beyond.Through this partnership, we’ve built strong relationships with policy shapers, innovators, and ecosystem leaders, contributing to blockchain inclusion and digital economy discussions. In 2024, BCH Hub was recognized with an award for its contribution to blockchain awareness and ecosystem growth.This year, we plan to continue that presence at the ISN Annual Gathering 2025, strengthening BCH’s role in Africa’s innovation policy landscape and highlighting Bitcoin Cash as a technology that drives real impact.📎 Past Engagements & Highlights:https://x.com/Nurain_Dev/status/1854618189302276376https://x.com/Nurain_Dev/status/1854618204867281075👩🏽‍💻 Bitcoin Cash at DevFest 2025The Google Developer Community (GDG) is one of the largest tech networks across Sub-Saharan Africa — and Bitcoin Cash has built a strong relationship with it since 2022.Over the years, BCH has supported and participated in multiple DevFests across Nigerian cities, connecting with developers, students, and innovators, showcasing BCH as a powerful open-source payment technology.This season, we plan to support and participate in two key DevFests — Abuja and Bauchi, where BCH Hub will engage developers, host demo sessions, and share educational resources.These events help expand the BCH builders’ network, foster grassroots adoption, and spark new innovation projects powered by Bitcoin Cash.📎 Past Engagements & Highlights:https://x.com/bch_nigeria/status/1721936874455831032https://x.com/bch_nigeria/status/1727731920190996865https://x.com/Nurain_Dev/status/1852697024560267298💰 What This FundMe CoversYour contribution will help us:Support travel and accommodation for BCH Hub reps attending these eventsProduce BCH-branded swags and educational materialsFund on-site BCH giveaways and engagement activitiesCover booth setup and participation logistics💚 Goal: 5 BCHWe’re raising 5 BCH to power this end-of-year push.This campaign will help us continue what we started, building on the momentum from the Africa Stablecoin Conference, GITEX, Open Source Festival, and more — and ensure Bitcoin Cash keeps showing up, connecting, and growing in Africa’s biggest tech and innovation spaces.Together, we’re putting Bitcoin Cash on the global map and ensuring the green movement stays visible and impactful.

title: The Bitcoin Cash Podcast: Episodes 166 - 175
description: FundMe: Episodes 166 - 175 of The Bitcoin Cash PodcastFunding requested: 29 BCH (~$491 / BCH)Deliverable- Episodes 166 - 175. The same show you already know and (hopefully) love.- Weekly BCH Bullet newsletter on Sundays during the delivery period of the episodes.NotesFundMeThe Podcast is now located in its own studio! This is part of an effort to increase the professionalism of the broadcast, with improved cameras, lightning & set design. Further improvements to come on this front but viewers should already note the initial changes on stream. A dedicated workspace has also helped in the focus & output of the Podcast generally. Of course, more space & equipment itself requires more investment - but BCH can't grow with media production done from bedrooms forever. In other news, BLAZE is underway & the Podcast has been thrilled to help with that initiative. Materials from BLAZE can be archived for future developers joining the ecosystem & it's giving BCH a strong outwards impression to new builders and industry competitors alike.VisibilityThe Podcast has now passed 3 000 Youtube subscribers, and appears rate limited by Twitter somehow on its final precipe before 10 000 Twitter followers. Nevertheless, the Podcast has innovated around these visibility limits by introducing The BCH Bullet, now with 29 Substack subscribers and 165 Twitter followers but already pulling in 1.5-4k views per tweet! The Bullet has delivered 13 weekly columns to date to enthusiastic community interest, validating its concept & making it a suitable include in the offerings of the Podcast to the BCH FundMe community.Note to the communityThe final stretch of November/December for 2025 is upon us. Layla is locking in, BCH is increasingly shining in a market where BTC is embroiled in pointless infighting, most alternatives seem to be just scams or trapped in circular techno""innovating"" & both digital cash & privacy are beginning to receive recognition and interest. Feels like 2026 might be the BCH break out year, and we can set us ourselves up with a strong finish in 2025.Let's keep building Bitcoin Cash.Proof of WorkI have aggregated previous delivery & community work (including previous 4x FundMes + 5x Flipstarter delivery & accountability) on a dedicated page on the Bitcoin Cash Podcast website. See it here: bitcoincashpodcast.com/proof-of-work.Cost breakdown:- 0.6 BCH topic research, episode hosting- 0.6 BCH production & editing costs- 0.1 BCH Thumbnail graphics & episode distribution- 0.15 BCH Timestamps, links & episode summary- 0.15 BCH Youtube Shorts creation & distribution1.6 BCH / episodex10 episodes = 16 BCH- 0.2 BCH per BCH Bulletx12 Bullets = 2.4 BCH0.5 BCH: BADGER burn.6 BCH: Studio rent.2 BCH: Accounting costs.2 BCH misc expenses: Ongoing costs such as website hosting & domain registration, supporting other community fundraisers, FAQ maintenance etc.0.1 BCH: Fundme.cash feeContributor rewards:Donations of > 0.5 BCH will receive a shoutout at the end of each show (like the Patrons and Sponsors) unless your name/message is rude or slanderous or you ask me not to or it's otherwise unreadable for some reason.Badgers.cashBadgers has been a strong success, and since the last FundMe has grown its TVL yet another 33% to now over 400 BCH TVL (!!!), with very minimal marketing & ongoing tech support. I really enjoy contributing to it with the Podcast FundMe raises as Proof of Community good will & I am still trying to encourage other ecosystem players to consider doing this too.-- Explanation of Badgers belowI discovered that BADGER token, obtainable only by provably time-locking BCH on-chain, has traded as high as #10 on Cauldron. Speculative liquidity thus created a native BCH yield for time-locking BCH. This is an incredible community innovation, as it allows anyone to yield on their BCH by provably HODLing (boosting price, liquidity & thus marketing reach) and it also allows anyone to generate goodwill in the community by provably purchasing & burning BADGER. It's always been possible to "donate" to all BCH holders by burning BCH itself, but that's psychologically unappealing due to the limited supply of BCH. BADGER avoids that, is fun, generates more transactions & usage of CashTokens tech.In order to boost Badger TVL, what is needed is some kind of non-speculative demand to buy (& ideally burn) BADGER tokens. My idea for this is that community projects can show goodwill to the community by provably burning & I'm going to start with the Podcast. As a part of this ongoing experiment, some of the desired raised funds will be used to purchase Badger on Cauldron.quest and provably burn it. Readers are more than welcome to frontrun this by purchasing BADGER, which achieves somewhat the same effect! If this caught on as a community initiative by aligned projects, it would encourage more people to lock BCH, boosting the price and bringing more resources to the community to run even more projects (which can burn more BADGER). Raising BCH on-chain TVL is one of the few ways to defeat naked short selling, Tether inflation or other manipulation of the BCH price so I am very interested in new & potentially compelling ways to encourage this.DisclaimerI will be completing the deliverables whether or not the FundMe gets funded, the continuation of the show & my assistance to the community is in no way contingent on this funding request. However, as per previous FundMe, I believe this is a valuable community service & get a lot of feedback in that regard. The community contributions do a lot to help the show operate in the community's best interest without compromising for financial necessity.ContactAs always, for any questions, comments or suggestions I can be reached at jeremy@bitcoincashpodcast.com, on Twitter at https://x.com/TheBCHPodcast or on Telegram https://t.me/BitcoinCashPodcast.

title: Cash Like Privacy on BCH - Phase 1 Demo Publish
description: What I’m delivering in Phase-1 (the demo you can run)Entry point: src/demo.js → demoSilentTransfer() (built to dist/demo.js)One-liner run: node dist/demo.jsEnd-to-end flow (exactly as implemented):Wallets & SRPA derivation: derive a stealth address for Bob from his paycode + my ephemeral key; extract Bob’s pubkey from the paycode.Alice prepares the spend: optional consolidation; token category genesis; ephemeral key; encrypt amount for Bob; embed ephemeral pubkey + ciphertext in OP_RETURN.Sigma ZK + Pedersen binding: generate an off-chain Sigma range proof; commit only HASH256(proofBytes) on-chain; bind the Pedersen commitment C into a CashTokens NFT commitment.Covenant (Bob-only): script enforces (a) proof-hash match, (b) exact first-output amount, (c) spender key matches Bob.Alice funds: build & broadcast the covenant output with token prefix; OP_RETURN carries the encrypted payload.Bob redeems: find the covenant UTXO by TXID; decrypt the amount; deterministically re-prove to get the same proof hash; verify NFT commitment matches his regenerated C; spend back to Alice.What’s hidden todayReceiver identity at funding (SRPA stealth receive).Proof internals (only a hash is on-chain).What isn’t hidden yet (drives Phase-2)Amounts (plaintext output value).Spender identity at redemption (Bob’s pubkey).OP_RETURN is visible (contents private).Release plan (FundMe-friendly)Repo status: currently private.Public release date: docs + cleaned repo in ~2–3 weeks, regardless of donations.Donations help me prioritize polish (docs, reference vectors, runbook) and keep pushing the roadmap.About Me & Prior WorkNostr Multi-Chain Tipping NIP — secp256k1 chains tipping on Nostrhttps://github.com/bastiancarmy/nostr-multi-chain-tipping-nipNostr Key Demo — derive BCH keys from nsec/npub identitieshttps://github.com/bastiancarmy/nostr-key-demoBitcoin Cash Node Bootstrap — BCH + Fulcrum via Ansiblehttps://github.com/bastiancarmy/bitcoin-cash-node-bootstrapnos2bch — Chrome extension wallet funding + Nostr npub tippinghttps://github.com/bastiancarmy/nos2bchTelegram: @bastiancarmichaelGitHub: https://github.com/bastiancarmy

title: Cash-Like Privacy on BCH - Phase 2 Beaconless Bind
description: This is a continuation of Phase 1 work hereWhy Private “Cash-Like” Payments MatterIn the real world, paying with cash doesn’t broadcast who paid whom or how much. Online, most payments do. Phase-2 aims to bring cash-like privacy to Bitcoin Cash—so people can transact without leaving an obvious trail, while every rule still verifies on-chain.What’s a “note,” in plain English? A note is a private claim on value. On-chain, it looks like an ordinary output; off-chain, only the intended recipient gets the information needed to spend it. No public beacons, no global scanning.Objective (Phase-2)Deliver a spend flow that provides:Shielded identities at both funding and spend.Shielded amounts (no plaintext values on-chain).Beaconless transactions (no OP_RETURN scanning; proofs bind to the actual outputs).On-chain verifiability (no oracles; standard network validation).Tight size budget (target ≤ ~100 KB for one-off spends).Standard-looking deposits/spends with private, end-to-end note delivery.Flexibility ClauseI’ll stay technology-agnostic (hash-centric, PQ-friendly preferred). If research reveals a better approach, I’ll adopt it and publish a short design note, an updated threat model, and a byte/CPU budget comparison.This Campaign Funds M2 — Beaconless Output BindingObjective (what this solves):Prove a transaction is paying the actual outputs—without revealing data in a public beacon like OP_RETURN. This keeps transactions standard-looking.What I’ll build:A contract routine that recomputes a compact fingerprint of the transaction’s own outputs (values, token fields if present, and a hash of each locking script).The prover supplies the same fingerprint off-chain.The contract compares them: match → spend succeeds; mismatch → fail.Why this matters for cash-like privacy:Removes visible metadata “tells”.Enables private note delivery off-chain while still binding proofs to the real on-chain outputs.Acceptance (done when):Fingerprint parity passes end-to-end tests.A tiny reference tool can recompute the fingerprint for any TX locally.Size budget respected (leaves room for later ZK).Overall Project Plan (Phase-2)M2 — Beaconless Output Binding (this campaign) Contract checks the real outputs (no public beacon) by matching on-chain and off-chain fingerprints.M3 — Shielded Note State (no global scanning) Minimal state UTXO + ordinary-looking note UTXOs; enforce membership & no-double-spend on-chain, within byte budget.M4 — Succinct Proof Integration (tech-agnostic) Add an on-chain-verifiable, succinct proof that attests ownership, correct outputs, and conservation—without revealing identities/amounts—under a strict size budget.M5 — Privacy Polish & Wallet UX Reduce surfaced key material, provide wallet hygiene guidance, and ship a public test harness with runnable examples.(Each future milestone will have its own FundMe page. Overflow from a campaign rolls forward into the next milestone or audits.)Claim PolicyBackers control their pledge until I claim. I’ll claim when I’m ready to start M2 so you retain full control before work begins.Use of FundsContract implementation & tests for output fingerprint parityMinimal reference tool to recompute fingerprintsHardening for size/CPU budget discipline and future ZK integrationOverflow goes to the next milestone or independent review.Risks & MitigationsSize/VM limits: I design to a strict budget; if a technique risks the budget, I switch per the Flexibility Clause.Integration risk: I provide a tiny local tool + runnable examples to make verification easy.R&D uncertainty: I favor well-understood, hash-centric approaches and publish trade-offs.Backer Guidance (optional but helpful)For privacy, consider using CashFusion before pledging, avoid reusing fused UTXOs until the campaign is claimed, and consider splitting larger pledges.About Me & Prior WorkNostr Multi-Chain Tipping NIP — secp256k1 chains tipping on Nostrhttps://github.com/bastiancarmy/nostr-multi-chain-tipping-nipNostr Key Demo — derive BCH keys from nsec/npub identitieshttps://github.com/bastiancarmy/nostr-key-demoBitcoin Cash Node Bootstrap — BCH + Fulcrum via Ansiblehttps://github.com/bastiancarmy/bitcoin-cash-node-bootstrapnos2bch — Chrome extension wallet funding + Nostr npub tippinghttps://github.com/bastiancarmy/nos2bchTelegram: @bastiancarmichaelGitHub: https://github.com/bastiancarmy
