title: Noticias BCH en Español
description: Buenas Comunidad de BCH en español.Somos BCH News en Español. Un pequeño equipo de 3 personas defensores del efectivo electrónico p2p.Desde hace aproximadamente 1 año nos dimos a la tarea de hacer disponibles en idioma español los boletines de noticias relacionadas a BCH que circulan en grupos de BCH de la comunidad angloparlante.Comenzando en Enero del presente año traduciendo el boletín semanal de la BitcoinCash Foundation y trabajando actualmente en los boletines de The BCH Bullet, llevamos casi un año entregando de forma ininterrumpida las últimas noticias a la comunidad hispanohablante.(Si bien es poco tiempo creo que es suficiente aval para mostrar nuestro compromiso y seriedad con la tarea.)* Por qué buscamos apoyo financiero?Si bien cada uno de nosotros entiende la importancia particular y el impacto de lo que hacemos, es un incentivo extra recibir una compensación en BCH para seguir entregando en tiempo y forma el boletín en español.Actualmente este pago corre a cargo de una donación que fue hecha anteriormente, la cual está llegando a su fin.Lo recaudado en este fliptarter serviría para incentivar nuestros esfuerzos por los siguientes 12 meses.* Por qué debería apoyar este flipstarter?Hoy en dia existen muchas herramientas muy buenas, incluso integradas con IA que traducen textos de una forma bastante coherente e impresionante, pero la jerga que se maneja en todo lo relacionado a criptos y en específico a BCH es bastante técnica y particular, combinando términos de finanzas, computación y criptografía.Esto hace que no sea una lectura sencilla para nuevos usuarios e interesados de la comunidad hispano hablante, por lo cual, buscamos siempre encontrar la forma más simple de hacer entender el contenido usando las palabras correctas según la intención del artículo o noticia(Esto es un trabajo realmente demandante).También buscamos ser referentes en cuanto a estandarización del vocabulario que se maneja, de forma tal que nuestras traducciones sirvan como guía a otras personas que se animan a escribir cualquier tipo de artículo o para ganar en cultura y vocabulario en general.* Nuestras cuentas:https://x.com/bchnewsespanol/https://medium.com/@bchnewsespanolhttps://read.cash/@BCHNEWSESPANOL

title: BitcoinCashOG: BCH Marketing & Community Growth
description: Bitcoin Cash: Empowering BCH Marketing and Community GrowthFunding requested 17.5 BCH (~ €430 / BCH)Deliverables & Costs Breakdown:4 Months work (December 2025 - March 2026)Bitcoin Cash Marketing via @BitcoinCashOG on x.com4 months X Premium Plus subscription: €152 104 hours (€70 p/h) €7280 (1 hour per day Monday-Saturday)FundMe fee 1.5%Last Campaign (September-November 2025) Delivery & Results:78 hours work: November's not even done, and we're at ~120 hours already: 73% killer content, 20% stakeholder syncs, outreach, Spaces & podcasts, plus admin and analytics. Total over-delivery, and we hope you are happy with the results so far!Target 1000 Followers: BitcoinCashOG has grown to over 4000 followers before the month has even ended, 400% over delivery on targets!Posts: 401 posts averaging 4.6 per day (and a whole caboodle of replies) covering Merchant Mondays, Did you know Tuesdays, Mid-Week Geek Outs, Fusion Friday, and Spotlight Saturday!Report: Statistical analysis report to hone posts for maximum engagements@BitcoinCashOG Highlights- 400k+ Impressions- 8.2% Overall Engagement rate- 34.3K EngagementsAccount Growth (First Two Weeks of November compared with previous 2 weeks)- 19% Increase in impressions- 28% Increase in engagements- 7.5% Increase in engagement rate (9% current)- 257% Increase in replies- 54% Increase in profile visitsWhy This Campaign?Bitcoin Cash is the best money that the world has ever created and has some of the best developers and engineers in the space. There’s just one, tiny, but important challenge to overcome! Getting people educated about this amazing technology and then using it!Despite having a market cap of over 10 billion dollars, Bitcoin Cash has a total marketing budget of a rounding error of a rounding error … marketing and outreach can be improved tremendously!This is a big challenge, and the running and growing of BitcoinCashOG on X.com is an important strategy to tackle this. Social media is a great way to reach millions or even billions of people, and Bitcoin Cash benefits from a prominent, easily discoverable presence on X.com.When large accounts, such as news outlets or crypto exchanges, tweet about a coin, they often use @links to the largest handles representing those coins. This allows X users to simply click the account hyperlink and hit follow on that account to stay in the loop with the latest, greatest news from that coin and its community. Until recently, Bitcoin Cash was the odd coin out and didn't have such an account. People are lazy, they likely won’t even hit the search bar. Even if they do, they will not find any large BCH account to follow, and therefore probably wont follow anyone in the space nor get any news on their feed. Worse still, they may likely conclude that BCH is a dead project with no people in it!@BitcoinCashOG solves this to ensure that when Bitcoin Cash is mentioned in a social media post, it can link to a:Well curatedGood vibes onlyPG 13Non-politicalNon-exclusionaryBeacon of pure BCH goodnessThis project aims to continuously improve the perception of BCH to be the lively, thriving ecosystem we all know and love, with boundless opportunity. The strategy relies on community buy in and support! Goals and Vision:Grow the AccountMilestone 1: 100 followers - end of week 2 (Completed 10th August 2025 / 11 days ahead of schedule 🎯)Milestone 2: 1000 followers – November (Completed 22nd September / 6 weeks ahead of schedule 🎯)Milestone 3: Rename to Bitcoin Cash (BCH) 🎯Milestone 4: 10,000 followers - February 2026Milestone 5: 50,000 followers - August 2026Milestone 6: 100,000 followers - January 2027Content Strategy: 3-4 posts/day, themes include:Merchant Monday:(adoption focus),Mid-Week Geek Out:(education),Feel-Good Friday:(engagement), and positive, informative updates.Community Benefits : Provide a clean, positive, high signal channel where anyone can learn about BCH's potential as everyday digital cash. Amplify user stories, dev projects, and real-world uses to inspire more adoption.Account Guidelines : The account has clear guidelines to ensure that it remains an overwhelmingly positive force for the Bitcoin Cash Community and network.BitcoinCashOG GuidelinesPosts from BitcoinCashOG are designed for maximum positive impact for the network and community, that hold the community and the network’s best interests at heart. To achieve this there are some guidelines to help keep posts, content, and frequency on point:3-4 posts per day (average)- Replies to other posts are not limited in numberPosts spread out where possible to cover 24 hoursNo profanityNo pornographic postsNo overly political posts- Although Bitcoin Cash is inherently political so will be a balanceNo talking of priceNo opinions on protocol changes- Posting of proposed changes to keep people informed is ok, but posts should be handled neutrally.No aggressive language or attacking / antagonistic behaviour. Will use humble humour and facts as a retort.No supporting any flipstarter/fundme. Reporting of successful campaigns is okIdeas on general themes on days (at least one post will match the theme on that day)Thank you so much for reading and for any and all donations!Have some questions? Reach out on @BitcoinCashOG on X.com or via the BitcoinCashOG telegram group and we’ll be happy all questions and get you as excited as us! Let’s do this 💪

title: La Economía P2P: Episodes 176–195
description: What do we do?We publish weekly content exploring the real opportunities of a peer-to-peer economy based on Bitcoin Cash, focusing on both the technical aspects and the philosophy and vision behind this technology. 🔍💡📚What have we achieved?Since we started this project more than 4 years ago, we have achieved the following with the support of the community:More than 2,000 subscribers.More than 130,000 visits.More than 175 episodes published.More than 40 weekly summaries of our episodes in Spanish and English.More than 4 years of uninterrupted content uploads.4K video with high-quality audio.All our videos dubbed into English, allowing our content to circulate globally. 🌍🚀🔥All our episodes are available on YouTube and Spotify.What is the purpose of the campaign and what does it fund?Thanks to the support of previous campaigns, we already have a solid technical infrastructure and a stable production flow. We also pay editors using Bitcoin Cash, keeping the project aligned with the values we promote. 💚⚡🎙️The goal now is to maintain consistency and improve the quality of each episode.With your support, we will fund:Production of 20 episodes: research, coordination, scripts, recording, and professional audio and video editing.Summaries and international reach: weekly summaries in Spanish and English (published every Wednesday).Quality and infrastructure: improvements in audio tools, software, and equipment; hosting and maintenance of the website and podcast; payment for X Premium.Community and reciprocity: exclusive material for contributors and occasional support for other BCH community projects when possible.What happens if it is not funded?Each episode requires many hours of work: reading, writing, recording, and editing.If the entire campaign is not funded, we will have to reduce the frequency of uploads, publish fewer summaries, and postpone technical improvements.We hope that, as on other occasions, the invaluable support of the community will be with us. We believe that this is a project worth continuing. And we are always looking to improve and willing to strengthen ties with the community.---📺 YouTube: @LaEconomiaP2P🎙️ Spotify: La Economía P2P🌐 Website: https://laeconomiap2p.com✉️ Telegram: https://t.me/LaEconomiaP2P🐦 X: https://twitter.com/LaEconomiaP2P---¿Qué hacemos?Publicamos contenido semanal explorando las oportunidades reales de una economía entre pares basada en Bitcoin Cash, con foco tanto en los aspectos técnicos como en la filosofía y la visión detrás de esta tecnología. 🔍💡📚¿Qué logramos?Desde que comenzamos con este proyecto, hace ya más de 4 años, logramos lo siguiente junto al apoyo de la comunidad:Más de 2.000 suscriptores.Más de 130.000 visitas.Más de 175 episodios publicados.Más de 40 resúmenes semanales en español e inglés de nuestros episodios.Más de 4 años subiendo contenido de manera ininterrumpida.Video en 4K con audio de alta calidad.Todos nuestros videos doblados al inglés, permitiendo que nuestro contenido circule a nivel global. 🌍🚀🔥Todos nuestros episodios están disponibles en YouTube y en Spotify.¿Cuál es el propósito de la campaña y qué financia?Gracias al apoyo de campañas anteriores ya contamos con una infraestructura técnica sólida y un flujo de producción estable. También pagamos editores usando Bitcoin Cash, manteniendo el proyecto alineado con los valores que promovemos. 💚⚡🎙️El objetivo ahora es mantener la constancia y mejorar la calidad de cada entrega.Con tu apoyo vamos a financiar:Producción de 20 episodios: investigación, coordinación, guiones, grabación y edición profesional de audio y video.Resúmenes y alcance internacional: resúmenes semanales en español e inglés (publicados cada miércoles).Calidad e infraestructura: mejoras en herramientas de audio, software y equipamiento; hosting y mantenimiento de la web y del pódcast; pago de X Premium.Comunidad y reciprocidad: material exclusivo para colaboradores y apoyo puntual a otros proyectos de la comunidad BCH cuando sea posible.¿Qué pasa si no se financia?Cada episodio requiere muchas horas de trabajo: lectura, escritura, grabación y edición.Si no se financia la campaña completa, deberemos reducir la frecuencia de subida, publicar menos resúmenes y postergar mejoras técnicas.Esperamos que, como otras veces, el apoyo inestimable de la comunidad nos acompañe. Contamos con que este es un proyecto digno de mantenerse. Y nos mantenemos siempre buscando mejorar y dispuestos a estrechar los lazos con la comunidad.---📺 YouTube: @LaEconomiaP2P🎙️ Spotify: La Economía P2P🌐 Website: https://laeconomiap2p.com✉️ Telegram: https://t.me/LaEconomiaP2P🐦 X: https://twitter.com/LaEconomiaP2P

title: issues.cash
description: issues.cash is a bounty platform on Bitcoin Cash. By providing tools for bounty management, discovery, and payment, its goal is to improve the maintainer-developer relationship, and stimulate the growth of the ecosystem. By leveraging the incentives offered by bounties, it brings visibility to projects that need development, while also making it easier for independent developers to find work.How it worksWith the provided apps, project maintainers are able to create task-based bounties. A smart contract is used to hold the bounty amount, which is released automatically to the contributor when their work gets approved by the maintainer, or refunded upon cancellation or after an expiry date. Maintainers can also track active bounties and active attempts.On the other hand, developers can explore open bounties in the community, and submit work based on their interest. They can also check whether a bounty is already funded by the maintainer, bringing more transparency to the process. When their work is approved, the smart contract settles immediately, making the payment effortless to the maintainer, and eliminating any delays.All the operations can be done with commands via GitHub/GitLab comments on issues and pull requests. For example:Commenting:/bounty 0.1 --refund <address> --expiry <days>...on an issue assigns a bounty to it.Creating a pull request with:/claim <issue_number> --payout <address>...registers an attempt to claim the bounty.PreviewTrust modelissues.cash acts as an oracle that monitors the project repositories, and calls the smart contract when:a task is completed and approved (pull request gets merged)the bounty is cancelledthe bounty expiresIt is not completely trustless, as full trustlessness is not the immediate goal. This can be improved in later versions by introducing arbiters and a reputation system.As of the current model:the maintainer must trust the oracle, which can call the smart contract settlement functionsthe developer must trust the oracle, but also the maintainer to act in good faith, so as to not copy their work prior to it being approved and settled, favor a competing attempt unfairly, or discarding good quality work for any reasonA maintainer could theoretically benefit by acting in bad faith, but doing so is inconvenient, costly, and detrimental to their reputation, thus not beneficial long-term.The maintainer can mitigate the need to trust the oracle by self-hosting the platform, but would thereby lose some benefits like publicity of their bounties.DeliveryEstimation for completion: 45–60 days after funding. Securing funding allows me to dedicate full-time focus to this project.DeliverablesBy the end of the delivery, the open source platform will be online, for free, charging zero commission. The only fees are network fees, which are deposited in advance to the contract to fund the settlement or refund transactions.FeaturesSupports GitHub and GitLab repositoriesCreate, fund, and cancel bountiesBounty funding verificationBounty auto-settlement once pull/merge request is mergedBounty expiry date with auto-refundWebsite with open bounty list and statsFor future considerationThese are not part of the initial scope, but could be explored based on community interest:Stable token bountiesArbitration system for disputesReputation scores for maintainers and contributorsFundingRequested: 42 BCH (~$520/BCH)Including:Architecture, building, and testingDedicated server for official oracle (1 year)Maintenance and security updates (1 year)If the campaign contributions exceed the initial amount, the remaining will be re-invested in the platform by funding future development.Current statusThe smart contract is drafted and available for review here.GitHub integration is prototyped and has a few webhooks and automatic comments on issues already working, including hashing of the contract with the provided parameters.The developerI’m Mateus, a software developer who has been a Bitcoin enthusiast for 10+ years.I’ve worked for 3 years at Nash, a regulated crypto platform, where I had my longest professional experience in blockchain. There, I worked on various products like a fully-fledged decentralized exchange, a mobile wallet, and a fiat-ramps SDK.Over the last year, I’ve proudly contributed to Selene Wallet doing mostly paid contributions through bounties, and also some voluntary ones.ContactEmail: mateus@dlbn.coX: https://x.com/dlbncoTelegram: @dlbnco

title: Help Build 365 Days of BCH Content
description: Help Build 365 Days of BCH Content — Spanish Today, English TomorrowFor more than a year I’ve shown one thing consistently: commitment.I’ve created over 450 pieces of content dedicated almost entirely to Bitcoin Cash — long-form videos, shorts, livestreams, educational series, news breakdowns, and community discussions. I’ve been present every week, growing two YouTube channels, staying active in the BCH community on X/Twitter, and building a Telegram group where people can learn, discuss, and stay connected.This campaign isn’t a promise from zero — it’s the continuation of a proven track record.I’ve already invested hundreds of hours, built an audience, created daily content, and maintained a constant BCH presence across platforms. The work is real, the effort is consistent, and the results are public.Supporting this campaign means funding someone who is already doing the work — and is committed to scaling it for an entire year.Value Proposition“Sustain one year of daily Spanish-language BCH content on YouTube — with long-form videos, shorts, livestreams and education. If the funding goal is doubled, I will also create a full English-language BCH channel replicating all content for a global audience.”The project will focus on Spanish-language content, targeting the fast-growing LATAM crypto audience—one of the regions where BCH has the strongest potential for real-world adoption.However, if the campaign reaches 50% extra the target, I will launch a new English-only channel, replicating all long-form videos and distilling the best shorts for a global audience.This transforms the project from a regional BCH voice into a bilingual media ecosystem.Updated Campaign DescriptionCryptoMinders 2026 — Community-Funded BCH Content (Spanish → English expansion optional).For the past year I’ve built two Spanish-language channels entirely dedicated to Bitcoin Cash:CryptoMinders Shorts — 720+ subscribershttps://www.youtube.com/@cryptomindershortsCryptoMinders (Long-form) — 130+ subscribershttps://www.youtube.com/@cryptomindersWith more than 450 videos produced, BCH has been the center of almost everything I’ve published.This campaign funds a full year of Spanish-language BCH content:Monthly:8 long-form videos (2/week)90 shorts (3/day)A permanent 24/7 BCH livestreamAnnual total:96 long videos900+ shorts24/7 livestream nonstopTo make this project truly sustainable for an entire year, I will need to invest not only my time, discipline, research, creativity, and consistency, but also significantly upgrade the tools that make this work possible. That includes improving my PC and production equipment, updating editing and design software, and maintaining the streaming infrastructure required to keep a 24/7 BCH livestream running without interruptions.This is why I’m asking for your support.Without the help of the community, I will have no choice but to scale down — or even abandon — the effort I’ve been carrying almost entirely on my own. In the early months, a few generous supporters helped keep the project alive, and I am deeply grateful for that. But those small contributions, while meaningful, are not enough to sustain the level of production, equipment upgrades, and operational costs required for the next phase.Your support doesn’t fund dreams — it funds the continuity of a real project, already in motion, that needs the community behind it to grow instead of fade away.🎯 Funding Goal9 BCH (Base Goal)Funds one year of Spanish-language content.14 BCH (Double Goal — English Expansion)Unlocks:Full English-language replicationCreation of a new English channelBilingual content productionDual-audience outreach (LATAM + Global)All content in this campaign will be produced in Spanish, focusing on Latin America — one of the most receptive regions for BCH adoption.However… if the funding goal is exceeded:I will create a new English-language channel, where:Every long video will be re-recorded or adapted into EnglishSelected shorts will be recreatedNew English livestream material will be addedBranding and messaging will target a global audienceThis effectively converts the project from a single-language creator into a bilingual BCH media networkAnd as a Closing Bonus: Three BCH Projects Powered by This CampaignThis campaign isn’t only about sustaining content — it’s about building a broader Bitcoin Cash ecosystem.If we reach our goal, I will be able to continue developing three additional BCH-focused projects that will strengthen visibility, culture, and real-time information across the entire community.1. BCHContentHub / BCHHub.cashBCHHub.cashhttps://fundme.cash/campaign/65Funded on FundMe.cash and currently under development, BCHHub.cash aims to become a centralized portal for BCH creators, tools, resources, tutorials, and community projects. A place where newcomers and long-time supporters can discover everything happening in the BCH space.This campaign helps ensure the project is completed and expanded with high-quality media and coverage.2. BCH Monitor — Real-Time BCH Information for the 24/7 StreamsAs a major bonus to the livestream channel, I will develop BCH Monitor, a real-time information panel displaying:Live BCH priceRecent blocks minedHashrate updatesMarket volatility indicatorsNetwork statisticsKey ecosystem alertsThis will transform the 24/7 livestream into a dynamic, informative, and constantly updated BCH broadcast, something the ecosystem has never had before.3. BCH Hits — Music and Culture for the Bitcoin Cash Movementhttps://youtube.com/live/fU3adTgA1rwAnother unique addition will be BCH Hits, a music project designed to promote BCH culture through curated playlists, themed mixes, and branded audio content that can run on streams, videos, and community events.It's a creative way to bring BCH identity to life and reach new audiences emotionally — not just technically.A Final MessageThis campaign is more than a funding request.It’s a chance to solidify a full-year BCH media presence, expand into a bilingual ecosystem, keep creating educational content, strengthen livestream infrastructure, and push forward three valuable community projects.Your support empowers all of this.With it, Bitcoin Cash gains visibility, culture, creativity, and constant presence.Without it, these efforts simply cannot continue at the scale BCH deserves.Thank you for considering supporting this mission — and for being part of building the future of BCH content.Contact me X https://x.com/cryptomindersytTelegram https://t.me/c/2336571313/1

title: BCH‑1: Phase 2 Booster Plan
description: BCH‑1: Phase 2 Booster PlanIntroduction:BCH‑1 is the signal layer for Bitcoin Cash.It is a standing initiative focused on identifying, supporting, and advancing serious builders and projects on BCH.Phase 1 of the BCH‑1 Hackcelerator is live. The platform, brand, and operational structure are in place. Developer submissions are open, ecosystem curation is active, and outreach to builders inside and outside the BCH ecosystem is ongoing.This campaign is focused on strengthening Phase 2 by increasing builder incentives, expanding reach, and improving execution capacity.BCH‑1 is already broadcasting BCH as the best UTXO chain to build on.The hackcelerator structure for onboarding the next generation of BCH builders is in place.BCH‑1 marketing, brand, videos, and ecosystem outreach have been shipped.What’s Been Done So Far:The BCH‑1 Hackcelerator was launched using 100 BCH from the remaining SLP fund. The initiative launched as a follow-on from Blaze Hackathon, BCH-1 aims to continue that with a stronger focus on making projects - investable, shippable and marketed.Already Done:Phase 1 is live with active developer submissions (40+ already onboarded)BCH‑1 marketing, brand, videos, and ecosystem outreach have been shipped.The BCH‑1 website has been shipped and is maintainedCashtoken Markets is operational for easier token discoveryEcosystem project showcases is ongoingBCH‑1 has published regular messaging on why BCH is structurally well-suited for application-layer growthOutreach is targeting new developer segments beyond the existing BCH communityBCH‑1 marketing, brand, videos, and ecosystem outreach have been shipped.These components are functioning and continue to operate regardless of the outcome of this campaign.For Sponsors:Contributing to this campaign will automatically make you into a sponsor, if you wish we will put you as an official sponsor, consider your sponsorship be contributing to the prize pool, if you have specific bounties you would like to deploy, we can make it happen through the prize pool.You will also be eligible for our experimental CHIP points program, these are CashToken points that we encourage you to distribute to BCH community members who do thankless work, and deserve some appreciation for the work they do, especially in amplifying BCH's social media's presense and ecosystem awareness, but are mostly doing it out of passion and do not get core developer type of reimbursement.Why We’re Raising MoreHelp me by supporting BCH-1 Hackcelerator run longer and bigger, with more after-care support for the projects involved.This campaign seeks to raise around $45,000 USD (accounting for swings in BCH price) to strengthen Phase 2 execution:$20,000 — Expanded reward pool for high-quality projects, totally the builder rewards prize pool from $20,000 to a total of $40,00$10,000 — Strategic marketing and targeted ecosystem outreach (Marketing costs, such as promotions, KOL, reserved if no good vectors found)$5,000 — Mentor compensation for technical and product guidance (Makes it easier for BCH key figures to justify spending some time helping out)$10,000 — Coordinator capacity for team support and partner development. (Yes, that's me J Master Pig)The expanded reward pool increases competitiveness with other ecosystems and helps serious teams take their builds further. Additional funds also support outreach into developer spaces beyond BCH, where prize size and mentorship access meaningfully affect participation.We will move phase 2 about 3 weeks back, to allow more devs to see the increase in prize pool, if this raise is successful.Current Budget Overview — Where the SLP Funds WentAt the conclusion of the SLP Fund, 200 BCH remained. That amount has already been fully allocated:100 BCH was used to launch BCH‑1 and fund Phase 1 (J Master Pig as coordinator and developer, Marketing Team, Website, Current prize pool of $20k)100 BCH was allocated to BCHD to support ongoing node maintenance and developmentThis campaign is separate. It does not overlap with or replace the existing BCH‑1 allocation. BCH‑1 Phase 1 is already operating with its existing budget. This raise is focused entirely on expanding capacity and builder incentives in Phase 2.How the BCH‑1 Fund Actually WorksFunds from this campaign will not be distributed automatically.No guaranteed spend: If submitted projects do not meet defined quality standards, funds will remain unused.Staged rewards: Disbursements are conditional on verified execution, not just participation.Phase 2 trigger: Prize allocations are only activated if a minimum threshold of quality is met. (The raised $20k will be ringfenced for another hackcelerator,Retroactive incentives: Secondary rewards may be issued to builders who demonstrate follow-through or launch on mainnet after the hackcelerator period.If no such outcomes emerge, the capital will either be reserved for a future cohort or returned. A public post-campaign report will disclose how all funds were handled.What’s Coming NextThe following initiatives are planned as part of the Phase 2 pipeline:A reward point system to recognize ecosystem-positive actions, including cross-promotion of other BCH projects and public builder activity, similiar to yapping on Kaito.Continued onboarding and support for teams progressing through Phase 1Technical and product mentorship for selected projectsPublic-facing showcases of qualifying submissionsPhase 2 will not begin until:At least 15 submitted projects are deemed viable as fully polished projects through reviewWe aim to end up with a target 5 projects reach a standard considered investable or ready for external support, and will help draw up the capex structure and present it to potentially interested parties who may want to invest in the BCH ecosystem.If that threshold is not met. No prize funds are distributed until these criteria are achieved.Long-Term Plans for BCH‑1BCH‑1 is designed as a standing initiative, not a one-off campaign.Planned next steps include, but depends on my output and how the initiative scales:Recurring hackcelerator sprints on an annual cycleContinued post-hack support for high-potential teamsDevelopment of BCH Atlas — a registry and tracking layer for BCH ecosystem projects, campaigns, and funding flowsAdding NFTs to CashToken Markets with a OpenSea like interfaceTools to help teams transition from prototypes to deployable Cashtoken-based applicationsCoordination with external investors for follow-on funding and growth support (ideally!)Campaign to fund decentralised BD on Bitcoin Cash to integrate BCH with the wider crypto ecosystem. E.g. Cashtokens on exchanges.The long-term intent is to create durable infrastructure around builder discovery, project visibility, and network-aligned capital deployment.Who’s Involved — and Who We’ve Reached Out ToCoordinationBCH‑1 is currently led by J Master Pig a long-time contributor to the Bitcoin Cash ecosystem:Organizer of the early Hong Kong BCH meetups (Over 100 attendees!), worked with BCA Former director of the SLP Foundation, help drive early creation of the SLP infra and ecosystemCo-creator of Spice Token and SpiceFeed (Transfered to Joemar Taganna)Collaborated with Bitcoin.com to bring USDT to SLPEarly contributor to SLP NFT-1 standards (e.g. Juungle)Built one of the first Telegram-native NFT games on BCH: Enter The Sphere (Reactivating)Fundraising in the crypto world outside of BCH, Near Protocol, ArbitrumCo-founder of Arbius.ai (AI inference protocol) (Left)Former Lead Dev of HeyAmica.com, an open-source AI companion system (Left) (1000+ stars on Github)TeamA small social media team contributes part-time AI tools are used to increase output efficiency, but additional help is still needed for outreach, builder support, and partner coordinationI really need help lol, so if you are reading this and don't pledge, know that i'm drowning in work and would appreciate any help.Partnerships and OutreachTransitioned directly from Blaze HackathonPartnered with the Bitcoin Cash PodcastCoordinating with projects like Cauldron DEX, Parity USD.Outreach to General Protocols, Paytacca and other polished BCH teamsContinuing outreach to BCH-aligned and external teams with build-ready productsFinal Note: I want to emphasize, i'm taking on quite a lot, it might sound like the ask is a bit large, but $40k is still a small hackathon by web3 standards, let alone an accelerator program. I'm doing it because i'm sold that BCH could be something much larger fairly soon.It makes me incredibly apprehensive to ask for 90 BCH . My dream with other BCHers is one day $50k isn't considered a large amount in BCH, even with this budget, I will still be doing a lot of things alone, like my fellow BCHers : )) It is my hope that people will see the value of what Blaze, BCH-1 and Bliss (and other nascent programs) is doing for BCH and we'll eventually build pipelines to rival the mechanisms they have on well funded cryptos like BTC. We won't get there in a week or even a few months, but I think if we can build this stuff and get outsider investors interested, then we'll solve a whole host of things on BCH that hinders its ecosystem from truly scaling and competing on a global scale.

title: BCH tipping in Nostr Client OstrichGram
description: This fundraise is to fund the software development work to add an integrated Bitcoin Cash wallet into the OstrichGram nostr chat software. I became interested in Nostr a few years ago when I first discovered it. I liked the minimalist structure and open source nature of it and believed that open source solutions for social media are important. I still believe that. My first Nostr client attempt was an Electron Cash plugin "Nostron" which implemented the basics. The UX was not impressive, but I took a second attempt with OstrichGram, a cross platform implementation in Flutter, which compiles for Linux Desktop and Windows Desktop, and theoretically for MacOS. An android version would be possible if the UI was reworked. I have always been a fan of Telegram and so OstrichGram is designed with a similar look and feel. Although I liked the design of Nostr, the leadership was centralized around BTC type people, but I didn't let that stop me. I was one of the first in the BCH community to engage with and explore nostr. The most difficult thing about nostr is gaining network effect. Some have suggested using telegram-nostr bridges to help. Another challenge was integrating BCH payments. My plan is to build an integrated wallet right into the chat software and allow payments in the simplest way possible. Reseach indicates it may be possible to pay directly to a nostr identity (npub). I had set aside the project for a while but I'm getting reinvigorated to work on this software, in part because of realizing how well BCH could integrate with it, but perhaps even more importantly: after witnessing how important free expression is today, and how much it is being attacked lately. There had been a lot of discussion around Nostr pubkeys and whether the BIP340 signature scheme would actually work. At first it was unclear, then appeared it would be incompatible, but now seems like it could work. Despite the different signature schemes, both the nostr identity signing as well as BCH transaction signing could co-exist, albeit it may be considered non-best practice but still very viable for a tipping situation. The goal of the development here is two-fold: First, integrate a basic BCH wallet into a nostr client. Secondly, create an environment that allows in-chat tipping with BCH. If time permits, I also will build the nostr-TG bridge, although the exact functionality will depend on what is possible in this domain, but this consideration will also inform us of the best way to display tips in the chat. Some form of a bridge to go along with this would be extremely helpful in actually creating a chat-with-BCH-tips environment that we could actually experience sooner rather than later. I plan to complete this work in early 2026. For those unfamiliar with me, I go by "Jonald" or simply "JF". I have been involved with Bitcoin Cash since day 1 and I am the lead maintainer of the longest running BCH project (Electron Cash) and have worked on various projects over the years including SLP, CashShuffle, and CashFusion. Questions about this fundraise are welcome and I can be contacted on telegram: @jf010. Existing client: https://github.com/OstrichGram/OstrichGram

title: Bitcoin Cash Event Calendar
description: Self Introduction: Hi, I'm CM a also known as Graphix by CM. A graphic artist and video editor from the Bitcoin Cash community. My field is to create Bitcoin Cash related arts and graphics and editing Bitcoin Cash related videos.An artist behind the BCH Bliss graphics/promotional graphics and an editor of the Layla Upgrade, BCHBliss, BCH Blaze,Chip Process explainer Videos, Pump It Up (BCH Maximalist Remix) - Endor and many more.Some of my BCH graphics works in the past year are the Parity USD Logo, CashColumn Magazine and the cashdrop logo.So there we go.. Proposed Project:Imagine a Calendar where you can track an upcoming important dates on the Bitcoin Cash Ecosystem on your Desk or Desktop. A Calendar where you can access online and print your own. Bitcoin Cash has so many events within a year, creating a BCH Calendar will help us track what is happening in the upcoming months or years. Here's the concept: 01 The Cover Page and Month Page Concept (PRINTED VERSION) 02 Page Sample for the Month of May 03 Page Sample for the Month of NovemberDesign Ideas and Contents· Each month has a theme for instance on month of May, it has a theme of "built for everyday use" with its corresponding image on the left side. Same as Month of November with the theme of "Smart Money" with its image.· The favorite part, the highlight of the date based on the events. For instance in the sample design above, on Month of May, there's BankRun on May 1 and 15. The date box has elements that is related to event. Perhaps a logo, color scheme or brand of the said event.· The important part, the Reminder Section where the date and the event title is displayed. Visitor will automatically know the upcoming events.So far, that's the initial contents. If you have suggestions/Opinions/Feedback please fill this form.Or send a DM to my contacts:Graphics by CMXTelegramemail:contact.cmworks@gmail.com Goal: 2BCHThis will include the deliverables, management for the calendar to be updated, domain and research. Output to Deliver:· Online Version(Landscape)- Priority· Template Source File Version· PDF For Printing option· 2 Versions Orientations (Portrait and Landscape) Perks for the Contributors:A special page for the Sponsors/Contributors Long Term Plan:The Calendar Contents should be updated every year to keep up with the current happenings in the Bitcoin Cash ecosystem.The calendar will do a series of versions based on the suggestions and feedback of the community. Thank you, BCH Fam!

title: issues.cash
description: 2nd attemptThis is the second attempt of funding this project, as the first campaign expired on January 4th. The reception was good, but we weren’t able to raise the full amount.Given the positive feedback, continued interest from pledgers, and recent price rise, I’ve decided to relaunch it with a lower requested amount. The price rose by about 20%, and the requested amount was lowered by approximately 15% to account for any potential drops.Scope stays the same, the only thing that changes is the amount being requested. The “status” section was also updated with the latest progress.This campaign will be shorter in duration, as the goal is just to extend the contribution window with a more reachable goal.Introductionissues.cash is a bounty platform on Bitcoin Cash. It allows maintainers post and manage bounties, and helps developers discover paid work, improving collaboration and bringing visibility to projects that need development.How it worksDemos of the actual implementation can be checked in this GitHub issue.With the provided apps, project maintainers are able to create task-based bounties. A smart contract is used to hold the bounty amount, which is released automatically to the contributor when their work gets approved by the maintainer, or refunded upon cancellation or after an expiry date. Maintainers can also track active bounties and active attempts. Any project, BCH-related or not, can implement it.On the other hand, developers can explore open bounties in the community, and submit work based on their interest. They can also check whether a bounty is already funded by the maintainer, bringing more transparency to the process. When their work is approved, the smart contract settles immediately, making the payment effortless to the maintainer, and eliminating any delays.All the operations can be done with commands via GitHub/GitLab comments on issues and pull requests. For example:Commenting:/bounty 0.1 —refund <address> —expiry <days>…on an issue assigns a bounty to it.Creating a pull request with:/claim <issue_number> —payout <address>…registers an attempt to claim the bounty.PreviewTrust modelissues.cash acts as an oracle that monitors the project repositories, and calls the smart contract when:a task is completed and approved (pull request gets merged)the bounty is cancelledthe bounty expiresIt is not completely trustless, as full trustlessness is not the immediate goal. This can be improved in later versions by introducing arbiters and a reputation system.As of the current model:the maintainer must trust the oracle, which has access to the smart contract settlement functionsthe developer must trust the oracle, but also the maintainer to act in good faith, so as to not copy their work prior to it being approved and settled, or favor a competing attempt unfairlyA maintainer could theoretically benefit by acting in bad faith, but doing so is inconvenient, costly, detrimental to their reputation, thus not beneficial long-term.The maintainer can mitigate the need to trust the oracle by self-hosting the platform, but would thereby lose some benefits like publicity of their bounties.DeliveryEstimation for completion: 45–60 days after funding. Securing funding allows me to dedicate full-time focus to this project.DeliverablesBy the end of the delivery, the open source platform will be online, for free, charging zero commission. The only fees are network fees, which are deposited in advance to the contract to fund the settlement or refund transactions.FeaturesSupports GitHub and GitLab repositoriesCreate, fund, and cancel bountiesBounty funding verificationBounty auto-settlement once pull/merge request is mergedBounty expiry date with auto-refundWebsite with open bounty list and statsFor future considerationThese are not part of the initial scope, but could be explored based on community interest:Stable token bountiesArbitration system for disputesReputation scores for maintainers and contributorsFundingRequested: 42 BCH 36 BCHIncluding:Architecture, building, and testingDedicated server for official oracle (1 year)Maintenance and security updates (1 year)If the campaign contributions exceed the initial amount, the remaining will be re-invested in the platform by funding future development.Current status: 10%The smart contract is stable and available for review here.GitHub integration is mostly ready, and has all the main smart contract interactions working and tested on testnet3.The current progress is at roughly 10% quantitatively, and task breakdown can be followed on the public repository.The developerI’m Mateus, a software developer who has been a Bitcoin enthusiast for 10+ years.I’ve worked for 3 years at Nash, a regulated crypto platform, where I had my longest professional experience in blockchain. There, I worked on various products like a fully-fledged decentralized exchange, a mobile wallet, and a fiat-ramps SDK.Over the last year, I’ve proudly contributed to Selene Wallet doing mostly paid contributions via bounties, but also some voluntary contributions.ContactEmail: mateus@dlbn.coX: https://x.com/dlbncoTelegram: @dlbnco

title: Confidential Transactions - Phase 2
description: Phase 2 — Beaconless Binding + Sharded Pool Plumbing for Cash-Like Payments on Bitcoin CashFundMe.cash / Flipstarter (Campaign 1 of 1)Target: 20 BCH • Duration: 45 days • Network: Chipnet (demo) • Repo: public (GitHub)One-paragraph summaryMost online payments broadcast who paid whom and how much. This campaign funds three concrete, BCH-native milestones:Sharded Pool Scaffold (on-chain demo) — a working chipnet prototype for evolving “confidential-shaped” transactions via per-user pool shards (CashTokens mutable NFTs + covenant state transitions).Reusable Packages (alpha) — factor the prototype into publishable, reusable libraries that the UI (and future work) can depend on cleanly.Desktop Demo UI (Electron — the JavaScript app framework, not Electron Cash) — a single-pane-of-glass app that runs the full demo end-to-end on chipnet and makes it easy to audit.Phase 2 ships a real on-chain skeleton + usable UX. Phase 3 (outside this campaign) can later plug in a ZKP verifier kernel for full confidentiality.Why this mattersPhysical cash doesn’t broadcast a social graph. Online payments usually do.Our goal: bring cash-like payment properties to Bitcoin Cash while keeping:No trust assumptions (no oracles, no custodians, no committees)On-chain enforceability (rules are validated by normal nodes)Standard-looking transactions (no obvious metadata beacons)What 20 BCH buys (Milestones)Milestone 1 — Sharded Pool Scaffold (Chipnet Demo)Goal: Deliver an end-to-end, on-chain sharded pool demo that evolves transaction state without requiring a global beacon or trusted party.NotesThis milestone is already substantially implemented in the current work; this funding request is being posted late. The campaign funds completion hardening: cleanup, tests, reproducible runs, and packaging into a tagged deliverable.DeliverablesA runnable chipnet demo that supports:Init: create N per-user shards as CashTokens mutable NFTs locked to a state-covenant.Deposit: create a “stealth-style” inbound payment (currently RPA-secp convenience layer).Import: move deposit value into one selected shard and mutate the shard commitment.Withdraw: spend a shard, mutate commitment again, and emit an outbound payment output.Clear documentation of what is real vs. placeholder:binding placeholders, authorization placeholders, conservation placeholders, nullifier placeholdersReproducible scripts + a state file format suitable for demos (idempotent steps)Acceptance criteria (“done when”)A clean, documented, repeatable end-to-end demo run on chipnet.Shard commitments mutate deterministically and are visible/auditable on-chain.A fresh user can follow the README to reproduce a successful run.Milestone 2 — Reusable Packages (Alpha)Goal: Turn the prototype into a small ecosystem of reusable, versioned packages that the UI can import cleanly — and that the broader BCH community can evaluate and build on.Scope note: The exact package list is intentionally open-ended. We will add/remove/merge packages as the ElectronJS UI reveals what should be stable APIs vs. demo glue. The deliverable is a coherent package set, not a fixed count.DeliverablesWorkspace packages (TypeScript-first) covering the core building blocks the demo needs, e.g.:chain scanning (RPA scan + windowed scanners)actor/wallet abstractionspool/shard operations (init/import/withdraw) with typed interfacestx building utilities (inputs/outputs, fee estimation helpers, CashTokens helpers)electrum client wrappers (typed, testable)demo state persistence interfaces (so the UI can store and replay)Packaging & publishing hygiene:correct package.json + exports maps for ESM buildsbuild outputs in dist/ with sourcemapsminimal docs per package (README + example snippet)unit tests for high-risk logic (hashing, encodings, script building, parsing)ZKP-ready “plumbing” as packages/utilities where it belongs:beaconless output binding utilities (canonical output fingerprinting)canonical hashing/transcript conventions (domain separation tags, stable parsing)budgeting hooks/guards (so later proof integration is bounded and testable)Acceptance criteria (“done when”)The demo can be run by importing only from workspace packages (no long relative imports from src/).Packages build cleanly under the pinned toolchain (Yarn 4 + TypeScript) and can be consumed as ESM.At least one end-to-end flow is covered by tests and/or deterministic test vectors (JSON fixtures).Milestone 3 — Desktop Demo UI (ElectronJS)Goal: Replace the CLI-first workflow with a packaged desktop app so non-developers can run and audit the demo end-to-end on chipnet.DeliverablesElectronJS desktop app wrapping the demo runner with UI controls for:init shardsdepositimportwithdrawUI screens replacing CLI prompts:funding screen (show address + “I funded it” → re-check UTXOs → continue)progress timeline (init → deposit → import → withdraw)live log pane (streamed events, copyable)txids + explorer links (audit-friendly)Safe, deterministic local storage for demo state and wallets in the app-data directory (not cwd).Acceptance criteria (“done when”)A new user can run the demo end-to-end on chipnet without touching the CLI.UI exposes txids, addresses, and a clear step log so observers can verify each stage in an explorer.Packaged builds run locally (at least one OS target; ideally macOS + Windows + Linux).What backers will be able to verifyBackers can independently verify progress by running the app and checking on-chain transactions:The Electron demo produces copyable txids and explorer links for every step.The sharded pool scaffold demonstrates real on-chain state evolution using CashTokens NFT commitments.Beaconless binding work (as introduced in Phase 2) avoids OP_RETURN-style beacons for binding private context to outputs.Privacy non-negotiablesWe will not ship an “efficient leak.”Only accept proofs that are truly zero-knowledge (masking/blinding isn’t optional).Never recurse/batch non-ZK proofs.Never promote witness-bearing openings into public inputs.Domain-separated randomness + no reuse across proofs/layers/batches.When funded, final Work will be delivered as public github repository under MIT license with link posted to this campaign and in Telegram channel.

title: BCH‑1: Phase 2 Booster Plan
description: IntroductionBCH‑1 brings developers to Bitcoin Cash and helps them ship.We find serious builders, provide resources and mentorship, and help them polish their products to an investable state.Bitcoin Cash has real technical capabilities, but the outside world doesn't see it yet. When polished, fundable projects start launching on BCH, that changes. BCH-1 exists to make that visible.Phase 1 of the BCH-1 Hackcelerator is already live, submissions are open, outreach is ongoing, and the platform is operational. This campaign raises funds to expand Phase 2 with a larger prize pool, broader outreach, and better support for the teams that make it through.What’s Been Done So Far:BCH-1 was launched in September.We worked with Blaze to transition their progression directly onto the hackcelerator.To date:Phase 1 is live with active developer submissions (114 Developers, 20 projects submitted at time of this writing) (https://dorahacks.io/hackathon/bch-1-hackcelerator/detail)The BCH‑1 website has been shipped and is maintained (https://bch-1.org)Cashtoken Markets is operational for easier token discovery(https://cashtokenmarkets.bch-1.org) For months, BCH‑1 has published regular messaging on why BCH is structurally well-suited for application-layer growth (https://x.com/bch_1_official) Prize Pool of $20,000 LockedInitial Example Projects Submitted:FlowGuard — Payment streaming for BCH and CashTokens (similar to Sablier)NexOps — AI-assisted IDE for CashScript with contract testing and auditingRoastMyBag — Viral token sharing using BCH's unique capabilitiesOPTN Labs — Smart contract marketplace and plug and play mobile walletFun(d) Tokens — Bundle multiple tokens in a single transactionBCHUBkey — Token-gated access control for communitiesCashDropKit — CashToken Airdrop tools built on BCH's scalable L1 featuresSecureVote — CashTokens, On-chain voting (similar to Snapshot)All contributors become sponsors. If you'd like to be listed publicly on our website, let us know. Your contribution goes to the prize pool—and if you have specific bounties in mind, we can work with you to deploy them.Why We’re Raising More:This campaign raises $40,000 USD to expand Phase 2:$20,000 — Prize pool expansion (doubles the builder rewards from $20k to $40k total)$7,500 — Marketing and outreach (promotions, KOLs, reaching developers outside BCH)$5,000 — Mentor compensation (technical and product guidance from BCH ecosystem leaders)$7,500 — Coordinator (team support, partner development, operational capacity)50% of funds raised goes directly to builders. A larger prize pool makes BCH-1 competitive with other ecosystem accelerators and gives serious teams a reason to build here.Current Budget Overview — Where the SLP Funds Went:At the conclusion of the SLP Fund, 200 BCH remained. That amount has been fully allocated:100 BCH — Launched BCH-1 and funded Phase 1 (coordinator, marketing team, website, $20k prize pool)100 BCH — Allocated to BCHD for node maintenance and developmentPhase 1 is already funded and operational. This campaign is separate—it expands Phase 2, not replace existing allocation.How the BCH‑1 Fund Actually Works:Funds from this campaign will not be distributed automatically.If projects don't meet quality standards, funds remain unspentIf quality thresholds aren't met, capital is reserved for future cohorts or returnedBuilders who launch on mainnet after the program may receive retroactive rewardsA public post-campaign report will disclose how all funds were handled.What’s Coming Next:The following initiatives are planned as part of the Phase 2 pipeline:A reward point system to recognize ecosystem-positive actions, including cross-promotion of other BCH projects and public builder activity.Continued onboarding and support for teams progressing through Phase 1Technical and product mentorship for selected projectsPublic-facing showcases of qualifying submissionsPhase 2 will not begin until:At least 15 submitted projects are deemed viable as fully polished projects through reviewWe aim to end up with a target 5 projects reach a standard considered investable or ready for external support, and will help draw up the capex structure and present it to potentially interested parties who may want to invest in the BCH ecosystem.If that threshold is not met.No raised prize funds are distributed until these criteria are achieved.Long-Term Plans for BCH‑1:BCH-1 is a standing initiative, not a one-off campaign.Recurring hackcelerator sprints (annual cycle)Post-program support for high-potential teamsBCH Atlas — a registry for BCH ecosystem projects, campaigns, and fundingNFT support for CashToken MarketsCoordination with external investors for follow-on fundingThe long-term intent is to create durable infrastructure around builder discovery, project visibility, and network-aligned capital deployment.Who’s Involved — and Who We’ve Reached Out To:CoordinationBCH‑1 is currently led by J Master Pig a long-time contributor to the Bitcoin Cash ecosystem:You can watch his podcast here with Jeremy: youtube.com/watch?v=V7WGmRF9lCU Organizer of the early Hong Kong BCH meetups (Over 100 attendees!), worked with BCAFormer director of the SLP Foundation, help drive early creation of the SLP infra and ecosystemCo-creator of Spice Token and SpiceFeed (Transfered to Joemar Taganna)Collaborated with Bitcoin.com to bring USDT to SLPEarly contributor to SLP NFT-1 standards (e.g. Juungle)Built one of the first Telegram-native NFT games on BCH: Enter The Sphere Fundraising in the crypto world outside of BCH, Near Protocol, ArbitrumCo-founder of Arbius.ai (AI inference protocol) (Left)Former Lead of HeyAmica.com, an open-source AI companion system (Left) (1000+ stars on Github)TeamA small social media team contributes part-timeAI tools are used to increase output efficiency, but additional help is still needed for outreach, builder support, and partner coordinationPartnerships and OutreachSponsored and transitioned directly from Blaze HackathonPartnered with the Bitcoin Cash PodcastCoordinating with projects like Cauldron DEXOutreach to General Protocols, Paytacca and other polished BCH teamsContinuing outreach to BCH-aligned and external teams with build-ready productsCommunity Support:Bitcoincashautist: “ J Master Pig has had good reputation in my book since 2020, which he's re-confirmed through smartly building out BCH-1 and delivering meaningful community proof of work. I trust he'll put the raised capital to good use, looking forward to seeing what he'll deliver”ShadowofHarbinger: "BCH-1 is the missing link between developers and delivery, it can bring real value to Bitcoin Cash and Crypto by accelerating funding and connecting people who ship code with people who ship real life-useful products"Jeremy: "I am impressed with the work BCH-1 has been doing so far, since the very inception there has been communication with me about how it can best serve the community and feedback has been noted and implemented. My key objective for it is to become a next-step for BLAZE builders to convert their enthusiasm and prototypes into launched MVPs, and in that regard it's looking good so far. The list of Phase 1 projects already includes several newcomers to BCH that got started in BLAZE, and I'm really looking forward to seeing how far they can get with a little extra support. /someone needs to run this kind of project, and J Master Pig is stepping up to be that person."

title: Seedless Wallet Infrastructure for Bitcoin Cash
description: Seedless Wallet Infrastructure for Bitcoin CashWhen Satoshi released Bitcoin Cash, it was miles ahead of what existed: a simple, open way to send money instantly to anyone, anywhere - without banks and gatekeepers. However, over the years, smartphones, internet banking, and apps like Venmo and Cash App have closed the convenience gap, despite being tangled in the same problems of modern finance. Moreover, people’s expectations have shifted: passwordless logins and Face ID taught the world that security can be both strong and effortless.Fortunately, BCH is now among only a handful of cryptocurrencies capable of bridging that gap due to the upcoming May 2026 Layla Upgrade. I have tested and developed a low-cost contract that supports modern device-backed signing keys (P-256), dramatically lowering the barrier to entry and improving the user experience and convenience. ide.bitauth.com/import-gist/16786d7eaf81d509ad907705f057f8faOnce deployed, a new generation of users, social apps, and non-crypto communities will be able to send, receive, and manage BCH, Cashtokens, and Identity on-chain without having to manage secrets.ProposalThis proposal funds the creation of seedless, device-bound wallets for Bitcoin Cash using P-256 (secp256r1) keys, backed by hardware security modules.The work includes:Production-ready P256 Smart Contract on the BCH VMBuilding a reference CLI vault wallet for macOSCreating a fork of Cashonize that requests WebAuthn P-256 signaturesPublishing a JavaScript library enabling developers to easily build seedless BCH wallet flowsThe result is a dramatically lower barrier to entry for users, with wallets that can be hardware-backed, seedless, cloud-recoverable, and familiar to non-crypto audiences.Funding request: 35 BCHDuration: ~4 months active development + cleanup/publishingGoal: Have working reference implementations before the May network upgrade.Progress: https://github.com/SeedlessBCH/signatures.cashMotivationToday, Bitcoin Cash wallets still rely primarily on seed phrases, which are confusing for new users, easy to lose or mishandle, and a major source of anxiety and technical burden.Nearly all modern devices already support secure, hardware-backed cryptography via:Apple Secure EnclaveAndroid StrongBox and KeystoreWindows TPMYubiKeysOS-level secure key storage as a fallbackBy enabling P-256 directly on the BCH VM, we unlock:Seedless wallets tied to a deviceOptional cloud backup on device restore (platform dependent)Familiar UX patterns similar to Apple Pay, passkeys, and modern passwordless login.This undertaking will be a crucial step toward making BCH usable for non-technical users, non-crypto communities, and real-world applications. The vision is to enable these users to onboard quickly and seamlessly, transacting either with BCH or CashTokens.As a BCH Developer with over 5 years of experience building BCH Applications and Smart Contracts, I will be able to achieve this by May 15 upgrade with your support.Scope of Work1. P-256 Smart Contract (Already Implemented)Finalize and document the existing P-256 Smart Contract implementationProvide example scripts and test vectorsThis is already complete but will need to be polished, documented, and made ready for developer use.2. macOS CLI Secure Vault (Reference Application)Command-line vault application for macOSUses OS-backed or hardware-backed P-256 keysNo seed phrase requiredThis serves as a reference and proof that BCH can function without mnemonic seeds.3. Cashonize Fork with WebAuthn P-256 SigningFork Cashonize to request WebAuthn signatures (assertions) using P-256Supports platform authenticators (Touch ID / Face ID / Windows Hello / TPM, etc.)The WebAuthn private key never leaves the authenticator (no key export)Extends the P-256 Smart Contract to parse and validate that a transaction is authorized by verifying:The returned P-256 signatureThe associated client data and authenticator dataThis demonstrates browser-based, seedless BCH wallets using the same primitives as passkeys and passwordless login.4. Publish JavaScript LibraryUnified JS library exposing:P-256 script templates for BCHWebAuthn signing / assertion handlingCLI and browser reference integrationsThis is designed for reuse across wallets, apps, and services.Stretch Goal: OAuth-Based Onboarding (Optional)In addition to a seedless wallet, it will also be possible to implement a novel onboarding method for new users by adding P-256 Smart Contracts, namely the ability to send funds to a specific Google or Apple ID that can be unlocked only by providing a valid OAuth response from the OpenID Provider. On spend, the coins are moved to a new device-bound secure signing (WebAuthn / platform keys).This enables flows like:“Send BCH to someone who only has a Google/Apple login — no wallet setup required.”TimelineMonth 1: Finalize P-256 Smart Contract, documentation, example vectorsMonth 2: macOS CLI vault wallet implementationMonth 3: Cashonize WebAuthn signing forkMonth 4: Cleanup, docs, and publishing JS library for all functionalityTarget: have working reference apps before the May upgrade.Funding Request35 BCH to complete Phase 1.This covers:Engineering timeReference implementationsDocumentation and test vectorsOpen-source publishing and maintainabilityOngoing support, upgrades, and consultation to integrate with popular BCH Wallets.ClosingBitcoin Cash can significantly reduce onboarding friction by embracing modern, hardware-backed cryptography and seedless flows, without turning the ecosystem into a custodial one.This proposal delivers immediate usability improvements, strong developer tooling, and a foundation for identity-linked and privacy-enhancing applications.Thank you for your support! Telegram: @sahidmiller

title: BCH Dev Infrastructure and Tooling
description: Project scope#1 - mainnet-jsContinue forward on the mainnet-js development front after recent big achievements: releasing the v3.0.0 with HDWallet support (https://x.com/mainnet_pat/status/2018289563647570197) with its integration in Cashonize wallet (https://x.com/cashonize/status/2019077673666974092), creation of the new ElectrumCash client library `rpckit`(https://x.com/mainnet_pat/status/2019772722453045418) which has huge stability and resilience enhancements with fallback and cluster meta-transports.I would like to fully migrate to`rpckit` as mainnet-js' network provider; eliminate most of the issue backlog; responding to feature requests (double-spend-proof alert integration, encrypted secret storage, etc.); modernise the project infrastructure to prevent the code rot.#2 - WalletConnect2 extended specification to support HDWalletsExtend the WalletConnect2 specification (https://github.com/mainnet-pat/wc2-bch-bcr) to support the HDWallets and upgrade Cashonize to use it, update the existing tooling based on mainnet-js and libauth for others to use (https://github.com/mainnet-pat/bch-wc2, https://github.com/mainnet-pat/dapp-starter). This will allow us to harden the stability and reliability of the WC2 in BCH dApps.#3 - CashScript Contract Development KitCreate a Ethereum's Hardhat-like experience for BCH: extract the CashScript testing suite (hosted in https://github.com/CashScript/cashscript/tree/master/examples/testing-suite, but authored by me long ago in https://cs-flip.pat.mn) into a separate project and standalone npm package, build a `npx create-cashscript-devkit` scaffolding CLI, and begin framework features (task system, network management, contract interaction CLI). Contract developers use this suite to develop and debug contract packages like xSushi, Olando, etc., in a sandboxed environment before developing the user-facing apps. This should narrow our developer experience gap when compared with Ethereum's ecosystem, HardHat and Foundry environments.Further improve the testing-specific MockNetworkProvider to use `rpckit` to be fully compatible implementation of ElectrumCash protocol in memory, for node.js and browsers. This would allow to build custom testing blockchains, extend and track UTXO set, validate transactions upon submissions using libauth. This approach will also allow to "fork" the existing blockchains like BCH mainnet to evaluate the transactions against existing (contract) UTXOs. This approach is already standard for EVM world (https://www.tevm.sh) and we should catch up.#4 - TapSwap / BCHPUMPWith the help of the prerequisite step #3, extract and formalise for others to reuse the current TapSwap.cash contracts (Selene wallet wants to embed them into the wallet app). Create new TapSwap contract types – partial limit orders for fungible tokens, limited-time (expiring) contracts (also to mitigate the UTXO bloat and kill too old contracts), and auction contracts. Plus, upgrade the marketplace UI (it is almost 3 years old) for a better trading experience. Furthermore, developing a fast, dedicated TapSwap contract indexer will be a big UX improvement. And since BCHPump.cash lives in the same codebase as TapSwap, it will benefit a lot from these developments too.BonusAs a bonus track with minor items, I would like to get back to some of my existing projects to modernize them: revitalize the EmeraldDAO app; rework the p2p-cash (https://github.com/mainnet-pat/p2p-cash) to use libauth and `rpckit` for the new full typing with failover and cluster meta-transport strategies; create DefiLlama adapters for TapSwap and BCHPump

title: The Bitcoin Cash Podcast: Episodes 176 - 185
description: # FundMe: Episodes 176 - 185 of The Bitcoin Cash Podcast### Funding requested: 28.5 BCH (~$570 / BCH)## Deliverable- Episodes 176 - 185. The same show you already know and (hopefully) love.- Weekly BCH Bullet newsletter on Sundays during the delivery period of the episodes.## Notes### FundMeThe Podcast is thriving with a dedicated workspace and broadcast area. The Podcast has now been running for more than 5 years, and it's amazing for me to reflect on how things have changed in that time. Appreciation to all listeners, and bonus appreciation to the ones that have been part of the entire 5 year journey!### VisibilityThe Podcast has grown another 10% on Youtube since the last FundMe, now reaching 3.37k subs. It is still rate limited on Twitter, every time it hits 9 990 followers it then "mysteriously" loses 50 or so followers - as has been the case for many months at this point. Even large visibility from discussion around Epstein and Who Killed Bitcoin related commentary apparently did not gain any new followers (curious!).Regardless, The BCH Bullet is still growing and has nearly doubled in size to 49 subscribers and 300 Twitter followers. That's really nice to see our expanding reach.Although it is not specifically a Podcast initiative, my hope is that BCH can also work around algorithmic suppression on Twitter with BChat from Selene. Between that and growing interest from outsiders maybe we can break through to the next level of reach.## Note to the community2026 has started well for the BCH community, rising in price while the market falls apart for competitors. Epstein papers have also created huge interest in the Hijacking, which is increasing interest in BCH as a byproduct.Very excited for the Layla upgrade to go live in May at BLISS! Looking forward to meeting new Podcast listeners in person and catching up with some regulars!Let's keep building Bitcoin Cash.### Proof of WorkI have aggregated previous delivery & community work (including previous 5x FundMes + 5x Flipstarter delivery & accountability) on a dedicated page on the Bitcoin Cash Podcast website. See it here: [https://bitcoincashpodcast.com/proof-of-work](https://bitcoincashpodcast.com/proof-of-work).## Cost breakdown:- 0.5 BCH topic research, episode hosting- 0.5 BCH production & editing costs- 0.1 BCH Thumbnail graphics & episode distribution- 0.15 BCH Timestamps, links & episode summary- 0.15 BCH Youtube Shorts creation & distribution1.4 BCH / episodex10 episodes = 14 BCH- 0.2 BCH per BCH Bulletx12 Bullets = 2.4 BCH6 BCH: Studio rent.2 BCH: Accounting costs.2 BCH: Additional media on-site help for livestream coverage at BLISS.2 BCH misc expenses: Ongoing costs such as website hosting & domain registration, supporting other community fundraisers, FAQ maintenance etc.0.1 BCH: Fundme.cash fee## Contributor rewards:Donations of > 0.5 BCH will receive a shoutout at the end of each show (like the Patrons and Sponsors) unless your name/message is rude or slanderous or you ask me not to or it's otherwise unreadable for some reason.## Badgers.cashNo Badgers burning like on previous FundMes. I was hoping to make this kind of community incentive for proof-of-HODL to gain extra traction but it doesn't seem very in demand. I still like the concept, but after several attempts without getting much flow on traction I'm deciding it's better to listen to the feedback of the market.## DisclaimerI will be completing the deliverables whether or not the FundMe gets funded, the continuation of the show & my assistance to the community is in not directly contingent on this funding request. However, as per previous FundMe, I believe this is a valuable community service & get a lot of feedback in that regard. The community contributions do a lot to help the show operate in the community's best interest without compromising for financial necessity.## ContactAs always, for any questions, comments or suggestions I can be reached at jeremy@bitcoincashpodcast.com, on Twitter at https://x.com/TheBCHPodcast or on Telegram https://t.me/BitcoinCashPodcast.
